verses.Luke 9:4: Whatever house you enter, stay there until you leave that city.
verses.Luke 9:5: And as for those who do not receive you, as you go out from that city, shake the dust off your feet as a testimony against them.”
verses.Luke 9:13: But He said to them, “You give them something to eat!” And they said, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish, unless perhaps we go and buy food for all these people.”
verses.Luke 9:14: (For there were about five thousand men.) And He said to His disciples, “Have them sit down to eat in groups of about fifty each.”
verses.Luke 9:18: And it happened that while He was praying alone, the disciples were with Him, and He questioned them, saying, “Who do the people say that I am?”
verses.Luke 9:20: And He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” And Peter answered and said, “The Christ of God.”
verses.Luke 9:22: saying, “The Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed and be raised up on the third day.”
verses.Luke 9:23: And He was saying to them all, “If anyone wishes to come after Me, he must deny himself, and take up his cross daily and follow Me.
verses.Luke 9:24: For whoever wishes to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake, he is the one who will save it.
verses.Luke 9:25: For what is a man profited if he gains the whole world, and loses or forfeits himself?
verses.Luke 9:26: For whoever is ashamed of Me and My words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of him when He comes in His glory, and the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
verses.Luke 9:27: But I say to you truthfully, there are some of those standing here who will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God.”
verses.Luke 9:41: And Jesus answered and said, “You unbelieving and perverted generation, how long shall I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.”
verses.Luke 9:44: “Let these words sink into your ears; for the Son of Man is going to be delivered into the hands of men.”
verses.Luke 9:48: and said to them, “Whoever receives this child in My name receives Me, and whoever receives Me receives Him who sent Me; for the one who is least among all of you, this is the one who is great.”
verses.Luke 9:50: But Jesus said to him, “Do not hinder him; for he who is not against you is for you.”
verses.Luke 9:55: But He turned and rebuked them, [and said, “You do not know what kind of spirit you are of;
verses.Luke 9:56: for the Son of Man did not come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them.”] And they went on to another village.
verses.Luke 9:58: And Jesus said to him, “The foxes have holes and the birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head.”
verses.Luke 9:59: And He said to another, “Follow Me.” But he said, “Lord, permit me first to go and bury my father.”
verses.Luke 9:60: But He said to him, “Allow the dead to bury their own dead; but as for you, go and proclaim everywhere the kingdom of God.”
verses.Luke 9:62: But Jesus said to him, “No one, after putting his hand to the plow and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God.”
verses.Luke 10:2: And He was saying to them, “The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few; therefore beseech the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into His harvest.
verses.Luke 10:3: Go; behold, I send you out as lambs in the midst of wolves.
verses.Luke 10:4: Carry no money belt, no bag, no shoes; and greet no one on the way.
verses.Luke 10:5: Whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace be to this house.’
verses.Luke 10:6: If a man of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.
verses.Luke 10:7: Stay in that house, eating and drinking what they give you; for the laborer is worthy of his wages. Do not keep moving from house to house.
verses.Luke 10:8: Whatever city you enter and they receive you, eat what is set before you;
verses.Luke 10:9: and heal those in it who are sick, and say to them, ‘The kingdom of God has come near to you.’
verses.Luke 10:10: But whatever city you enter and they do not receive you, go out into its streets and say,
verses.Luke 10:11: ‘Even the dust of your city which clings to our feet we wipe off in protest against you; yet be sure of this, that the kingdom of God has come near.’
verses.Luke 10:12: I say to you, it will be more tolerable in that day for Sodom than for that city.
verses.Luke 10:13: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles had been performed in Tyre and Sidon which occurred in you, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
verses.Luke 10:14: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.
verses.Luke 10:15: And you, Capernaum, will not be exalted to heaven, will you? You will be brought down to Hades!
verses.Luke 10:16: “The one who listens to you listens to Me, and the one who rejects you rejects Me; and he who rejects Me rejects the One who sent Me.”
verses.Luke 10:18: And He said to them, “I was watching Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
verses.Luke 10:19: Behold, I have given you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy, and nothing will injure you.
verses.Luke 10:20: Nevertheless do not rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are recorded in heaven.”
verses.Luke 10:21: At that very time He rejoiced greatly in the Holy Spirit, and said, “I praise You, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that You have hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have revealed them to infants. Yes, Father, for this way was well-pleasing in Your sight.
verses.Luke 10:22: All things have been handed over to Me by My Father, and no one knows who the Son is except the Father, and who the Father is except the Son, and anyone to whom the Son wills to reveal Him.”
verses.Luke 10:23: Turning to the disciples, He said privately, “Blessed are the eyes which see the things you see,
verses.Luke 10:24: for I say to you, that many prophets and kings wished to see the things which you see, and did not see them, and to hear the things which you hear, and did not hear them.”
verses.Luke 10:26: And He said to him, “What is written in the Law? How does it read to you?”
verses.Luke 10:28: And He said to him, “You have answered correctly; do this and you will live.”
verses.Luke 10:30: Jesus replied and said, “A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among robbers, and they stripped him and beat him, and went away leaving him half dead.
verses.Luke 10:31: And by chance a priest was going down on that road, and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
verses.Luke 10:32: Likewise a Levite also, when he came to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.
verses.Luke 10:33: But a Samaritan, who was on a journey, came upon him; and when he saw him, he felt compassion,
verses.Luke 10:34: and came to him and bandaged up his wounds, pouring oil and wine on them; and he put him on his own beast, and brought him to an inn and took care of him.
verses.Luke 10:35: On the next day he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper and said, ‘Take care of him; and whatever more you spend, when I return I will repay you.’
verses.Luke 10:36: Which of these three do you think proved to be a neighbor to the man who fell into the robbers’ hands?”
verses.Luke 10:37: And he said, “The one who showed mercy toward him.” Then Jesus said to him, “Go and do the same.”
verses.Luke 10:41: But the Lord answered and said to her, “Martha, Martha, you are worried and bothered about so many things;
verses.Luke 10:42: but only one thing is necessary, for Mary has chosen the good part, which shall not be taken away from her.”
verses.Luke 11:2: And He said to them, “When you pray, say: ‘Father, hallowed be Your name. Your kingdom come.
verses.Luke 11:3: Give us each day our daily bread.
verses.Luke 11:4: And forgive us our sins, For we ourselves also forgive everyone who is indebted to us. And lead us not into temptation.’ ”
verses.Luke 11:5: Then He said to them, “Suppose one of you has a friend, and goes to him at midnight and says to him, ‘Friend, lend me three loaves;
verses.Luke 11:6: for a friend of mine has come to me from a journey, and I have nothing to set before him’;
verses.Luke 11:7: and from inside he answers and says, ‘Do not bother me; the door has already been shut and my children and I are in bed; I cannot get up and give you anything.’
verses.Luke 11:8: I tell you, even though he will not get up and give him anything because he is his friend, yet because of his persistence he will get up and give him as much as he needs.
verses.Luke 11:9: “So I say to you, ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
verses.Luke 11:10: For everyone who asks, receives; and he who seeks, finds; and to him who knocks, it will be opened.
verses.Luke 11:11: Now suppose one of you fathers is asked by his son for a fish; he will not give him a snake instead of a fish, will he?
verses.Luke 11:12: Or if he is asked for an egg, he will not give him a scorpion, will he?
verses.Luke 11:13: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask Him?”
verses.Luke 11:17: But He knew their thoughts and said to them, “Any kingdom divided against itself is laid waste; and a house divided against itself falls.
verses.Luke 11:18: If Satan also is divided against himself, how will his kingdom stand? For you say that I cast out demons by Beelzebul.
verses.Luke 11:19: And if I by Beelzebul cast out demons, by whom do your sons cast them out? So they will be your judges.
verses.Luke 11:20: But if I cast out demons by the finger of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.
verses.Luke 11:21: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own house, his possessions are undisturbed.
verses.Luke 11:22: But when someone stronger than he attacks him and overpowers him, he takes away from him all his armor on which he had relied and distributes his plunder.
verses.Luke 11:23: He who is not with Me is against Me; and he who does not gather with Me, scatters.
verses.Luke 11:24: “When the unclean spirit goes out of a man, it passes through waterless places seeking rest, and not finding any, it says, ‘I will return to my house from which I came.’
verses.Luke 11:25: And when it comes, it finds it swept and put in order.
verses.Luke 11:26: Then it goes and takes along seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they go in and live there; and the last state of that man becomes worse than the first.”
verses.Luke 11:28: But He said, “On the contrary, blessed are those who hear the word of God and observe it.”
verses.Luke 11:29: As the crowds were increasing, He began to say, “This generation is a wicked generation; it seeks for a sign, and yet no sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah.
verses.Luke 11:30: For just as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so will the Son of Man be to this generation.
verses.Luke 11:31: The Queen of the South will rise up with the men of this generation at the judgment and condemn them, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, something greater than Solomon is here.
verses.Luke 11:32: The men of Nineveh will stand up with this generation at the judgment and condemn it, because they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, something greater than Jonah is here.
verses.Luke 11:33: “No one, after lighting a lamp, puts it away in a cellar nor under a basket, but on the lampstand, so that those who enter may see the light.
verses.Luke 11:34: The eye is the lamp of your body; when your eye is clear, your whole body also is full of light; but when it is bad, your body also is full of darkness.
verses.Luke 11:35: Then watch out that the light in you is not darkness.
verses.Luke 11:36: If therefore your whole body is full of light, with no dark part in it, it will be wholly illumined, as when the lamp illumines you with its rays.”
verses.Luke 11:39: But the Lord said to him, “Now you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and of the platter; but inside of you, you are full of robbery and wickedness.
verses.Luke 11:40: You foolish ones, did not He who made the outside make the inside also?
verses.Luke 11:41: But give that which is within as charity, and then all things are clean for you.
verses.Luke 11:42: “But woe to you Pharisees! For you pay tithe of mint and rue and every kind of garden herb, and yet disregard justice and the love of God; but these are the things you should have done without neglecting the others.
verses.Luke 11:43: Woe to you Pharisees! For you love the chief seats in the synagogues and the respectful greetings in the market places.
verses.Luke 11:44: Woe to you! For you are like concealed tombs, and the people who walk over them are unaware of it.”
verses.Luke 11:46: But He said, “Woe to you lawyers as well! For you weigh men down with burdens hard to bear, while you yourselves will not even touch the burdens with one of your fingers.
verses.Luke 11:47: Woe to you! For you build the tombs of the prophets, and it was your fathers who killed them.
verses.Luke 11:48: So you are witnesses and approve the deeds of your fathers; because it was they who killed them, and you build their tombs.
verses.Luke 11:49: For this reason also the wisdom of God said, ‘I will send to them prophets and apostles, and some of them they will kill and some they will persecute,
verses.Luke 11:50: so that the blood of all the prophets, shed since the foundation of the world, may be charged against this generation,
verses.Luke 11:51: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who was killed between the altar and the house of God; yes, I tell you, it shall be charged against this generation.’
verses.Luke 11:52: Woe to you lawyers! For you have taken away the key of knowledge; you yourselves did not enter, and you hindered those who were entering.”
verses.Luke 12:1: Under these circumstances, after so many thousands of people had gathered together that they were stepping on one another, He began saying to His disciples first of all, “Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
verses.Luke 12:2: But there is nothing covered up that will not be revealed, and hidden that will not be known.
verses.Luke 12:3: Accordingly, whatever you have said in the dark will be heard in the light, and what you have whispered in the inner rooms will be proclaimed upon the housetops.
verses.Luke 12:4: “I say to you, My friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that have no more that they can do.
verses.Luke 12:5: But I will warn you whom to fear: fear the One who, after He has killed, has authority to cast into hell; yes, I tell you, fear Him!
verses.Luke 12:6: Are not five sparrows sold for two cents? Yet not one of them is forgotten before God.
verses.Luke 12:7: Indeed, the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Do not fear; you are more valuable than many sparrows.
verses.Luke 12:8: “And I say to you, everyone who confesses Me before men, the Son of Man will confess him also before the angels of God;
verses.Luke 12:9: but he who denies Me before men will be denied before the angels of God.
verses.Luke 12:10: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but he who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven him.
verses.Luke 12:11: When they bring you before the synagogues and the rulers and the authorities, do not worry about how or what you are to speak in your defense, or what you are to say;
verses.Luke 12:12: for the Holy Spirit will teach you in that very hour what you ought to say.”
verses.Luke 12:14: But He said to him, “Man, who appointed Me a judge or arbitrator over you?”
verses.Luke 12:15: Then He said to them, “Beware, and be on your guard against every form of greed; for not even when one has an abundance does his life consist of his possessions.”
verses.Luke 12:16: And He told them a parable, saying, “The land of a rich man was very productive.
verses.Luke 12:17: And he began reasoning to himself, saying, ‘What shall I do, since I have no place to store my crops?’
verses.Luke 12:18: Then he said, ‘This is what I will do: I will tear down my barns and build larger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.
verses.Luke 12:19: And I will say to my soul, “Soul, you have many goods laid up for many years to come; take your ease, eat, drink and be merry.” ’
verses.Luke 12:20: But God said to him, ‘You fool! This very night your soul is required of you; and now who will own what you have prepared?’
verses.Luke 12:21: So is the man who stores up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.”
verses.Luke 12:22: And He said to His disciples, “For this reason I say to you, do not worry about your life, as to what you will eat; nor for your body, as to what you will put on.
verses.Luke 12:23: For life is more than food, and the body more than clothing.
verses.Luke 12:24: Consider the ravens, for they neither sow nor reap; they have no storeroom nor barn, and yet God feeds them; how much more valuable you are than the birds!
verses.Luke 12:25: And which of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life’s span?
verses.Luke 12:26: If then you cannot do even a very little thing, why do you worry about other matters?
verses.Luke 12:27: Consider the lilies, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin; but I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory clothed himself like one of these.
verses.Luke 12:28: But if God so clothes the grass in the field, which is alive today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, how much more will He clothe you? You men of little faith!
verses.Luke 12:29: And do not seek what you will eat and what you will drink, and do not keep worrying.
verses.Luke 12:30: For all these things the nations of the world eagerly seek; but your Father knows that you need these things.
verses.Luke 12:31: But seek His kingdom, and these things will be added to you.
verses.Luke 12:32: Do not be afraid, little flock, for your Father has chosen gladly to give you the kingdom.
verses.Luke 12:33: “Sell your possessions and give to charity; make yourselves money belts which do not wear out, an unfailing treasure in heaven, where no thief comes near nor moth destroys.
verses.Luke 12:34: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
verses.Luke 12:35: “Be dressed in readiness, and keep your lamps lit.
verses.Luke 12:36: Be like men who are waiting for their master when he returns from the wedding feast, so that they may immediately open the door to him when he comes and knocks.
verses.Luke 12:37: Blessed are those slaves whom the master will find on the alert when he comes; truly I say to you, that he will gird himself to serve, and have them recline at the table, and will come up and wait on them.
verses.Luke 12:38: Whether he comes in the second watch, or even in the third, and finds them so, blessed are those slaves.
verses.Luke 12:39: “But be sure of this, that if the head of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
verses.Luke 12:40: You too, be ready; for the Son of Man is coming at an hour that you do not expect.”
verses.Luke 12:42: And the Lord said, “Who then is the faithful and sensible steward, whom his master will put in charge of his servants, to give them their rations at the proper time?
verses.Luke 12:43: Blessed is that slave whom his master finds so doing when he comes.
verses.Luke 12:44: Truly I say to you that he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
verses.Luke 12:45: But if that slave says in his heart, ‘My master will be a long time in coming,’ and begins to beat the slaves, both men and women, and to eat and drink and get drunk;
verses.Luke 12:46: the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not know, and will cut him in pieces, and assign him a place with the unbelievers.
verses.Luke 12:47: And that slave who knew his master’s will and did not get ready or act in accord with his will, will receive many lashes,
verses.Luke 12:48: but the one who did not know it, and committed deeds worthy of a flogging, will receive but few. From everyone who has been given much, much will be required; and to whom they entrusted much, of him they will ask all the more.
verses.Luke 12:49: “I have come to cast fire upon the earth; and how I wish it were already kindled!
verses.Luke 12:50: But I have a baptism to undergo, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!
verses.Luke 12:51: Do you suppose that I came to grant peace on earth? I tell you, no, but rather division;
verses.Luke 12:52: for from now on five members in one household will be divided, three against two and two against three.
verses.Luke 12:53: They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.”
verses.Luke 12:54: And He was also saying to the crowds, “When you see a cloud rising in the west, immediately you say, ‘A shower is coming,’ and so it turns out.
verses.Luke 12:55: And when you see a south wind blowing, you say, ‘It will be a hot day,’ and it turns out that way.
verses.Luke 12:56: You hypocrites! You know how to analyze the appearance of the earth and the sky, but why do you not analyze this present time?
verses.Luke 12:57: “And why do you not even on your own initiative judge what is right?
verses.Luke 12:58: For while you are going with your opponent to appear before the magistrate, on your way there make an effort to settle with him, so that he may not drag you before the judge, and the judge turn you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
verses.Luke 12:59: I say to you, you will not get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.”
verses.Luke 13:2: And Jesus said to them, “Do you suppose that these Galileans were greater sinners than all other Galileans because they suffered this fate?
verses.Luke 13:3: I tell you, no, but unless you repent, you will all likewise perish.
verses.Luke 13:4: Or do you suppose that those eighteen on whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them were worse culprits than all the men who live in Jerusalem?
verses.Luke 13:5: I tell you, no, but unless you repent, you will all likewise perish.”
verses.Luke 13:6: And He began telling this parable: “A man had a fig tree which had been planted in his vineyard; and he came looking for fruit on it and did not find any.
verses.Luke 13:7: And he said to the vineyard-keeper, ‘Behold, for three years I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree without finding any. Cut it down! Why does it even use up the ground?’
verses.Luke 13:8: And he answered and said to him, ‘Let it alone, sir, for this year too, until I dig around it and put in fertilizer;
verses.Luke 13:9: and if it bears fruit next year, fine; but if not, cut it down.’ ”
verses.Luke 13:12: When Jesus saw her, He called her over and said to her, “Woman, you are freed from your sickness.”
verses.Luke 13:15: But the Lord answered him and said, “You hypocrites, does not each of you on the Sabbath untie his ox or his donkey from the stall and lead him away to water him?
verses.Luke 13:16: And this woman, a daughter of Abraham as she is, whom Satan has bound for eighteen long years, should she not have been released from this bond on the Sabbath day?”
verses.Luke 13:18: So He was saying, “What is the kingdom of God like, and to what shall I compare it?
verses.Luke 13:19: It is like a mustard seed, which a man took and threw into his own garden; and it grew and became a tree, and the birds of the air nested in its branches.”
verses.Luke 13:20: And again He said, “To what shall I compare the kingdom of God?
verses.Luke 13:21: It is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three pecks of flour until it was all leavened.”
verses.Luke 13:24: “Strive to enter through the narrow door; for many, I tell you, will seek to enter and will not be able.
verses.Luke 13:25: Once the head of the house gets up and shuts the door, and you begin to stand outside and knock on the door, saying, ‘Lord, open up to us!’ then He will answer and say to you, ‘I do not know where you are from.’
verses.Luke 13:26: Then you will begin to say, ‘We ate and drank in Your presence, and You taught in our streets’;
verses.Luke 13:27: and He will say, ‘I tell you, I do not know where you are from; depart from Me, all you evildoers.’
verses.Luke 13:28: In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham and Isaac and Jacob and all the prophets in the kingdom of God, but yourselves being thrown out.
verses.Luke 13:29: And they will come from east and west and from north and south, and will recline at the table in the kingdom of God.
verses.Luke 13:30: And behold, some are last who will be first and some are first who will be last.”
verses.Luke 13:32: And He said to them, “Go and tell that fox, ‘Behold, I cast out demons and perform cures today and tomorrow, and the third day I reach My goal.’
verses.Luke 13:33: Nevertheless I must journey on today and tomorrow and the next day; for it cannot be that a prophet would perish outside of Jerusalem.
verses.Luke 13:34: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the city that kills the prophets and stones those sent to her! How often I wanted to gather your children together, just as a hen gathers her brood under her wings, and you would not have it!
verses.Luke 13:35: Behold, your house is left to you desolate; and I say to you, you will not see Me until the time comes when you say, ‘Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord!’ ”
verses.Luke 14:3: And Jesus answered and spoke to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath, or not?”
verses.Luke 14:5: And He said to them, “Which one of you will have a son or an ox fall into a well, and will not immediately pull him out on a Sabbath day?”
verses.Luke 14:8: “When you are invited by someone to a wedding feast, do not take the place of honor, for someone more distinguished than you may have been invited by him,
verses.Luke 14:9: and he who invited you both will come and say to you, ‘Give your place to this man,’ and then in disgrace you proceed to occupy the last place.
verses.Luke 14:10: But when you are invited, go and recline at the last place, so that when the one who has invited you comes, he may say to you, ‘Friend, move up higher’; then you will have honor in the sight of all who are at the table with you.
verses.Luke 14:11: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted.”
verses.Luke 14:12: And He also went on to say to the one who had invited Him, “When you give a luncheon or a dinner, do not invite your friends or your brothers or your relatives or rich neighbors, otherwise they may also invite you in return and that will be your repayment.
verses.Luke 14:13: But when you give a reception, invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, the blind,
verses.Luke 14:14: and you will be blessed, since they do not have the means to repay you; for you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous.”
verses.Luke 14:16: But He said to him, “A man was giving a big dinner, and he invited many;
verses.Luke 14:17: and at the dinner hour he sent his slave to say to those who had been invited, ‘Come; for everything is ready now.’
verses.Luke 14:18: But they all alike began to make excuses. The first one said to him, ‘I have bought a piece of land and I need to go out and look at it; please consider me excused.’
verses.Luke 14:19: Another one said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to try them out; please consider me excused.’
verses.Luke 14:20: Another one said, ‘I have married a wife, and for that reason I cannot come.’
verses.Luke 14:21: And the slave came back and reported this to his master. Then the head of the household became angry and said to his slave, ‘Go out at once into the streets and lanes of the city and bring in here the poor and crippled and blind and lame.’
verses.Luke 14:22: And the slave said, ‘Master, what you commanded has been done, and still there is room.’
verses.Luke 14:23: And the master said to the slave, ‘Go out into the highways and along the hedges, and compel them to come in, so that my house may be filled.
verses.Luke 14:24: For I tell you, none of those men who were invited shall taste of my dinner.’ ”
verses.Luke 14:26: “If anyone comes to Me, and does not hate his own father and mother and wife and children and brothers and sisters, yes, and even his own life, he cannot be My disciple.
verses.Luke 14:27: Whoever does not carry his own cross and come after Me cannot be My disciple.
verses.Luke 14:28: For which one of you, when he wants to build a tower, does not first sit down and calculate the cost to see if he has enough to complete it?
verses.Luke 14:29: Otherwise, when he has laid a foundation and is not able to finish, all who observe it begin to ridicule him,
verses.Luke 14:30: saying, ‘This man began to build and was not able to finish.’
verses.Luke 14:31: Or what king, when he sets out to meet another king in battle, will not first sit down and consider whether he is strong enough with ten thousand men to encounter the one coming against him with twenty thousand?
verses.Luke 14:32: Or else, while the other is still far away, he sends a delegation and asks for terms of peace.
verses.Luke 14:33: So then, none of you can be My disciple who does not give up all his own possessions.
verses.Luke 14:34: “Therefore, salt is good; but if even salt has become tasteless, with what will it be seasoned?
verses.Luke 14:35: It is useless either for the soil or for the manure pile; it is thrown out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”
verses.Luke 15:4: “What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and has lost one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the open pasture and go after the one which is lost until he finds it?
verses.Luke 15:5: When he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
verses.Luke 15:6: And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!’
verses.Luke 15:7: I tell you that in the same way, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.
verses.Luke 15:8: “Or what woman, if she has ten silver coins and loses one coin, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and search carefully until she finds it?
verses.Luke 15:9: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin which I had lost!’
verses.Luke 15:10: In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
verses.Luke 15:11: And He said, “A man had two sons.
verses.Luke 15:12: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of the estate that falls to me.’ So he divided his wealth between them.
verses.Luke 15:13: And not many days later, the younger son gathered everything together and went on a journey into a distant country, and there he squandered his estate with loose living.
verses.Luke 15:14: Now when he had spent everything, a severe famine occurred in that country, and he began to be impoverished.
verses.Luke 15:15: So he went and hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.
verses.Luke 15:16: And he would have gladly filled his stomach with the pods that the swine were eating, and no one was giving anything to him.
verses.Luke 15:17: But when he came to his senses, he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired men have more than enough bread, but I am dying here with hunger!
verses.Luke 15:18: I will get up and go to my father, and will say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight;
verses.Luke 15:19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; make me as one of your hired men.” ’
verses.Luke 15:20: So he got up and came to his father. But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt compassion for him, and ran and embraced him and kissed him.
verses.Luke 15:21: And the son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
verses.Luke 15:22: But the father said to his slaves, ‘Quickly bring out the best robe and put it on him, and put a ring on his hand and sandals on his feet;
verses.Luke 15:23: and bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let us eat and celebrate;
verses.Luke 15:24: for this son of mine was dead and has come to life again; he was lost and has been found.’ And they began to celebrate.
verses.Luke 15:25: “Now his older son was in the field, and when he came and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
verses.Luke 15:26: And he summoned one of the servants and began inquiring what these things could be.
verses.Luke 15:27: And he said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf because he has received him back safe and sound.’
verses.Luke 15:28: But he became angry and was not willing to go in; and his father came out and began pleading with him.
verses.Luke 15:29: But he answered and said to his father, ‘Look! For so many years I have been serving you and I have never neglected a command of yours; and yet you have never given me a young goat, so that I might celebrate with my friends;
verses.Luke 15:30: but when this son of yours came, who has devoured your wealth with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.’
verses.Luke 15:31: And he said to him, ‘Son, you have always been with me, and all that is mine is yours.
verses.Luke 15:32: But we had to celebrate and rejoice, for this brother of yours was dead and has begun to live, and was lost and has been found.’ ”
verses.Luke 16:1: Now He was also saying to the disciples, “There was a rich man who had a manager, and this manager was reported to him as squandering his possessions.
verses.Luke 16:2: And he called him and said to him, ‘What is this I hear about you? Give an accounting of your management, for you can no longer be manager.’
verses.Luke 16:3: The manager said to himself, ‘What shall I do, since my master is taking the management away from me? I am not strong enough to dig; I am ashamed to beg.
verses.Luke 16:4: I know what I shall do, so that when I am removed from the management people will welcome me into their homes.’
verses.Luke 16:5: And he summoned each one of his master’s debtors, and he began saying to the first, ‘How much do you owe my master?’
verses.Luke 16:6: And he said, ‘A hundred measures of oil.’ And he said to him, ‘Take your bill, and sit down quickly and write fifty.’
verses.Luke 16:7: Then he said to another, ‘And how much do you owe?’ And he said, ‘A hundred measures of wheat.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and write eighty.’
verses.Luke 16:8: And his master praised the unrighteous manager because he had acted shrewdly; for the sons of this age are more shrewd in relation to their own kind than the sons of light.
verses.Luke 16:9: And I say to you, make friends for yourselves by means of the wealth of unrighteousness, so that when it fails, they will receive you into the eternal dwellings.
verses.Luke 16:10: “He who is faithful in a very little thing is faithful also in much; and he who is unrighteous in a very little thing is unrighteous also in much.
verses.Luke 16:11: Therefore if you have not been faithful in the use of unrighteous wealth, who will entrust the true riches to you?
verses.Luke 16:12: And if you have not been faithful in the use of that which is another’s, who will give you that which is your own?
verses.Luke 16:13: No servant can serve two masters; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or else he will be devoted to one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.”
verses.Luke 16:15: And He said to them, “You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of men, but God knows your hearts; for that which is highly esteemed among men is detestable in the sight of God.
verses.Luke 16:16: “The Law and the Prophets were proclaimed until John; since that time the gospel of the kingdom of God has been preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.
verses.Luke 16:17: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one stroke of a letter of the Law to fail.
verses.Luke 16:18: “Everyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and he who marries one who is divorced from a husband commits adultery.
verses.Luke 16:19: “Now there was a rich man, and he habitually dressed in purple and fine linen, joyously living in splendor every day.
verses.Luke 16:20: And a poor man named Lazarus was laid at his gate, covered with sores,
verses.Luke 16:21: and longing to be fed with the crumbs which were falling from the rich man’s table; besides, even the dogs were coming and licking his sores.
verses.Luke 16:22: Now the poor man died and was carried away by the angels to Abraham’s bosom; and the rich man also died and was buried.
verses.Luke 16:23: In Hades he lifted up his eyes, being in torment, and saw Abraham far away and Lazarus in his bosom.
verses.Luke 16:24: And he cried out and said, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus so that he may dip the tip of his finger in water and cool off my tongue, for I am in agony in this flame.’
verses.Luke 16:25: But Abraham said, ‘Child, remember that during your life you received your good things, and likewise Lazarus bad things; but now he is being comforted here, and you are in agony.
verses.Luke 16:26: And besides all this, between us and you there is a great chasm fixed, so that those who wish to come over from here to you will not be able, and that none may cross over from there to us.’
verses.Luke 16:27: And he said, ‘Then I beg you, father, that you send him to my father’s house—
verses.Luke 16:28: for I have five brothers—in order that he may warn them, so that they will not also come to this place of torment.’
verses.Luke 16:29: But Abraham said, ‘They have Moses and the Prophets; let them hear them.’
verses.Luke 16:30: But he said, ‘No, father Abraham, but if someone goes to them from the dead, they will repent!’
verses.Luke 16:31: But he said to him, ‘If they do not listen to Moses and the Prophets, they will not be persuaded even if someone rises from the dead.’ ”
verses.Luke 17:1: He said to His disciples, “It is inevitable that stumbling blocks come, but woe to him through whom they come!
verses.Luke 17:2: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck and he were thrown into the sea, than that he would cause one of these little ones to stumble.
verses.Luke 17:3: Be on your guard! If your brother sins, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.
verses.Luke 17:4: And if he sins against you seven times a day, and returns to you seven times, saying, ‘I repent,’ forgive him.”
verses.Luke 17:6: And the Lord said, “If you had faith like a mustard seed, you would say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and be planted in the sea’; and it would obey you.
verses.Luke 17:7: “Which of you, having a slave plowing or tending sheep, will say to him when he has come in from the field, ‘Come immediately and sit down to eat’?
verses.Luke 17:8: But will he not say to him, ‘Prepare something for me to eat, and properly clothe yourself and serve me while I eat and drink; and afterward you may eat and drink’?
verses.Luke 17:9: He does not thank the slave because he did the things which were commanded, does he?
verses.Luke 17:10: So you too, when you do all the things which are commanded you, say, ‘We are unworthy slaves; we have done only that which we ought to have done.’ ”
verses.Luke 17:14: When He saw them, He said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” And as they were going, they were cleansed.
verses.Luke 17:17: Then Jesus answered and said, “Were there not ten cleansed? But the nine—where are they?
verses.Luke 17:18: Was no one found who returned to give glory to God, except this foreigner?”
verses.Luke 17:19: And He said to him, “Stand up and go; your faith has made you well.”
verses.Luke 17:20: Now having been questioned by the Pharisees as to when the kingdom of God was coming, He answered them and said, “The kingdom of God is not coming with signs to be observed;
verses.Luke 17:21: nor will they say, ‘Look, here it is!’ or, ‘There it is!’ For behold, the kingdom of God is in your midst.”
verses.Luke 17:22: And He said to the disciples, “The days will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
verses.Luke 17:23: They will say to you, ‘Look there! Look here!’ Do not go away, and do not run after them.
verses.Luke 17:24: For just like the lightning, when it flashes out of one part of the sky, shines to the other part of the sky, so will the Son of Man be in His day.
verses.Luke 17:25: But first He must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
verses.Luke 17:26: And just as it happened in the days of Noah, so it will be also in the days of the Son of Man:
verses.Luke 17:27: they were eating, they were drinking, they were marrying, they were being given in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed them all.
verses.Luke 17:28: It was the same as happened in the days of Lot: they were eating, they were drinking, they were buying, they were selling, they were planting, they were building;
verses.Luke 17:29: but on the day that Lot went out from Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven and destroyed them all.
verses.Luke 17:30: It will be just the same on the day that the Son of Man is revealed.
verses.Luke 17:31: On that day, the one who is on the housetop and whose goods are in the house must not go down to take them out; and likewise the one who is in the field must not turn back.
verses.Luke 17:33: Whoever seeks to keep his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life will preserve it.
verses.Luke 17:34: I tell you, on that night there will be two in one bed; one will be taken and the other will be left.
verses.Luke 17:35: There will be two women grinding at the same place; one will be taken and the other will be left.
verses.Luke 17:36: [Two men will be in the field; one will be taken and the other will be left.”]
verses.Luke 17:37: And answering they said to Him, “Where, Lord?” And He said to them, “Where the body is, there also the vultures will be gathered.”
verses.Luke 18:2: saying, “In a certain city there was a judge who did not fear God and did not respect man.
verses.Luke 18:3: There was a widow in that city, and she kept coming to him, saying, ‘Give me legal protection from my opponent.’
verses.Luke 18:4: For a while he was unwilling; but afterward he said to himself, ‘Even though I do not fear God nor respect man,
verses.Luke 18:5: yet because this widow bothers me, I will give her legal protection, otherwise by continually coming she will wear me out.’ ”
verses.Luke 18:6: And the Lord said, “Hear what the unrighteous judge said;
verses.Luke 18:7: now, will not God bring about justice for His elect who cry to Him day and night, and will He delay long over them?
verses.Luke 18:8: I tell you that He will bring about justice for them quickly. However, when the Son of Man comes, will He find faith on the earth?”
verses.Luke 18:10: “Two men went up into the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
verses.Luke 18:11: The Pharisee stood and was praying this to himself: ‘God, I thank You that I am not like other people: swindlers, unjust, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.
verses.Luke 18:12: I fast twice a week; I pay tithes of all that I get.’
verses.Luke 18:13: But the tax collector, standing some distance away, was even unwilling to lift up his eyes to heaven, but was beating his breast, saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, the sinner!’
verses.Luke 18:14: I tell you, this man went to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself will be exalted.”
verses.Luke 18:16: But Jesus called for them, saying, “Permit the children to come to Me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
verses.Luke 18:17: Truly I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a child will not enter it at all.”
verses.Luke 18:19: And Jesus said to him, “Why do you call Me good? No one is good except God alone.
verses.Luke 18:20: You know the commandments, ‘Do not commit adultery, Do not murder, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honor your father and mother.’ ”
verses.Luke 18:22: When Jesus heard this, He said to him, “One thing you still lack; sell all that you possess and distribute it to the poor, and you shall have treasure in heaven; and come, follow Me.”
verses.Luke 18:24: And Jesus looked at him and said, “How hard it is for those who are wealthy to enter the kingdom of God!
verses.Luke 18:25: For it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.”
verses.Luke 18:27: But He said, “The things that are impossible with people are possible with God.”
verses.Luke 18:29: And He said to them, “Truly I say to you, there is no one who has left house or wife or brothers or parents or children, for the sake of the kingdom of God,
verses.Luke 18:30: who will not receive many times as much at this time and in the age to come, eternal life.”
verses.Luke 18:31: Then He took the twelve aside and said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all things which are written through the prophets about the Son of Man will be accomplished.
verses.Luke 18:32: For He will be handed over to the Gentiles, and will be mocked and mistreated and spit upon,
verses.Luke 18:33: and after they have scourged Him, they will kill Him; and the third day He will rise again.”
verses.Luke 18:41: “What do you want Me to do for you?” And he said, “Lord, I want to regain my sight!”
verses.Luke 18:42: And Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight; your faith has made you well.”
verses.Luke 19:5: When Jesus came to the place, He looked up and said to him, “Zaccheus, hurry and come down, for today I must stay at your house.”
verses.Luke 19:9: And Jesus said to him, “Today salvation has come to this house, because he, too, is a son of Abraham.
verses.Luke 19:10: For the Son of Man has come to seek and to save that which was lost.”
verses.Luke 19:12: So He said, “A nobleman went to a distant country to receive a kingdom for himself, and then return.
verses.Luke 19:13: And he called ten of his slaves, and gave them ten minas and said to them, ‘Do business with this until I come back.’
verses.Luke 19:14: But his citizens hated him and sent a delegation after him, saying, ‘We do not want this man to reign over us.’
verses.Luke 19:15: When he returned, after receiving the kingdom, he ordered that these slaves, to whom he had given the money, be called to him so that he might know what business they had done.
verses.Luke 19:16: The first appeared, saying, ‘Master, your mina has made ten minas more.’
verses.Luke 19:17: And he said to him, ‘Well done, good slave, because you have been faithful in a very little thing, you are to be in authority over ten cities.’
verses.Luke 19:18: The second came, saying, ‘Your mina, master, has made five minas.’
verses.Luke 19:19: And he said to him also, ‘And you are to be over five cities.’
verses.Luke 19:20: Another came, saying, ‘Master, here is your mina, which I kept put away in a handkerchief;
verses.Luke 19:21: for I was afraid of you, because you are an exacting man; you take up what you did not lay down and reap what you did not sow.’
verses.Luke 19:22: He said to him, ‘By your own words I will judge you, you worthless slave. Did you know that I am an exacting man, taking up what I did not lay down and reaping what I did not sow?
verses.Luke 19:23: Then why did you not put my money in the bank, and having come, I would have collected it with interest?’
verses.Luke 19:24: Then he said to the bystanders, ‘Take the mina away from him and give it to the one who has the ten minas.’
verses.Luke 19:25: And they said to him, ‘Master, he has ten minas already.’
verses.Luke 19:26: ‘I tell you that to everyone who has, more shall be given, but from the one who does not have, even what he does have shall be taken away.
verses.Luke 19:27: But these enemies of mine, who did not want me to reign over them, bring them here and slay them in my presence.’ ”
verses.Luke 19:30: saying, “Go into the village ahead of you; there, as you enter, you will find a colt tied on which no one yet has ever sat; untie it and bring it here.
verses.Luke 19:31: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ you shall say, ‘The Lord has need of it.’ ”
verses.Luke 19:40: But Jesus answered, “I tell you, if these become silent, the stones will cry out!”
verses.Luke 19:42: saying, “If you had known in this day, even you, the things which make for peace! But now they have been hidden from your eyes.
verses.Luke 19:43: For the days will come upon you when your enemies will throw up a barricade against you, and surround you and hem you in on every side,
verses.Luke 19:44: and they will level you to the ground and your children within you, and they will not leave in you one stone upon another, because you did not recognize the time of your visitation.”
verses.Luke 19:46: saying to them, “It is written, ‘And My house shall be a house of prayer,’ but you have made it a robbers ’den.”
verses.Luke 20:3: Jesus answered and said to them, “I will also ask you a question, and you tell Me:
verses.Luke 20:4: Was the baptism of John from heaven or from men?”
verses.Luke 20:8: And Jesus said to them, “Nor will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
verses.Luke 20:9: And He began to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard and rented it out to vine-growers, and went on a journey for a long time.
verses.Luke 20:10: At the harvest time he sent a slave to the vine-growers, so that they would give him some of the produce of the vineyard; but the vine-growers beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
verses.Luke 20:11: And he proceeded to send another slave; and they beat him also and treated him shamefully and sent him away empty-handed.
verses.Luke 20:12: And he proceeded to send a third; and this one also they wounded and cast out.
verses.Luke 20:13: The owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.’
verses.Luke 20:14: But when the vine-growers saw him, they reasoned with one another, saying, ‘This is the heir; let us kill him so that the inheritance will be ours.’
verses.Luke 20:15: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What, then, will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
verses.Luke 20:16: He will come and destroy these vine-growers and will give the vineyard to others.” When they heard it, they said, “May it never be!”
verses.Luke 20:17: But Jesus looked at them and said, “What then is this that is written: ‘The stone which the builders rejected, This became the chief corner stone’?
verses.Luke 20:18: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces; but on whomever it falls, it will scatter him like dust.”
verses.Luke 20:24: “Show Me a denarius. Whose likeness and inscription does it have?” They said, “Caesar’s.”
verses.Luke 20:25: And He said to them, “Then render to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
verses.Luke 20:34: Jesus said to them, “The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage,
verses.Luke 20:35: but those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry nor are given in marriage;
verses.Luke 20:36: for they cannot even die anymore, because they are like angels, and are sons of God, being sons of the resurrection.
verses.Luke 20:37: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed, in the passage about the burning bush, where he calls the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
verses.Luke 20:38: Now He is not the God of the dead but of the living; for all live to Him.”
verses.Luke 20:41: Then He said to them, “How is it that they say the Christ is David’s son?
verses.Luke 20:42: For David himself says in the book of Psalms, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand,
verses.Luke 20:43: Until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet.” ’
verses.Luke 20:44: Therefore David calls Him ‘Lord,’ and how is He his son?”
verses.Luke 20:46: “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love respectful greetings in the market places, and chief seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets,
verses.Luke 20:47: who devour widows’ houses, and for appearance’s sake offer long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.”
verses.Luke 21:3: And He said, “Truly I say to you, this poor widow put in more than all of them;
verses.Luke 21:4: for they all out of their surplus put into the offering; but she out of her poverty put in all that she had to live on.”
verses.Luke 21:6: “As for these things which you are looking at, the days will come in which there will not be left one stone upon another which will not be torn down.”
verses.Luke 21:8: And He said, “See to it that you are not misled; for many will come in My name, saying, ‘I am He,’ and, ‘The time is near.’ Do not go after them.
verses.Luke 21:9: When you hear of wars and disturbances, do not be terrified; for these things must take place first, but the end does not follow immediately.”
verses.Luke 21:10: Then He continued by saying to them, “Nation will rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom,
verses.Luke 21:11: and there will be great earthquakes, and in various places plagues and famines; and there will be terrors and great signs from heaven.
verses.Luke 21:12: “But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, delivering you to the synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for My name’s sake.
verses.Luke 21:13: It will lead to an opportunity for your testimony.
verses.Luke 21:14: So make up your minds not to prepare beforehand to defend yourselves;
verses.Luke 21:15: for I will give you utterance and wisdom which none of your opponents will be able to resist or refute.
verses.Luke 21:16: But you will be betrayed even by parents and brothers and relatives and friends, and they will put some of you to death,
verses.Luke 21:17: and you will be hated by all because of My name.
verses.Luke 21:18: Yet not a hair of your head will perish.
verses.Luke 21:19: By your endurance you will gain your lives.
verses.Luke 21:20: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then recognize that her desolation is near.
verses.Luke 21:21: Then those who are in Judea must flee to the mountains, and those who are in the midst of the city must leave, and those who are in the country must not enter the city;
verses.Luke 21:22: because these are days of vengeance, so that all things which are written will be fulfilled.
verses.Luke 21:23: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days; for there will be great distress upon the land and wrath to this people;
verses.Luke 21:24: and they will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all the nations; and Jerusalem will be trampled under foot by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
verses.Luke 21:25: “There will be signs in sun and moon and stars, and on the earth dismay among nations, in perplexity at the roaring of the sea and the waves,
verses.Luke 21:26: men fainting from fear and the expectation of the things which are coming upon the world; for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
verses.Luke 21:27: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
verses.Luke 21:28: But when these things begin to take place, straighten up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”
verses.Luke 21:29: Then He told them a parable: “Behold the fig tree and all the trees;
verses.Luke 21:30: as soon as they put forth leaves, you see it and know for yourselves that summer is now near.
verses.Luke 21:31: So you also, when you see these things happening, recognize that the kingdom of God is near.
verses.Luke 21:32: Truly I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all things take place.
verses.Luke 21:33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will not pass away.
verses.Luke 21:34: “Be on guard, so that your hearts will not be weighted down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of life, and that day will not come on you suddenly like a trap;
verses.Luke 21:35: for it will come upon all those who dwell on the face of all the earth.
verses.Luke 21:36: But keep on the alert at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that are about to take place, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
verses.Luke 22:8: And Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us, so that we may eat it.”
verses.Luke 22:10: And He said to them, “When you have entered the city, a man will meet you carrying a pitcher of water; follow him into the house that he enters.
verses.Luke 22:11: And you shall say to the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room in which I may eat the Passover with My disciples?” ’
verses.Luke 22:12: And he will show you a large, furnished upper room; prepare it there.”
verses.Luke 22:15: And He said to them, “I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer;
verses.Luke 22:16: for I say to you, I shall never again eat it until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.”
verses.Luke 22:17: And when He had taken a cup and given thanks, He said, “Take this and share it among yourselves;

verses.Luke 22:25: And He said to them, “The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them; and those who have authority over them are called ‘Benefactors.’
verses.Luke 22:26: But it is not this way with you, but the one who is the greatest among you must become like the youngest, and the leader like the servant.

verses.Luke 22:28: “You are those who have stood by Me in My trials;

verses.Luke 22:34: And He said, “I say to you, Peter, the rooster will not crow today until you have denied three times that you know Me.”
verses.Luke 22:35: And He said to them, “When I sent you out without money belt and bag and sandals, you did not lack anything, did you?” They said, “No, nothing.”

verses.Luke 22:38: They said, “Lord, look, here are two swords.” And He said to them, “It is enough.”

verses.Luke 24:17: And He said to them, “What are these words that you are exchanging with one another as you are walking?” And they stood still, looking sad.

verses.Luke 24:26: Was it not necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and to enter into His glory?”

verses.Luke 24:41: While they still could not believe it because of their joy and amazement, He said to them, “Have you anything here to eat?”
verses.Luke 24:44: Now He said to them, “These are My words which I spoke to you while I was still with you, that all things which are written about Me in the Law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms must be fulfilled.”

verses.John 1:38: And Jesus turned and saw them following, and said to them, “What do you seek?” They said to Him, “Rabbi (which translated means Teacher), where are You staying?”

verses.John 3:3: Jesus answered and said to him, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born again he cannot see the kingdom of God.”
verses.John 3:5: Jesus answered, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.

verses.John 3:7: Do not be amazed that I said to you, ‘You must be born again.’
verses.John 3:8: The wind blows where it wishes and you hear the sound of it, but do not know where it comes from and where it is going; so is everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
verses.John 3:10: Jesus answered and said to him, “Are you the teacher of Israel and do not understand these things?
verses.John 3:11: Truly, truly, I say to you, we speak of what we know and testify of what we have seen, and you do not accept our testimony.
verses.John 3:12: If I told you earthly things and you do not believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
verses.John 3:13: No one has ascended into heaven, but He who descended from heaven: the Son of Man.
verses.John 3:14: As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of Man be lifted up;
verses.John 3:15: so that whoever believes will in Him have eternal life.
verses.John 3:16: “For God so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him shall not perish, but have eternal life.
verses.John 3:17: For God did not send the Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through Him.
verses.John 3:18: He who believes in Him is not judged; he who does not believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.
verses.John 3:19: This is the judgment, that the Light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the Light, for their deeds were evil.
verses.John 3:20: For everyone who does evil hates the Light, and does not come to the Light for fear that his deeds will be exposed.
verses.John 3:21: But he who practices the truth comes to the Light, so that his deeds may be manifested as having been wrought in God.”
verses.John 4:7: There came a woman of Samaria to draw water. Jesus said to her, “Give Me a drink.”
verses.John 4:10: Jesus answered and said to her, “If you knew the gift of God, and who it is who says to you, ‘Give Me a drink,’ you would have asked Him, and He would have given you living water.”
verses.John 4:13: Jesus answered and said to her, “Everyone who drinks of this water will thirst again;
verses.John 4:14: but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him shall never thirst; but the water that I will give him will become in him a well of water springing up to eternal life.”
verses.John 4:16: He said to her, “Go, call your husband and come here.”
verses.John 4:17: The woman answered and said, “I have no husband.” Jesus said to her, “You have correctly said, ‘I have no husband’;
verses.John 4:18: for you have had five husbands, and the one whom you now have is not your husband; this you have said truly.”
verses.John 4:21: Jesus said to her, “Woman, believe Me, an hour is coming when neither in this mountain nor in Jerusalem will you worship the Father.
verses.John 4:22: You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, for salvation is from the Jews.
verses.John 4:23: But an hour is coming, and now is, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth; for such people the Father seeks to be His worshipers.
verses.John 4:24: God is spirit, and those who worship Him must worship in spirit and truth.”
verses.John 4:26: Jesus said to her, “I who speak to you am He.”
verses.John 4:32: But He said to them, “I have food to eat that you do not know about.”
verses.John 4:34: Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of Him who sent Me and to accomplish His work.
verses.John 4:35: Do you not say, ‘There are yet four months, and then comes the harvest’? Behold, I say to you, lift up your eyes and look on the fields, that they are white for harvest.
verses.John 4:36: Already he who reaps is receiving wages and is gathering fruit for life eternal; so that he who sows and he who reaps may rejoice together.
verses.John 4:37: For in this case the saying is true, ‘One sows and another reaps.’
verses.John 4:38: I sent you to reap that for which you have not labored; others have labored and you have entered into their labor.”
verses.John 4:48: So Jesus said to him, “Unless you people see signs and wonders, you simply will not believe.”

verses.John 5:6: When Jesus saw him lying there, and knew that he had already been a long time in that condition, He said to him, “Do you wish to get well?”
verses.John 5:8: Jesus said to him, “Get up, pick up your pallet and walk.”
verses.John 5:11: But he answered them, “He who made me well was the one who said to me, ‘Pick up your pallet and walk.’”
verses.John 5:12: They asked him, “Who is the man who said to you, ‘Pick up your pallet and walk’?”
verses.John 5:14: Afterward Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, “Behold, you have become well; do not sin anymore, so that nothing worse happens to you.”
verses.John 5:17: But He answered them, “My Father is working until now, and I Myself am working.”
verses.John 5:19: Therefore Jesus answered and was saying to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, the Son can do nothing of Himself, unless it is something He sees the Father doing; for whatever the Father does, these things the Son also does in like manner.
verses.John 5:20: For the Father loves the Son, and shows Him all things that He Himself is doing; and the Father will show Him greater works than these, so that you will marvel.
verses.John 5:21: For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son also gives life to whom He wishes.
verses.John 5:22: For not even the Father judges anyone, but He has given all judgment to the Son,
verses.John 5:23: so that all will honor the Son even as they honor the Father. He who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent Him.
verses.John 5:24: “Truly, truly, I say to you, he who hears My word, and believes Him who sent Me, has eternal life, and does not come into judgment, but has passed out of death into life.
verses.John 5:25: Truly, truly, I say to you, an hour is coming and now is, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.
verses.John 5:26: For just as the Father has life in Himself, even so He gave to the Son also to have life in Himself;
verses.John 5:27: and He gave Him authority to execute judgment, because He is the Son of Man.
verses.John 5:28: Do not marvel at this; for an hour is coming, in which all who are in the tombs will hear His voice,
verses.John 5:29: and will come forth; those who did the good deeds to a resurrection of life, those who committed the evil deeds to a resurrection of judgment.
verses.John 5:30: “I can do nothing on My own initiative. As I hear, I judge; and My judgment is just, because I do not seek My own will, but the will of Him who sent Me.
verses.John 5:31: “If I alone testify about Myself, My testimony is not true.
verses.John 5:32: There is another who testifies of Me, and I know that the testimony which He gives about Me is true.
verses.John 5:33: You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.
verses.John 5:34: But the testimony which I receive is not from man, but I say these things so that you may be saved.
verses.John 5:35: He was the lamp that was burning and was shining and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
verses.John 5:36: But the testimony which I have is greater than the testimony of John; for the works which the Father has given Me to accomplish—the very works that I do—testify about Me, that the Father has sent Me.
verses.John 5:37: And the Father who sent Me, He has testified of Me. You have neither heard His voice at any time nor seen His form.
verses.John 5:38: You do not have His word abiding in you, for you do not believe Him whom He sent.
verses.John 5:39: You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; it is these that testify about Me;
verses.John 5:40: and you are unwilling to come to Me so that you may have life.
verses.John 5:41: I do not receive glory from men;
verses.John 5:42: but I know you, that you do not have the love of God in yourselves.
verses.John 5:43: I have come in My Father’s name, and you do not receive Me; if another comes in his own name, you will receive him.
verses.John 5:44: How can you believe, when you receive glory from one another and you do not seek the glory that is from the one and only God?
verses.John 5:45: Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father; the one who accuses you is Moses, in whom you have set your hope.
verses.John 5:46: For if you believed Moses, you would believe Me, for he wrote about Me.
verses.John 5:47: But if you do not believe his writings, how will you believe My words?”

verses.John 6:33: For the bread of God is that which comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world.”
verses.John 6:35: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life; he who comes to Me will not hunger, and he who believes in Me will never thirst.

verses.John 6:37: All that the Father gives Me will come to Me, and the one who comes to Me I will certainly not cast out.
verses.John 6:38: For I have come down from heaven, not to do My own will, but the will of Him who sent Me.
verses.John 6:39: This is the will of Him who sent Me, that of all that He has given Me I lose nothing, but raise it up on the last day.
verses.John 6:40: For this is the will of My Father, that everyone who beholds the Son and believes in Him will have eternal life, and I Myself will raise him up on the last day.”
verses.John 6:41: Therefore the Jews were grumbling about Him, because He said, “I am the bread that came down out of heaven.”
verses.John 6:42: They were saying, “Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How does He now say, ‘I have come down out of heaven’?”
verses.John 6:43: Jesus answered and said to them, “Do not grumble among yourselves.
verses.John 6:44: No one can come to Me unless the Father who sent Me draws him; and I will raise him up on the last day.
verses.John 6:45: It is written in the prophets, ‘And they shall all be taught of God.’ Everyone who has heard and learned from the Father, comes to Me.
verses.John 6:46: Not that anyone has seen the Father, except the One who is from God; He has seen the Father.
verses.John 6:47: Truly, truly, I say to you, he who believes has eternal life.
verses.John 6:48: I am the bread of life.
verses.John 6:49: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
verses.John 6:50: This is the bread which comes down out of heaven, so that one may eat of it and not die.
verses.John 6:51: I am the living bread that came down out of heaven; if anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever; and the bread also which I will give for the life of the world is My flesh.”
verses.John 6:53: So Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink His blood, you have no life in yourselves.
verses.John 6:54: He who eats My flesh and drinks My blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.
verses.John 6:55: For My flesh is true food, and My blood is true drink.
verses.John 6:56: He who eats My flesh and drinks My blood abides in Me, and I in him.
verses.John 6:57: As the living Father sent Me, and I live because of the Father, so he who eats Me, he also will live because of Me.
verses.John 6:58: This is the bread which came down out of heaven; not as the fathers ate and died; he who eats this bread will live forever.”
verses.John 6:61: But Jesus, conscious that His disciples grumbled at this, said to them, “Does this cause you to stumble?

verses.John 6:63: It is the Spirit who gives life; the flesh profits nothing; the words that I have spoken to you are spirit and are life.
verses.John 6:64: But there are some of you who do not believe.” For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who did not believe, and who it was that would betray Him.

verses.John 7:24: Do not judge according to appearance, but judge with righteous judgment.”

verses.John 7:34: You will seek Me, and will not find Me; and where I am, you cannot come.”

verses.John 7:37: Now on the last day, the great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, saying, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to Me and drink.

verses.John 8:15: You judge according to the flesh; I am not judging anyone.
verses.John 8:16: But even if I do judge, My judgment is true; for I am not alone in it, but I and the Father who sent Me.
verses.John 8:17: Even in your law it has been written that the testimony of two men is true.
verses.John 8:18: I am He who testifies about Myself, and the Father who sent Me testifies about Me.”
verses.John 8:19: So they were saying to Him, “Where is Your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither Me nor My Father; if you knew Me, you would know My Father also.”
verses.John 8:21: Then He said again to them, “I go away, and you will seek Me, and will die in your sin; where I am going, you cannot come.”

verses.John 8:23: And He was saying to them, “You are from below, I am from above; you are of this world, I am not of this world.

verses.John 8:25: So they were saying to Him, “Who are You?” Jesus said to them, “What have I been saying to you from the beginning?
