verses.John 8:34: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, everyone who commits sin is the slave of sin.
verses.John 8:35: The slave does not remain in the house forever; the son does remain forever.
verses.John 8:36: So if the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.
verses.John 8:37: I know that you are Abraham’s descendants; yet you seek to kill Me, because My word has no place in you.
verses.John 8:38: I speak the things which I have seen with My Father; therefore you also do the things which you heard from your father.”
verses.John 8:39: They answered and said to Him, “Abraham is our father.” Jesus said to them, “If you are Abraham’s children, do the deeds of Abraham.
verses.John 8:40: But as it is, you are seeking to kill Me, a man who has told you the truth, which I heard from God; this Abraham did not do.
verses.John 8:41: You are doing the deeds of your father.” They said to Him, “We were not born of fornication; we have one Father: God.”
verses.John 8:42: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love Me, for I proceeded forth and have come from God, for I have not even come on My own initiative, but He sent Me.
verses.John 8:43: Why do you not understand what I am saying? It is because you cannot hear My word.
verses.John 8:44: You are of your father the devil, and you want to do the desires of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth because there is no truth in him. Whenever he speaks a lie, he speaks from his own nature, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
verses.John 8:45: But because I speak the truth, you do not believe Me.
verses.John 8:46: Which one of you convicts Me of sin? If I speak truth, why do you not believe Me?
verses.John 8:47: He who is of God hears the words of God; for this reason you do not hear them, because you are not of God.”
verses.John 8:49: Jesus answered, “I do not have a demon; but I honor My Father, and you dishonor Me.
verses.John 8:50: But I do not seek My glory; there is One who seeks and judges.
verses.John 8:51: Truly, truly, I say to you, if anyone keeps My word he will never see death.”
verses.John 8:52: The Jews said to Him, “Now we know that You have a demon. Abraham died, and the prophets also; and You say, ‘If anyone keeps My word, he will never taste of death.’
verses.John 8:54: Jesus answered, “If I glorify Myself, My glory is nothing; it is My Father who glorifies Me, of whom you say, ‘He is our God’;
verses.John 8:55: and you have not come to know Him, but I know Him; and if I say that I do not know Him, I will be a liar like you, but I do know Him and keep His word.
verses.John 8:56: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see My day, and he saw it and was glad.”
verses.John 8:58: Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was born, I am.”
verses.John 9:3: Jesus answered, “It was neither that this man sinned, nor his parents; but it was so that the works of God might be displayed in him.
verses.John 9:4: We must work the works of Him who sent Me as long as it is day; night is coming when no one can work.
verses.John 9:5: While I am in the world, I am the Light of the world.”
verses.John 9:7: and said to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which is translated, Sent). So he went away and washed, and came back seeing.
verses.John 9:11: He answered, “The man who is called Jesus made clay, and anointed my eyes, and said to me, ‘Go to Siloam and wash’; so I went away and washed, and I received sight.”
verses.John 9:35: Jesus heard that they had put him out, and finding him, He said, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
verses.John 9:37: Jesus said to him, “You have both seen Him, and He is the one who is talking with you.”
verses.John 9:39: And Jesus said, “For judgment I came into this world, so that those who do not see may see, and that those who see may become blind.”
verses.John 9:41: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would have no sin; but since you say, ‘We see,’ your sin remains.
verses.John 10:1: “Truly, truly, I say to you, he who does not enter by the door into the fold of the sheep, but climbs up some other way, he is a thief and a robber.
verses.John 10:2: But he who enters by the door is a shepherd of the sheep.
verses.John 10:3: To him the doorkeeper opens, and the sheep hear his voice, and he calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
verses.John 10:4: When he puts forth all his own, he goes ahead of them, and the sheep follow him because they know his voice.
verses.John 10:5: A stranger they simply will not follow, but will flee from him, because they do not know the voice of strangers.”
verses.John 10:7: So Jesus said to them again, “Truly, truly, I say to you, I am the door of the sheep.
verses.John 10:8: All who came before Me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them.
verses.John 10:9: I am the door; if anyone enters through Me, he will be saved, and will go in and out and find pasture.
verses.John 10:10: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I came that they may have life, and have it abundantly.
verses.John 10:11: “I am the good shepherd; the good shepherd lays down His life for the sheep.
verses.John 10:12: He who is a hired hand, and not a shepherd, who is not the owner of the sheep, sees the wolf coming, and leaves the sheep and flees, and the wolf snatches them and scatters them.
verses.John 10:13: He flees because he is a hired hand and is not concerned about the sheep.
verses.John 10:14: I am the good shepherd, and I know My own and My own know Me,
verses.John 10:15: even as the Father knows Me and I know the Father; and I lay down My life for the sheep.
verses.John 10:16: I have other sheep, which are not of this fold; I must bring them also, and they will hear My voice; and they will become one flock with one shepherd.
verses.John 10:17: For this reason the Father loves Me, because I lay down My life so that I may take it again.
verses.John 10:18: No one has taken it away from Me, but I lay it down on My own initiative. I have authority to lay it down, and I have authority to take it up again. This commandment I received from My Father.”
verses.John 10:25: Jesus answered them, “I told you, and you do not believe; the works that I do in My Father’s name, these testify of Me.
verses.John 10:26: But you do not believe because you are not of My sheep.
verses.John 10:27: My sheep hear My voice, and I know them, and they follow Me;
verses.John 10:28: and I give eternal life to them, and they will never perish; and no one will snatch them out of My hand.
verses.John 10:29: My Father, who has given them to Me, is greater than all; and no one is able to snatch them out of the Father’s hand.
verses.John 10:30: I and the Father are one.”
verses.John 10:32: Jesus answered them, “I showed you many good works from the Father; for which of them are you stoning Me?”
verses.John 10:34: Jesus answered them, “Has it not been written in your Law, ‘I said, you are gods’?
verses.John 10:35: If he called them gods, to whom the word of God came (and the Scripture cannot be broken),
verses.John 10:36: do you say of Him, whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, ‘You are blaspheming,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God’?
verses.John 10:37: If I do not do the works of My Father, do not believe Me;
verses.John 10:38: but if I do them, though you do not believe Me, believe the works, so that you may know and understand that the Father is in Me, and I in the Father.”
verses.John 11:4: But when Jesus heard this, He said, “This sickness is not to end in death, but for the glory of God, so that the Son of God may be glorified by it.”
verses.John 11:7: Then after this He said to the disciples, “Let us go to Judea again.”
verses.John 11:9: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours in the day? If anyone walks in the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
verses.John 11:10: But if anyone walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.”
verses.John 11:11: This He said, and after that He said to them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep; but I go, so that I may awaken him out of sleep.”
verses.John 11:14: So Jesus then said to them plainly, “Lazarus is dead,
verses.John 11:15: and I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, so that you may believe; but let us go to him.”
verses.John 11:23: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”
verses.John 11:25: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life; he who believes in Me will live even if he dies,
verses.John 11:26: and everyone who lives and believes in Me will never die. Do you believe this?”
verses.John 11:34: and said, “Where have you laid him?” They said to Him, “Lord, come and see.”
verses.John 11:39: Jesus said, “Remove the stone.” Martha, the sister of the deceased, said to Him, “Lord, by this time there will be a stench, for he has been dead four days.”
verses.John 11:40: Jesus said to her, “Did I not say to you that if you believe, you will see the glory of God?”
verses.John 11:41: So they removed the stone. Then Jesus raised His eyes, and said, “Father, I thank You that You have heard Me.
verses.John 11:42: I knew that You always hear Me; but because of the people standing around I said it, so that they may believe that You sent Me.”
verses.John 11:43: When He had said these things, He cried out with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come forth.”
verses.John 11:44: The man who had died came forth, bound hand and foot with wrappings, and his face was wrapped around with a cloth. Jesus said to them, “Unbind him, and let him go.”
verses.John 12:7: Therefore Jesus said, “Let her alone, so that she may keep it for the day of My burial.
verses.John 12:8: For you always have the poor with you, but you do not always have Me.”
verses.John 12:23: And Jesus answered them, saying, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
verses.John 12:24: Truly, truly, I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.
verses.John 12:25: He who loves his life loses it, and he who hates his life in this world will keep it to life eternal.
verses.John 12:26: If anyone serves Me, he must follow Me; and where I am, there My servant will be also; if anyone serves Me, the Father will honor him.
verses.John 12:27: “Now My soul has become troubled; and what shall I say, ‘Father, save Me from this hour’? But for this purpose I came to this hour.
verses.John 12:28: Father, glorify Your name.” Then a voice came out of heaven: “I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.”
verses.John 12:30: Jesus answered and said, “This voice has not come for My sake, but for your sakes.
verses.John 12:31: Now judgment is upon this world; now the ruler of this world will be cast out.
verses.John 12:32: And I, if I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all men to Myself.”
verses.John 12:34: The crowd then answered Him, “We have heard out of the Law that the Christ is to remain forever; and how can You say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up’? Who is this Son of Man?”
verses.John 12:35: So Jesus said to them, “For a little while longer the Light is among you. Walk while you have the Light, so that darkness will not overtake you; he who walks in the darkness does not know where he goes.
verses.John 12:36: While you have the Light, believe in the Light, so that you may become sons of Light.” These things Jesus spoke, and He went away and hid Himself from them.
verses.John 12:44: And Jesus cried out and said, “He who believes in Me, does not believe in Me but in Him who sent Me.
verses.John 12:45: He who sees Me sees the One who sent Me.
verses.John 12:46: I have come as Light into the world, so that everyone who believes in Me will not remain in darkness.
verses.John 12:47: If anyone hears My sayings and does not keep them, I do not judge him; for I did not come to judge the world, but to save the world.
verses.John 12:48: He who rejects Me and does not receive My sayings, has one who judges him; the word I spoke is what will judge him at the last day.
verses.John 12:49: For I did not speak on My own initiative, but the Father Himself who sent Me has given Me a commandment as to what to say and what to speak.
verses.John 12:50: I know that His commandment is eternal life; therefore the things I speak, I speak just as the Father has told Me.”
verses.John 13:7: Jesus answered and said to him, “What I do you do not realize now, but you will understand hereafter.”
verses.John 13:8: Peter said to Him, “Never shall You wash my feet!” Jesus answered him, “If I do not wash you, you have no part with Me.”
verses.John 13:10: Jesus said to him, “He who has bathed needs only to wash his feet, but is completely clean; and you are clean, but not all of you.”
verses.John 13:11: For He knew the one who was betraying Him; for this reason He said, “Not all of you are clean.”
verses.John 13:12: So when He had washed their feet, and taken His garments and reclined at the table again, He said to them, “Do you know what I have done to you?
verses.John 13:13: You call Me Teacher and Lord; and you are right, for so I am.
verses.John 13:14: If I then, the Lord and the Teacher, washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another’s feet.
verses.John 13:15: For I gave you an example that you also should do as I did to you.
verses.John 13:16: Truly, truly, I say to you, a slave is not greater than his master, nor is one who is sent greater than the one who sent him.
verses.John 13:17: If you know these things, you are blessed if you do them.
verses.John 13:18: I do not speak of all of you. I know the ones I have chosen; but it is that the Scripture may be fulfilled, ‘He who eats My bread has lifted up his heel against Me.’
verses.John 13:19: From now on I am telling you before it comes to pass, so that when it does occur, you may believe that I am He.
verses.John 13:20: Truly, truly, I say to you, he who receives whomever I send receives Me; and he who receives Me receives Him who sent Me.”
verses.John 13:21: When Jesus had said this, He became troubled in spirit, and testified and said, “Truly, truly, I say to you, that one of you will betray Me.”
verses.John 13:26: Jesus then answered, “That is the one for whom I shall dip the morsel and give it to him.” So when He had dipped the morsel, He took and gave it to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot.
verses.John 13:27: After the morsel, Satan then entered into him. Therefore Jesus said to him, “What you do, do quickly.”
verses.John 13:31: Therefore when he had gone out, Jesus said, “Now is the Son of Man glorified, and God is glorified in Him;
verses.John 13:32: if God is glorified in Him, God will also glorify Him in Himself, and will glorify Him immediately.
verses.John 13:33: Little children, I am with you a little while longer. You will seek Me; and as I said to the Jews, now I also say to you, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come.’
verses.John 13:34: A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another, even as I have loved you, that you also love one another.
verses.John 13:35: By this all men will know that you are My disciples, if you have love for one another.”
verses.John 13:36: Simon Peter said to Him, “Lord, where are You going?” Jesus answered, “Where I go, you cannot follow Me now; but you will follow later.”
verses.John 13:38: Jesus answered, “Will you lay down your life for Me? Truly, truly, I say to you, a rooster will not crow until you deny Me three times.
verses.John 14:1: “Do not let your heart be troubled; believe in God, believe also in Me.
verses.John 14:2: In My Father’s house are many dwelling places; if it were not so, I would have told you; for I go to prepare a place for you.
verses.John 14:3: If I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and receive you to Myself, that where I am, there you may be also.
verses.John 14:4: And you know the way where I am going.”
verses.John 14:6: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life; no one comes to the Father but through Me.
verses.John 14:7: If you had known Me, you would have known My Father also; from now on you know Him, and have seen Him.”
verses.John 14:9: Jesus said to him, “Have I been so long with you, and yet you have not come to know Me, Philip? He who has seen Me has seen the Father; how can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
verses.John 14:10: Do you not believe that I am in the Father, and the Father is in Me? The words that I say to you I do not speak on My own initiative, but the Father abiding in Me does His works.
verses.John 14:11: Believe Me that I am in the Father and the Father is in Me; otherwise believe because of the works themselves.
verses.John 14:12: Truly, truly, I say to you, he who believes in Me, the works that I do, he will do also; and greater works than these he will do; because I go to the Father.
verses.John 14:13: Whatever you ask in My name, that will I do, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
verses.John 14:14: If you ask Me anything in My name, I will do it.
verses.John 14:15: “If you love Me, you will keep My commandments.
verses.John 14:16: I will ask the Father, and He will give you another Helper, that He may be with you forever;
verses.John 14:17: that is the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it does not see Him or know Him, but you know Him because He abides with you and will be in you.
verses.John 14:18: “I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you.
verses.John 14:19: After a little while the world will no longer see Me, but you will see Me; because I live, you will live also.
verses.John 14:20: In that day you will know that I am in My Father, and you in Me, and I in you.
verses.John 14:21: He who has My commandments and keeps them is the one who loves Me; and he who loves Me will be loved by My Father, and I will love him and will disclose Myself to him.”
verses.John 14:23: Jesus answered and said to him, “If anyone loves Me, he will keep My word; and My Father will love him, and We will come to him and make Our abode with him.
verses.John 14:24: He who does not love Me does not keep My words; and the word which you hear is not Mine, but the Father’s who sent Me.
verses.John 14:25: “These things I have spoken to you while abiding with you.
verses.John 14:26: But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in My name, He will teach you all things, and bring to your remembrance all that I said to you.
verses.John 14:27: Peace I leave with you; My peace I give to you; not as the world gives do I give to you. Do not let your heart be troubled, nor let it be fearful.
verses.John 14:28: You heard that I said to you, ‘I go away, and I will come to you.’ If you loved Me, you would have rejoiced because I go to the Father, for the Father is greater than I.
verses.John 14:29: Now I have told you before it happens, so that when it happens, you may believe.
verses.John 14:30: I will not speak much more with you, for the ruler of the world is coming, and he has nothing in Me;
verses.John 14:31: but so that the world may know that I love the Father, I do exactly as the Father commanded Me. Get up, let us go from here.
verses.John 15:1: “I am the true vine, and My Father is the vinedresser.
verses.John 15:2: Every branch in Me that does not bear fruit, He takes away; and every branch that bears fruit, He prunes it so that it may bear more fruit.
verses.John 15:3: You are already clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.
verses.John 15:4: Abide in Me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself unless it abides in the vine, so neither can you unless you abide in Me.
verses.John 15:5: I am the vine, you are the branches; he who abides in Me and I in him, he bears much fruit, for apart from Me you can do nothing.
verses.John 15:6: If anyone does not abide in Me, he is thrown away as a branch and dries up; and they gather them, and cast them into the fire and they are burned.
verses.John 15:7: If you abide in Me, and My words abide in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
verses.John 15:8: My Father is glorified by this, that you bear much fruit, and so prove to be My disciples.
verses.John 15:9: Just as the Father has loved Me, I have also loved you; abide in My love.
verses.John 15:10: If you keep My commandments, you will abide in My love; just as I have kept My Father’s commandments and abide in His love.
verses.John 15:11: These things I have spoken to you so that My joy may be in you, and that your joy may be made full.
verses.John 15:12: “This is My commandment, that you love one another, just as I have loved you.
verses.John 15:13: Greater love has no one than this, that one lay down his life for his friends.
verses.John 15:14: You are My friends if you do what I command you.
verses.John 15:15: No longer do I call you slaves, for the slave does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you friends, for all things that I have heard from My Father I have made known to you.
verses.John 15:16: You did not choose Me but I chose you, and appointed you that you would go and bear fruit, and that your fruit would remain, so that whatever you ask of the Father in My name He may give to you.
verses.John 15:17: This I command you, that you love one another.
verses.John 15:18: “If the world hates you, you know that it has hated Me before it hated you.
verses.John 15:19: If you were of the world, the world would love its own; but because you are not of the world, but I chose you out of the world, because of this the world hates you.
verses.John 15:20: Remember the word that I said to you, ‘A slave is not greater than his master.’ If they persecuted Me, they will also persecute you; if they kept My word, they will keep yours also.
verses.John 15:21: But all these things they will do to you for My name’s sake, because they do not know the One who sent Me.
verses.John 15:22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have sin, but now they have no excuse for their sin.
verses.John 15:23: He who hates Me hates My Father also.
verses.John 15:24: If I had not done among them the works which no one else did, they would not have sin; but now they have both seen and hated Me and My Father as well.
verses.John 15:25: But they have done this to fulfill the word that is written in their Law, ‘They hated Me without a cause.’
verses.John 15:26: “When the Helper comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, that is the Spirit of truth who proceeds from the Father, He will testify about Me,
verses.John 15:27: and you will testify also, because you have been with Me from the beginning.
verses.John 16:1: “These things I have spoken to you so that you may be kept from stumbling.
verses.John 16:2: They will make you outcasts from the synagogue, but an hour is coming for everyone who kills you to think that he is offering service to God.
verses.John 16:3: These things they will do because they have not known the Father or Me.
verses.John 16:4: But these things I have spoken to you, so that when their hour comes, you may remember that I told you of them. These things I did not say to you at the beginning, because I was with you.
verses.John 16:5: “But now I am going to Him who sent Me; and none of you asks Me, ‘Where are You going?’
verses.John 16:6: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your heart.
verses.John 16:7: But I tell you the truth, it is to your advantage that I go away; for if I do not go away, the Helper will not come to you; but if I go, I will send Him to you.
verses.John 16:8: And He, when He comes, will convict the world concerning sin and righteousness and judgment;
verses.John 16:9: concerning sin, because they do not believe in Me;
verses.John 16:10: and concerning righteousness, because I go to the Father and you no longer see Me;
verses.John 16:11: and concerning judgment, because the ruler of this world has been judged.
verses.John 16:12: “I have many more things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
verses.John 16:13: But when He, the Spirit of truth, comes, He will guide you into all the truth; for He will not speak on His own initiative, but whatever He hears, He will speak; and He will disclose to you what is to come.
verses.John 16:14: He will glorify Me, for He will take of Mine and will disclose it to you.
verses.John 16:15: All things that the Father has are Mine; therefore I said that He takes of Mine and will disclose it to you.
verses.John 16:16: “A little while, and you will no longer see Me; and again a little while, and you will see Me.”
verses.John 16:17: Some of His disciples then said to one another, “What is this thing He is telling us, ‘A little while, and you will not see Me; and again a little while, and you will see Me’; and, ‘because I go to the Father’?”
verses.John 16:18: So they were saying, “What is this that He says, ‘A little while’? We do not know what He is talking about.”
verses.John 16:19: Jesus knew that they wished to question Him, and He said to them, “Are you deliberating together about this, that I said, ‘A little while, and you will not see Me, and again a little while, and you will see Me’?
verses.John 16:20: Truly, truly, I say to you, that you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice; you will grieve, but your grief will be turned into joy.
verses.John 16:21: Whenever a woman is in labor she has pain, because her hour has come; but when she gives birth to the child, she no longer remembers the anguish because of the joy that a child has been born into the world.
verses.John 16:22: Therefore you too have grief now; but I will see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and no one will take your joy away from you.
verses.John 16:23: In that day you will not question Me about anything. Truly, truly, I say to you, if you ask the Father for anything in My name, He will give it to you.
verses.John 16:24: Until now you have asked for nothing in My name; ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be made full.
verses.John 16:25: “These things I have spoken to you in figurative language; an hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figurative language, but will tell you plainly of the Father.
verses.John 16:26: In that day you will ask in My name, and I do not say to you that I will request of the Father on your behalf;
verses.John 16:27: for the Father Himself loves you, because you have loved Me and have believed that I came forth from the Father.
verses.John 16:28: I came forth from the Father and have come into the world; I am leaving the world again and going to the Father.”
verses.John 16:31: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?
verses.John 16:32: Behold, an hour is coming, and has already come, for you to be scattered, each to his own home, and to leave Me alone; and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with Me.
verses.John 16:33: These things I have spoken to you, so that in Me you may have peace. In the world you have tribulation, but take courage; I have overcome the world.”
verses.John 17:1: Jesus spoke these things; and lifting up His eyes to heaven, He said, “Father, the hour has come; glorify Your Son, that the Son may glorify You,
verses.John 17:2: even as You gave Him authority over all flesh, that to all whom You have given Him, He may give eternal life.
verses.John 17:3: This is eternal life, that they may know You, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom You have sent.
verses.John 17:4: I glorified You on the earth, having accomplished the work which You have given Me to do.
verses.John 17:5: Now, Father, glorify Me together with Yourself, with the glory which I had with You before the world was.
verses.John 17:6: “I have manifested Your name to the men whom You gave Me out of the world; they were Yours and You gave them to Me, and they have kept Your word.
verses.John 17:7: Now they have come to know that everything You have given Me is from You;
verses.John 17:8: for the words which You gave Me I have given to them; and they received them and truly understood that I came forth from You, and they believed that You sent Me.
verses.John 17:9: I ask on their behalf; I do not ask on behalf of the world, but of those whom You have given Me; for they are Yours;
verses.John 17:10: and all things that are Mine are Yours, and Yours are Mine; and I have been glorified in them.
verses.John 17:11: I am no longer in the world; and yet they themselves are in the world, and I come to You. Holy Father, keep them in Your name, the name which You have given Me, that they may be one even as We are.
verses.John 17:12: While I was with them, I was keeping them in Your name which You have given Me; and I guarded them and not one of them perished but the son of perdition, so that the Scripture would be fulfilled.
verses.John 17:13: But now I come to You; and these things I speak in the world so that they may have My joy made full in themselves.
verses.John 17:14: I have given them Your word; and the world has hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
verses.John 17:15: I do not ask You to take them out of the world, but to keep them from the evil one.
verses.John 17:16: They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
verses.John 17:17: Sanctify them in the truth; Your word is truth.
verses.John 17:18: As You sent Me into the world, I also have sent them into the world.
verses.John 17:19: For their sakes I sanctify Myself, that they themselves also may be sanctified in truth.
verses.John 17:20: “I do not ask on behalf of these alone, but for those also who believe in Me through their word;
verses.John 17:21: that they may all be one; even as You, Father, are in Me and I in You, that they also may be in Us, so that the world may believe that You sent Me.
verses.John 17:22: The glory which You have given Me I have given to them, that they may be one, just as We are one;
verses.John 17:23: I in them and You in Me, that they may be perfected in unity, so that the world may know that You sent Me, and loved them, even as You have loved Me.
verses.John 17:24: Father, I desire that they also, whom You have given Me, be with Me where I am, so that they may see My glory which You have given Me, for You loved Me before the foundation of the world.
verses.John 17:25: “O righteous Father, although the world has not known You, yet I have known You; and these have known that You sent Me;
verses.John 17:26: and I have made Your name known to them, and will make it known, so that the love with which You loved Me may be in them, and I in them.”
verses.John 18:4: So Jesus, knowing all the things that were coming upon Him, went forth and said to them, “Whom do you seek?”
verses.John 18:5: They answered Him, “Jesus the Nazarene.” He said to them, “I am He.” And Judas also, who was betraying Him, was standing with them.
verses.John 18:6: So when He said to them, “I am He,” they drew back and fell to the ground.
verses.John 18:7: Therefore He again asked them, “Whom do you seek?” And they said, “Jesus the Nazarene.”
verses.John 18:8: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am He; so if you seek Me, let these go their way,”
verses.John 18:9: to fulfill the word which He spoke, “Of those whom You have given Me I lost not one.”
verses.John 18:11: So Jesus said to Peter, “Put the sword into the sheath; the cup which the Father has given Me, shall I not drink it?”
verses.John 18:20: Jesus answered him, “I have spoken openly to the world; I always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews come together; and I spoke nothing in secret.
verses.John 18:21: Why do you question Me? Question those who have heard what I spoke to them; they know what I said.”
verses.John 18:23: Jesus answered him, “If I have spoken wrongly, testify of the wrong; but if rightly, why do you strike Me?”
verses.John 18:34: Jesus answered, “Are you saying this on your own initiative, or did others tell you about Me?”
verses.John 18:36: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not of this world. If My kingdom were of this world, then My servants would be fighting so that I would not be handed over to the Jews; but as it is, My kingdom is not of this realm.”
verses.John 18:37: Therefore Pilate said to Him, “So You are a king?” Jesus answered, “You say correctly that I am a king. For this I have been born, and for this I have come into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth hears My voice.”
verses.John 19:11: Jesus answered, “You would have no authority over Me, unless it had been given you from above; for this reason he who delivered Me to you has the greater sin.”
verses.John 19:26: When Jesus then saw His mother, and the disciple whom He loved standing nearby, He said to His mother, “Woman, behold, your son!”
verses.John 19:27: Then He said to the disciple, “Behold, your mother!” From that hour the disciple took her into his own household.
verses.John 19:28: After this, Jesus, knowing that all things had already been accomplished, to fulfill the Scripture, said, “I am thirsty.”
verses.John 19:30: Therefore when Jesus had received the sour wine, He said, “It is finished!” And He bowed His head and gave up His spirit.
verses.John 20:15: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you seeking?” Supposing Him to be the gardener, she said to Him, “Sir, if you have carried Him away, tell me where you have laid Him, and I will take Him away.”
verses.John 20:16: Jesus said to her, “Mary!” She turned and said to Him in Hebrew, “Rabboni!” (which means, Teacher).
verses.John 20:17: Jesus said to her, “Stop clinging to Me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father; but go to My brethren and say to them, ‘I ascend to My Father and your Father, and My God and your God.’ ”
verses.John 20:19: So when it was evening on that day, the first day of the week, and when the doors were shut where the disciples were, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in their midst and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
verses.John 20:21: So Jesus said to them again, “Peace be with you; as the Father has sent Me, I also send you.”
verses.John 20:22: And when He had said this, He breathed on them and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
verses.John 20:23: If you forgive the sins of any, their sins have been forgiven them; if you retain the sins of any, they have been retained.”
verses.John 20:26: After eight days His disciples were again inside, and Thomas with them. Jesus came, the doors having been shut, and stood in their midst and said, “Peace be with you.”
verses.John 20:27: Then He said to Thomas, “Reach here with your finger, and see My hands; and reach here your hand and put it into My side; and do not be unbelieving, but believing.”
verses.John 20:29: Jesus said to him, “Because you have seen Me, have you believed? Blessed are they who did not see, and yet believed.”
verses.John 21:5: So Jesus said to them, “Children, you do not have any fish, do you?” They answered Him, “No.”
verses.John 21:6: And He said to them, “Cast the net on the right-hand side of the boat and you will find a catch.” So they cast, and then they were not able to haul it in because of the great number of fish.
verses.John 21:10: Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish which you have now caught.”
verses.John 21:12: Jesus said to them, “Come and have breakfast.” None of the disciples ventured to question Him, “Who are You?” knowing that it was the Lord.
verses.John 21:15: So when they had finished breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon, son of John, do you love Me more than these?” He said to Him, “Yes, Lord; You know that I love You.” He said to him, “Tend My lambs.”
verses.John 21:16: He said to him again a second time, “Simon, son of John, do you love Me?” He said to Him, “Yes, Lord; You know that I love You.” He said to him, “Shepherd My sheep.”
verses.John 21:17: He said to him the third time, “Simon, son of John, do you love Me?” Peter was grieved because He said to him the third time, “Do you love Me?” And he said to Him, “Lord, You know all things; You know that I love You.” Jesus said to him, “Tend My sheep.
verses.John 21:18: Truly, truly, I say to you, when you were younger, you used to gird yourself and walk wherever you wished; but when you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and someone else will gird you, and bring you where you do not wish to go.”
verses.John 21:19: Now this He said, signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God. And when He had spoken this, He said to him, “Follow Me!”
verses.John 21:22: Jesus said to him, “If I want him to remain until I come, what is that to you? You follow Me!”
verses.John 21:23: Therefore this saying went out among the brethren that that disciple would not die; yet Jesus did not say to him that he would not die, but only, “If I want him to remain until I come, what is that to you?”
verses.Matthew 1:1: The record of the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah, the son of David, the son of Abraham:
verses.Matthew 1:2: Abraham was the father of Isaac, Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers.
verses.Matthew 1:3: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, Perez was the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Ram.
verses.Matthew 1:4: Ram was the father of Amminadab, Amminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon.
verses.Matthew 1:5: Salmon was the father of Boaz by Rahab, Boaz was the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed the father of Jesse.
verses.Matthew 1:6: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon by Bathsheba who had been the wife of Uriah.
verses.Matthew 1:7: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam, Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asa.
verses.Matthew 1:8: Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah.
verses.Matthew 1:9: Uzziah was the father of Jotham, Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah.
verses.Matthew 1:10: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh, Manasseh the father of Amon, and Amon the father of Josiah.
verses.Matthew 1:11: Josiah became the father of Jeconiah and his brothers, at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
verses.Matthew 1:12: After the deportation to Babylon: Jeconiah became the father of Shealtiel, and Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel.
verses.Matthew 1:13: Zerubbabel was the father of Abihud, Abihud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor.
verses.Matthew 1:14: Azor was the father of Zadok, Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud.
verses.Matthew 1:15: Eliud was the father of Eleazar, Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob.
verses.Matthew 1:16: Jacob was the father of Joseph the husband of Mary, by whom Jesus was born, who is called the Messiah.
verses.Matthew 1:17: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; from David to the deportation to Babylon, fourteen generations; and from the deportation to Babylon to the Messiah, fourteen generations.
verses.Matthew 1:18: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was as follows: when His mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph, before they came together she was found to be with child by the Holy Spirit.
verses.Matthew 1:19: And Joseph her husband, being a righteous man and not wanting to disgrace her, planned to send her away secretly.
verses.Matthew 1:20: But when he had considered this, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife; for the Child who has been conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.
verses.Matthew 1:21: She will bear a Son; and you shall call His name Jesus, for He will save His people from their sins.”
verses.Matthew 1:22: Now all this took place to fulfill what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet:
verses.Matthew 1:23: “Behold, the virgin shall be with child and shall bear a Son, and they shall call His name Immanuel,” which translated means, “God with us.”
verses.Matthew 1:24: And Joseph awoke from his sleep and did as the angel of the Lord commanded him, and took Mary as his wife,
verses.Matthew 1:25: but kept her a virgin until she gave birth to a Son; and he called His name Jesus.
verses.Matthew 2:1: Now after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of Herod the king, magi from the east arrived in Jerusalem, saying,
verses.Matthew 2:2: “Where is He who has been born King of the Jews? For we saw His star in the east and have come to worship Him.”
verses.Matthew 2:3: When Herod the king heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
verses.Matthew 2:4: Gathering together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Messiah was to be born.
verses.Matthew 2:5: They said to him, “In Bethlehem of Judea; for this is what has been written by the prophet:
verses.Matthew 2:6: ‘And you, Bethlehem, land of Judah, Are by no means least among the leaders of Judah; For out of you shall come forth a Ruler Who will shepherd My people Israel.’ ”
verses.Matthew 2:7: Then Herod secretly called the magi and determined from them the exact time the star appeared.
verses.Matthew 2:8: And he sent them to Bethlehem and said, “Go and search carefully for the Child; and when you have found Him, report to me, so that I too may come and worship Him.”
verses.Matthew 2:9: After hearing the king, they went their way; and the star, which they had seen in the east, went on before them until it came and stood over the place where the Child was.
verses.Matthew 2:10: When they saw the star, they rejoiced exceedingly with great joy.
verses.Matthew 2:11: After coming into the house they saw the Child with Mary His mother; and they fell to the ground and worshiped Him. Then, opening their treasures, they presented to Him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
verses.Matthew 2:12: And having been warned by God in a dream not to return to Herod, the magi left for their own country by another way.
verses.Matthew 2:13: Now when they had gone, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Take the Child and His mother and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you; for Herod is going to search for the Child to destroy Him.”
verses.Matthew 2:14: So Joseph got up and took the Child and His mother while it was still night, and left for Egypt.
verses.Matthew 2:15: He remained there until the death of Herod. This was to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet: “Out of Egypt I called My Son.”
verses.Matthew 2:16: Then when Herod saw that he had been tricked by the magi, he became very enraged, and sent and slew all the male children who were in Bethlehem and all its vicinity, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had determined from the magi.
verses.Matthew 2:17: Then what had been spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled:
verses.Matthew 2:18: “A voice was heard in Ramah, Weeping and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children; And she refused to be comforted, Because they were no more.”
verses.Matthew 2:19: But when Herod died, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt, and said,
verses.Matthew 2:20: “Get up, take the Child and His mother, and go into the land of Israel; for those who sought the Child’s life are dead.”
verses.Matthew 2:21: So Joseph got up, took the Child and His mother, and came into the land of Israel.
verses.Matthew 2:22: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. Then after being warned by God in a dream, he left for the regions of Galilee,
verses.Matthew 2:23: and came and lived in a city called Nazareth. This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophets: “He shall be called a Nazarene.”
verses.Matthew 3:1: Now in those days John the Baptist came, preaching in the wilderness of Judea, saying,
verses.Matthew 3:2: “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”
verses.Matthew 3:3: For this is the one referred to by Isaiah the prophet when he said, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make ready the way of the Lord, Make His paths straight!’ ”
verses.Matthew 3:4: Now John himself had a garment of camel’s hair and a leather belt around his waist; and his food was locusts and wild honey.
verses.Matthew 3:5: Then Jerusalem was going out to him, and all Judea and all the district around the Jordan;
verses.Matthew 3:6: and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River, as they confessed their sins.
verses.Matthew 3:7: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming for baptism, he said to them, “You brood of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
verses.Matthew 3:8: Therefore bear fruit in keeping with repentance;
verses.Matthew 3:9: and do not suppose that you can say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham for our father’; for I say to you that from these stones God is able to raise up children to Abraham.
verses.Matthew 3:10: The axe is already laid at the root of the trees; therefore every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
verses.Matthew 3:11: “As for me, I baptize you with water for repentance, but He who is coming after me is mightier than I, and I am not fit to remove His sandals; He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
verses.Matthew 3:12: His winnowing fork is in His hand, and He will thoroughly clear His threshing floor; and He will gather His wheat into the barn, but He will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”
verses.Matthew 3:13: Then Jesus arrived from Galilee at the Jordan coming to John, to be baptized by him.
verses.Matthew 3:14: But John tried to prevent Him, saying, “I have need to be baptized by You, and do You come to me?”
verses.Matthew 3:16: After being baptized, Jesus came up immediately from the water; and behold, the heavens were opened, and he saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove and lighting on Him,
verses.Matthew 3:17: and behold, a voice out of the heavens said, “This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well-pleased.”
verses.Matthew 4:1: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
verses.Matthew 4:2: And after He had fasted forty days and forty nights, He then became hungry.
verses.Matthew 4:3: And the tempter came and said to Him, “If You are the Son of God, command that these stones become bread.”
verses.Matthew 4:5: Then the devil took Him into the holy city and had Him stand on the pinnacle of the temple,
verses.Matthew 4:6: and said to Him, “If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down; for it is written, ‘He will command His angels concerning You’; and ‘On their hands they will bear You up, So that You will not strike Your foot against a stone.’ ”
verses.Matthew 4:8: Again, the devil took Him to a very high mountain and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory;
verses.Matthew 4:9: and he said to Him, “All these things I will give You, if You fall down and worship me.”
verses.Matthew 4:11: Then the devil left Him; and behold, angels came and began to minister to Him.
verses.Matthew 4:12: Now when Jesus heard that John had been taken into custody, He withdrew into Galilee;
verses.Matthew 4:13: and leaving Nazareth, He came and settled in Capernaum, which is by the sea, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali.
verses.Matthew 4:14: This was to fulfill what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet:
verses.Matthew 4:15: “The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, By the way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles—
verses.Matthew 4:16: The people who were sitting in darkness saw a great Light, And those who were sitting in the land and shadow of death, Upon them a Light dawned.”
verses.Matthew 4:18: Now as Jesus was walking by the Sea of Galilee, He saw two brothers, Simon who was called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen.
verses.Matthew 4:20: Immediately they left their nets and followed Him.
verses.Matthew 4:21: Going on from there He saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets; and He called them.
verses.Matthew 4:22: Immediately they left the boat and their father, and followed Him.
verses.Matthew 4:23: Jesus was going throughout all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every kind of disease and every kind of sickness among the people.
verses.Matthew 4:24: The news about Him spread throughout all Syria; and they brought to Him all who were ill, those suffering with various diseases and pains, demoniacs, epileptics, paralytics; and He healed them.
verses.Matthew 4:25: Large crowds followed Him from Galilee and the Decapolis and Jerusalem and Judea and from beyond the Jordan.
verses.Matthew 5:1: When Jesus saw the crowds, He went up on the mountain; and after He sat down, His disciples came to Him.
verses.Matthew 5:2: He opened His mouth and began to teach them, saying,
verses.Matthew 7:28: When Jesus had finished these words, the crowds were amazed at His teaching;
verses.Matthew 7:29: for He was teaching them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.
verses.Matthew 8:1: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed Him.
verses.Matthew 8:2: And a leper came to Him and bowed down before Him, and said, “Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.”
verses.Matthew 8:5: And when Jesus entered Capernaum, a centurion came to Him, imploring Him,
verses.Matthew 8:6: and saying, “Lord, my servant is lying paralyzed at home, fearfully tormented.”
verses.Matthew 8:8: But the centurion said, “Lord, I am not worthy for You to come under my roof, but just say the word, and my servant will be healed.
verses.Matthew 8:9: For I also am a man under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to this one, ‘Go!’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes, and to my slave, ‘Do this!’ and he does it.”
verses.Matthew 8:14: When Jesus came into Peter’s home, He saw his mother-in-law lying sick in bed with a fever.
verses.Matthew 8:15: He touched her hand, and the fever left her; and she got up and waited on Him.
verses.Matthew 8:16: When evening came, they brought to Him many who were demon-possessed; and He cast out the spirits with a word, and healed all who were ill.
verses.Matthew 8:17: This was to fulfill what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet: “He Himself took our infirmities and carried away our diseases.”
verses.Matthew 8:18: Now when Jesus saw a crowd around Him, He gave orders to depart to the other side of the sea.
verses.Matthew 8:19: Then a scribe came and said to Him, “Teacher, I will follow You wherever You go.”
verses.Matthew 8:21: Another of the disciples said to Him, “Lord, permit me first to go and bury my father.”
verses.Matthew 8:23: When He got into the boat, His disciples followed Him.
verses.Matthew 8:24: And behold, there arose a great storm on the sea, so that the boat was being covered with the waves; but Jesus Himself was asleep.
verses.Matthew 8:25: And they came to Him and woke Him, saying, “Save us, Lord; we are perishing!”
verses.Matthew 8:27: The men were amazed, and said, “What kind of a man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey Him?”
verses.Matthew 8:28: When He came to the other side into the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed met Him as they were coming out of the tombs. They were so extremely violent that no one could pass by that way.
verses.Matthew 8:29: And they cried out, saying, “What business do we have with each other, Son of God? Have You come here to torment us before the time?”
verses.Matthew 8:30: Now there was a herd of many swine feeding at a distance from them.
verses.Matthew 8:31: The demons began to entreat Him, saying, “If You are going to cast us out, send us into the herd of swine.”
verses.Matthew 8:33: The herdsmen ran away, and went to the city and reported everything, including what had happened to the demoniacs.
verses.Matthew 8:34: And behold, the whole city came out to meet Jesus; and when they saw Him, they implored Him to leave their region.
verses.Matthew 9:1: Getting into a boat, Jesus crossed over the sea and came to His own city.
verses.Matthew 9:3: And some of the scribes said to themselves, “This fellow blasphemes.”
verses.Matthew 9:7: And he got up and went home.
verses.Matthew 9:8: But when the crowds saw this, they were awestruck, and glorified God, who had given such authority to men.
verses.Matthew 9:10: Then it happened that as Jesus was reclining at the table in the house, behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and were dining with Jesus and His disciples.
verses.Matthew 9:11: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to His disciples, “Why is your Teacher eating with the tax collectors and sinners?”
verses.Matthew 9:14: Then the disciples of John came to Him, asking, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast, but Your disciples do not fast?”
verses.Matthew 9:18: While He was saying these things to them, a synagogue official came and bowed down before Him, and said, “My daughter has just died; but come and lay Your hand on her, and she will live.”
verses.Matthew 9:19: Jesus got up and began to follow him, and so did His disciples.
verses.Matthew 9:20: And a woman who had been suffering from a hemorrhage for twelve years, came up behind Him and touched the fringe of His cloak;
verses.Matthew 9:21: for she was saying to herself, “If I only touch His garment, I will get well.”
verses.Matthew 9:23: When Jesus came into the official’s house, and saw the flute-players and the crowd in noisy disorder,
verses.Matthew 9:25: But when the crowd had been sent out, He entered and took her by the hand, and the girl got up.
verses.Matthew 9:26: This news spread throughout all that land.
verses.Matthew 9:27: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed Him, crying out, “Have mercy on us, Son of David!”
verses.Matthew 9:31: But they went out and spread the news about Him throughout all that land.
verses.Matthew 9:32: As they were going out, a mute, demon-possessed man was brought to Him.
verses.Matthew 9:33: After the demon was cast out, the mute man spoke; and the crowds were amazed, and were saying, “Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.”
verses.Matthew 9:34: But the Pharisees were saying, “He casts out the demons by the ruler of the demons.”
verses.Matthew 9:35: Jesus was going through all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every kind of disease and every kind of sickness.
verses.Matthew 9:36: Seeing the people, He felt compassion for them, because they were distressed and dispirited like sheep without a shepherd.
verses.Matthew 10:1: Jesus summoned His twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal every kind of disease and every kind of sickness.
verses.Matthew 10:2: Now the names of the twelve apostles are these: The first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; and James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother;
verses.Matthew 10:3: Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus;
verses.Matthew 10:4: Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot, the one who betrayed Him.
verses.Matthew 11:1: When Jesus had finished giving instructions to His twelve disciples, He departed from there to teach and preach in their cities.
verses.Matthew 11:2: Now when John, while imprisoned, heard of the works of Christ, he sent word by his disciples
verses.Matthew 11:3: and said to Him, “Are You the Expected One, or shall we look for someone else?”
verses.Matthew 11:20: Then He began to denounce the cities in which most of His miracles were done, because they did not repent.
verses.Matthew 12:1: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath, and His disciples became hungry and began to pick the heads of grain and eat.
verses.Matthew 12:2: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to Him, “Look, Your disciples do what is not lawful to do on a Sabbath.”
verses.Matthew 12:9: Departing from there, He went into their synagogue.
verses.Matthew 12:10: And a man was there whose hand was withered. And they questioned Jesus, asking, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”—so that they might accuse Him.
verses.Matthew 12:14: But the Pharisees went out and conspired against Him, as to how they might destroy Him.
verses.Matthew 12:15: But Jesus, aware of this, withdrew from there. Many followed Him, and He healed them all,
verses.Matthew 12:16: and warned them not to tell who He was.
verses.Matthew 12:17: This was to fulfill what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet:
verses.Matthew 12:18: “Behold, My Servant whom I have chosen; My Beloved in whom My soul is well- pleased; I will put My Spirit upon Him, And He shall proclaim justice to the Gentiles.
verses.Matthew 12:19: He will not quarrel, nor cry out; Nor will anyone hear His voice in the streets.
verses.Matthew 12:20: A battered reed He will not break off, And a smoldering wick He will not put out, Until He leads justice to victory.
verses.Matthew 12:21: And in His name the Gentiles will hope.”
verses.Matthew 12:22: Then a demon-possessed man who was blind and mute was brought to Jesus, and He healed him, so that the mute man spoke and saw.
verses.Matthew 12:23: All the crowds were amazed, and were saying, “This man cannot be the Son of David, can he?”
verses.Matthew 12:24: But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, “This man casts out demons only by Beelzebul the ruler of the demons.”
verses.Matthew 12:38: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to Him, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from You.”
verses.Matthew 12:46: While He was still speaking to the crowds, behold, His mother and brothers were standing outside, seeking to speak to Him.
verses.Matthew 12:47: Someone said to Him, “Behold, Your mother and Your brothers are standing outside seeking to speak to You.”
verses.Matthew 13:1: That day Jesus went out of the house and was sitting by the sea.
verses.Matthew 13:2: And large crowds gathered to Him, so He got into a boat and sat down, and the whole crowd was standing on the beach.
verses.Matthew 13:10: And the disciples came and said to Him, “Why do You speak to them in parables?”
verses.Matthew 13:34: All these things Jesus spoke to the crowds in parables, and He did not speak to them without a parable.
verses.Matthew 13:35: This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet: “I will open My mouth in parables; I will utter things hidden since the foundation of the world.”
verses.Matthew 13:36: Then He left the crowds and went into the house. And His disciples came to Him and said, “Explain to us the parable of the tares of the field.”
verses.Matthew 13:53: When Jesus had finished these parables, He departed from there.
verses.Matthew 13:54: He came to His hometown and began teaching them in their synagogue, so that they were astonished, and said, “Where did this man get this wisdom and these miraculous powers?
verses.Matthew 13:55: Is not this the carpenter’s son? Is not His mother called Mary, and His brothers, James and Joseph and Simon and Judas?
verses.Matthew 13:56: And His sisters, are they not all with us? Where then did this man get all these things?”
verses.Matthew 13:58: And He did not do many miracles there because of their unbelief.
verses.Matthew 14:1: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard the news about Jesus,
verses.Matthew 14:2: and said to his servants, “This is John the Baptist; he has risen from the dead, and that is why miraculous powers are at work in him.”
verses.Matthew 14:3: For when Herod had John arrested, he bound him and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip.
verses.Matthew 14:4: For John had been saying to him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
verses.Matthew 14:5: Although Herod wanted to put him to death, he feared the crowd, because they regarded John as a prophet.
verses.Matthew 14:6: But when Herod’s birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced before them and pleased Herod,
verses.Matthew 14:7: so much that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she asked.
verses.Matthew 14:8: Having been prompted by her mother, she said, “Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptist.”
verses.Matthew 14:9: Although he was grieved, the king commanded it to be given because of his oaths, and because of his dinner guests.
verses.Matthew 14:10: He sent and had John beheaded in the prison.
verses.Matthew 14:11: And his head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, and she brought it to her mother.
verses.Matthew 14:12: His disciples came and took away the body and buried it; and they went and reported to Jesus.
verses.Matthew 14:13: Now when Jesus heard about John, He withdrew from there in a boat to a secluded place by Himself; and when the people heard of this, they followed Him on foot from the cities.
verses.Matthew 14:14: When He went ashore, He saw a large crowd, and felt compassion for them and healed their sick.
verses.Matthew 14:15: When it was evening, the disciples came to Him and said, “This place is desolate and the hour is already late; so send the crowds away, that they may go into the villages and buy food for themselves.”
verses.Matthew 14:17: They said to Him, “We have here only five loaves and two fish.”
verses.Matthew 14:19: Ordering the people to sit down on the grass, He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up toward heaven, He blessed the food, and breaking the loaves He gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds,
verses.Matthew 14:20: and they all ate and were satisfied. They picked up what was left over of the broken pieces, twelve full baskets.
verses.Matthew 14:21: There were about five thousand men who ate, besides women and children.
verses.Matthew 14:22: Immediately He made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead of Him to the other side, while He sent the crowds away.
verses.Matthew 14:23: After He had sent the crowds away, He went up on the mountain by Himself to pray; and when it was evening, He was there alone.
verses.Matthew 14:24: But the boat was already a long distance from the land, battered by the waves; for the wind was contrary.
verses.Matthew 14:25: And in the fourth watch of the night He came to them, walking on the sea.
verses.Matthew 14:26: When the disciples saw Him walking on the sea, they were terrified, and said, “It is a ghost!” And they cried out in fear.
verses.Matthew 14:28: Peter said to Him, “Lord, if it is You, command me to come to You on the water.”
verses.Matthew 14:30: But seeing the wind, he became frightened, and beginning to sink, he cried out, “Lord, save me!”
verses.Matthew 14:32: When they got into the boat, the wind stopped.
verses.Matthew 14:33: And those who were in the boat worshiped Him, saying, “You are certainly God’s Son!”
verses.Matthew 14:34: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret.
verses.Matthew 14:35: And when the men of that place recognized Him, they sent word into all that surrounding district and brought to Him all who were sick;
verses.Matthew 14:36: and they implored Him that they might just touch the fringe of His cloak; and as many as touched it were cured.
verses.Matthew 15:1: Then some Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said,
verses.Matthew 15:2: “Why do Your disciples break the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash their hands when they eat bread.”
verses.Matthew 15:12: Then the disciples came and said to Him, “Do You know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this statement?”
verses.Matthew 15:15: Peter said to Him, “Explain the parable to us.”
verses.Matthew 15:21: Jesus went away from there, and withdrew into the district of Tyre and Sidon.
verses.Matthew 15:22: And a Canaanite woman from that region came out and began to cry out, saying, “Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David; my daughter is cruelly demon-possessed.”
verses.Matthew 15:23: But He did not answer her a word. And His disciples came and implored Him, saying, “Send her away, because she keeps shouting at us.”
verses.Matthew 15:25: But she came and began to bow down before Him, saying, “Lord, help me!”
verses.Matthew 15:27: But she said, “Yes, Lord; but even the dogs feed on the crumbs which fall from their masters’ table.”
verses.Matthew 15:29: Departing from there, Jesus went along by the Sea of Galilee, and having gone up on the mountain, He was sitting there.
verses.Matthew 15:30: And large crowds came to Him, bringing with them those who were lame, crippled, blind, mute, and many others, and they laid them down at His feet; and He healed them.
verses.Matthew 15:31: So the crowd marveled as they saw the mute speaking, the crippled restored, and the lame walking, and the blind seeing; and they glorified the God of Israel.
verses.Matthew 15:33: The disciples said to Him, “Where would we get so many loaves in this desolate place to satisfy such a large crowd?”
verses.Matthew 15:35: And He directed the people to sit down on the ground;
verses.Matthew 15:36: and He took the seven loaves and the fish; and giving thanks, He broke them and started giving them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people.
verses.Matthew 15:37: And they all ate and were satisfied, and they picked up what was left over of the broken pieces, seven large baskets full.
verses.Matthew 15:38: And those who ate were four thousand men, besides women and children.
verses.Matthew 15:39: And sending away the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
verses.Matthew 16:1: The Pharisees and Sadducees came up, and testing Jesus, they asked Him to show them a sign from heaven.
verses.Matthew 16:5: And the disciples came to the other side of the sea, but they had forgotten to bring any bread.
verses.Matthew 16:7: They began to discuss this among themselves, saying, “He said that because we did not bring any bread.”
verses.Matthew 16:12: Then they understood that He did not say to beware of the leaven of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
verses.Matthew 16:14: And they said, “Some say John the Baptist; and others, Elijah; but still others, Jeremiah, or one of the prophets.”
verses.Matthew 16:16: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”
verses.Matthew 16:20: Then He warned the disciples that they should tell no one that He was the Christ.
verses.Matthew 16:21: From that time Jesus began to show His disciples that He must go to Jerusalem, and suffer many things from the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised up on the third day.
verses.Matthew 16:22: Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him, saying, “God forbid it, Lord! This shall never happen to You.”
verses.Matthew 17:1: Six days later Jesus took with Him Peter and James and John his brother, and led them up on a high mountain by themselves.
verses.Matthew 17:2: And He was transfigured before them; and His face shone like the sun, and His garments became as white as light.
verses.Matthew 17:3: And behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Him.
verses.Matthew 17:4: Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here; if You wish, I will make three tabernacles here, one for You, and one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
verses.Matthew 17:5: While he was still speaking, a bright cloud overshadowed them, and behold, a voice out of the cloud said, “This is My beloved Son, with whom I am well-pleased; listen to Him!”
verses.Matthew 17:6: When the disciples heard this, they fell face down to the ground and were terrified.
verses.Matthew 17:8: And lifting up their eyes, they saw no one except Jesus Himself alone.
verses.Matthew 17:10: And His disciples asked Him, “Why then do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
verses.Matthew 17:13: Then the disciples understood that He had spoken to them about John the Baptist.
verses.Matthew 17:14: When they came to the crowd, a man came up to Jesus, falling on his knees before Him and saying,
verses.Matthew 17:15: “Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is a lunatic and is very ill; for he often falls into the fire and often into the water.
verses.Matthew 17:16: I brought him to Your disciples, and they could not cure him.”
verses.Matthew 17:18: And Jesus rebuked him, and the demon came out of him, and the boy was cured at once.
verses.Matthew 17:19: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and said, “Why could we not drive it out?”
verses.Matthew 17:24: When they came to Capernaum, those who collected the two-drachma tax came to Peter and said, “Does your teacher not pay the two-drachma tax?”
verses.Matthew 18:1: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and said, “Who then is greatest in the kingdom of heaven?”
verses.Matthew 18:2: And He called a child to Himself and set him before them,
verses.Matthew 18:21: Then Peter came and said to Him, “Lord, how often shall my brother sin against me and I forgive him? Up to seven times?”
verses.Matthew 19:1: When Jesus had finished these words, He departed from Galilee and came into the region of Judea beyond the Jordan;
verses.Matthew 19:2: and large crowds followed Him, and He healed them there.
verses.Matthew 19:3: Some Pharisees came to Jesus, testing Him and asking, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any reason at all?”
verses.Matthew 19:7: They said to Him, “Why then did Moses command to give her a certificate of divorce and send her away?”
verses.Matthew 19:10: The disciples said to Him, “If the relationship of the man with his wife is like this, it is better not to marry.”
verses.Matthew 19:13: Then some children were brought to Him so that He might lay His hands on them and pray; and the disciples rebuked them.
verses.Matthew 19:15: After laying His hands on them, He departed from there.
verses.Matthew 19:16: And someone came to Him and said, “Teacher, what good thing shall I do that I may obtain eternal life?”
verses.Matthew 19:20: The young man said to Him, “All these things I have kept; what am I still lacking?”
verses.Matthew 19:22: But when the young man heard this statement, he went away grieving; for he was one who owned much property.
verses.Matthew 19:25: When the disciples heard this, they were very astonished and said, “Then who can be saved?”
verses.Matthew 19:27: Then Peter said to Him, “Behold, we have left everything and followed You; what then will there be for us?”

verses.Matthew 20:24: And hearing this, the ten became indignant with the two brothers.
verses.Matthew 20:29: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed Him.
verses.Matthew 20:30: And two blind men sitting by the road, hearing that Jesus was passing by, cried out, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
verses.Matthew 20:31: The crowd sternly told them to be quiet, but they cried out all the more, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!”
verses.Matthew 20:33: They said to Him, “Lord, we want our eyes to be opened.”
verses.Matthew 20:34: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes; and immediately they regained their sight and followed Him.
verses.Matthew 21:1: When they had approached Jerusalem and had come to Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,
verses.Matthew 21:4: This took place to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet:
verses.Matthew 21:5: “Say to the daughter of Zion, ‘Behold your King is coming to you, Gentle, and mounted on a donkey, Even on a colt, the foal of a beast of burden.’ ”
verses.Matthew 21:6: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had instructed them,
verses.Matthew 21:7: and brought the donkey and the colt, and laid their coats on them; and He sat on the coats.
verses.Matthew 21:8: Most of the crowd spread their coats in the road, and others were cutting branches from the trees and spreading them in the road.
verses.Matthew 21:9: The crowds going ahead of Him, and those who followed, were shouting, “Hosanna to the Son of David; Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord; Hosanna in the highest!”
verses.Matthew 21:10: When He had entered Jerusalem, all the city was stirred, saying, “Who is this?”
verses.Matthew 21:11: And the crowds were saying, “This is the prophet Jesus, from Nazareth in Galilee.”
verses.Matthew 21:12: And Jesus entered the temple and drove out all those who were buying and selling in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling doves.
verses.Matthew 21:14: And the blind and the lame came to Him in the temple, and He healed them.
verses.Matthew 21:15: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonderful things that He had done, and the children who were shouting in the temple, “Hosanna to the Son of David,” they became indignant
verses.Matthew 21:17: And He left them and went out of the city to Bethany, and spent the night there.
verses.Matthew 21:18: Now in the morning, when He was returning to the city, He became hungry.
verses.Matthew 21:20: Seeing this, the disciples were amazed and asked, “How did the fig tree wither all at once?”

verses.Matthew 21:45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard His parables, they understood that He was speaking about them.
verses.Matthew 21:46: When they sought to seize Him, they feared the people, because they considered Him to be a prophet.
verses.Matthew 22:1: Jesus spoke to them again in parables, saying,
verses.Matthew 22:15: Then the Pharisees went and plotted together how they might trap Him in what He said.
verses.Matthew 22:16: And they sent their disciples to Him, along with the Herodians, saying, “Teacher, we know that You are truthful and teach the way of God in truth, and defer to no one; for You are not partial to any.
verses.Matthew 22:17: Tell us then, what do You think? Is it lawful to give a poll-tax to Caesar, or not?”
verses.Matthew 22:22: And hearing this, they were amazed, and leaving Him, they went away.
verses.Matthew 22:23: On that day some Sadducees (who say there is no resurrection) came to Jesus and questioned Him,
verses.Matthew 22:24: asking, “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies having no children, his brother as next of kin shall marry his wife, and raise up children for his brother.’
verses.Matthew 22:25: Now there were seven brothers with us; and the first married and died, and having no children left his wife to his brother;
verses.Matthew 22:26: so also the second, and the third, down to the seventh.
verses.Matthew 22:27: Last of all, the woman died.
verses.Matthew 22:28: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife of the seven will she be? For they all had married her.”
verses.Matthew 22:33: When the crowds heard this, they were astonished at His teaching.
verses.Matthew 22:34: But when the Pharisees heard that Jesus had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered themselves together.
verses.Matthew 22:35: One of them, a lawyer, asked Him a question, testing Him,
verses.Matthew 22:36: “Teacher, which is the great commandment in the Law?”
verses.Matthew 22:41: Now while the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them a question:
verses.Matthew 22:46: No one was able to answer Him a word, nor did anyone dare from that day on to ask Him another question.

verses.Matthew 26:1: When Jesus had finished all these words, He said to His disciples,
verses.Matthew 26:3: Then the chief priests and the elders of the people were gathered together in the court of the high priest, named Caiaphas;
verses.Matthew 26:4: and they plotted together to seize Jesus by stealth and kill Him.
verses.Matthew 26:5: But they were saying, “Not during the festival, otherwise a riot might occur among the people.”
verses.Matthew 26:6: Now when Jesus was in Bethany, at the home of Simon the leper,
verses.Matthew 26:7: a woman came to Him with an alabaster vial of very costly perfume, and she poured it on His head as He reclined at the table.
verses.Matthew 26:8: But the disciples were indignant when they saw this, and said, “Why this waste?
verses.Matthew 26:9: For this perfume might have been sold for a high price and the money given to the poor.”
verses.Matthew 26:14: Then one of the twelve, named Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests
verses.Matthew 26:15: and said, “What are you willing to give me to betray Him to you?” And they weighed out thirty pieces of silver to him.
verses.Matthew 26:16: From then on he began looking for a good opportunity to betray Jesus.
verses.Matthew 26:17: Now on the first day of Unleavened Bread the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Where do You want us to prepare for You to eat the Passover?”
verses.Matthew 26:19: The disciples did as Jesus had directed them; and they prepared the Passover.
verses.Matthew 26:20: Now when evening came, Jesus was reclining at the table with the twelve disciples.
verses.Matthew 26:22: Being deeply grieved, they each one began to say to Him, “Surely not I, Lord?”
verses.Matthew 26:30: After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
verses.Matthew 26:33: But Peter said to Him, “Even though all may fall away because of You, I will never fall away.”
verses.Matthew 26:35: Peter said to Him, “Even if I have to die with You, I will not deny You.” All the disciples said the same thing too.
verses.Matthew 26:37: And He took with Him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to be grieved and distressed.
verses.Matthew 26:43: Again He came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.
verses.Matthew 26:44: And He left them again, and went away and prayed a third time, saying the same thing once more.
verses.Matthew 26:47: While He was still speaking, behold, Judas, one of the twelve, came up accompanied by a large crowd with swords and clubs, who came from the chief priests and elders of the people.
verses.Matthew 26:48: Now he who was betraying Him gave them a sign, saying, “Whomever I kiss, He is the one; seize Him.”
verses.Matthew 26:49: Immediately Judas went to Jesus and said, “Hail, Rabbi!” and kissed Him.
verses.Matthew 26:51: And behold, one of those who were with Jesus reached and drew out his sword, and struck the slave of the high priest and cut off his ear.
verses.Matthew 26:57: Those who had seized Jesus led Him away to Caiaphas, the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were gathered together.
verses.Matthew 26:58: But Peter was following Him at a distance as far as the courtyard of the high priest, and entered in, and sat down with the officers to see the outcome.
verses.Matthew 26:59: Now the chief priests and the whole Council kept trying to obtain false testimony against Jesus, so that they might put Him to death.
verses.Matthew 26:60: They did not find any, even though many false witnesses came forward. But later on two came forward,
verses.Matthew 26:61: and said, “This man stated, ‘I am able to destroy the temple of God and to rebuild it in three days.’ ”
verses.Matthew 26:62: The high priest stood up and said to Him, “Do You not answer? What is it that these men are testifying against You?”
verses.Matthew 26:63: But Jesus kept silent. And the high priest said to Him, “I adjure You by the living God, that You tell us whether You are the Christ, the Son of God.”
verses.Matthew 26:65: Then the high priest tore his robes and said, “He has blasphemed! What further need do we have of witnesses? Behold, you have now heard the blasphemy;
verses.Matthew 26:66: what do you think?” They answered, “He deserves death!”
verses.Matthew 26:67: Then they spat in His face and beat Him with their fists; and others slapped Him,
verses.Matthew 26:68: and said, “Prophesy to us, You Christ; who is the one who hit You?”
