verses.Matthew 26:69: Now Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard, and a servant-girl came to him and said, “You too were with Jesus the Galilean.”
verses.Matthew 26:70: But he denied it before them all, saying, “I do not know what you are talking about.”
verses.Matthew 26:71: When he had gone out to the gateway, another servant-girl saw him and said to those who were there, “This man was with Jesus of Nazareth.”
verses.Matthew 26:72: And again he denied it with an oath, “I do not know the man.”
verses.Matthew 26:73: A little later the bystanders came up and said to Peter, “Surely you too are one of them; for even the way you talk gives you away.”
verses.Matthew 26:74: Then he began to curse and swear, “I do not know the man!” And immediately a rooster crowed.
verses.Matthew 27:1: Now when morning came, all the chief priests and the elders of the people conferred together against Jesus to put Him to death;
verses.Matthew 27:2: and they bound Him, and led Him away and delivered Him to Pilate the governor.
verses.Matthew 27:3: Then when Judas, who had betrayed Him, saw that He had been condemned, he felt remorse and returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
verses.Matthew 27:4: saying, “I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.” But they said, “What is that to us? See to that yourself!”
verses.Matthew 27:5: And he threw the pieces of silver into the temple sanctuary and departed; and he went away and hanged himself.
verses.Matthew 27:6: The chief priests took the pieces of silver and said, “It is not lawful to put them into the temple treasury, since it is the price of blood.”
verses.Matthew 27:7: And they conferred together and with the money bought the Potter’s Field as a burial place for strangers.
verses.Matthew 27:8: For this reason that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
verses.Matthew 27:9: Then that which was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled: “And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of the one whose price had been set by the sons of Israel;
verses.Matthew 27:10: and they gave them for the Potter’s Field, as the Lord directed me.”
verses.Matthew 27:12: And while He was being accused by the chief priests and elders, He did not answer.
verses.Matthew 27:13: Then Pilate said to Him, “Do You not hear how many things they testify against You?”
verses.Matthew 27:14: And He did not answer him with regard to even a single charge, so the governor was quite amazed.
verses.Matthew 27:15: Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to release for the people any one prisoner whom they wanted.
verses.Matthew 27:16: At that time they were holding a notorious prisoner, called Barabbas.
verses.Matthew 27:17: So when the people gathered together, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release for you? Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?”
verses.Matthew 27:18: For he knew that because of envy they had handed Him over.
verses.Matthew 27:19: While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent him a message, saying, “Have nothing to do with that righteous Man; for last night I suffered greatly in a dream because of Him.”
verses.Matthew 27:20: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and to put Jesus to death.
verses.Matthew 27:21: But the governor said to them, “Which of the two do you want me to release for you?” And they said, “Barabbas.”
verses.Matthew 27:22: Pilate said to them, “Then what shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?” They all said, “Crucify Him!”
verses.Matthew 27:23: And he said, “Why, what evil has He done?” But they kept shouting all the more, saying, “Crucify Him!”
verses.Matthew 27:24: When Pilate saw that he was accomplishing nothing, but rather that a riot was starting, he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd, saying, “I am innocent of this Man’s blood; see to that yourselves.”
verses.Matthew 27:25: And all the people said, “His blood shall be on us and on our children!”
verses.Matthew 27:26: Then he released Barabbas for them; but after having Jesus scourged, he handed Him over to be crucified.
verses.Matthew 27:27: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the Praetorium and gathered the whole Roman cohort around Him.
verses.Matthew 27:28: They stripped Him and put a scarlet robe on Him.
verses.Matthew 27:29: And after twisting together a crown of thorns, they put it on His head, and a reed in His right hand; and they knelt down before Him and mocked Him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
verses.Matthew 27:30: They spat on Him, and took the reed and began to beat Him on the head.
verses.Matthew 27:31: After they had mocked Him, they took the scarlet robe off Him and put His own garments back on Him, and led Him away to crucify Him.
verses.Matthew 27:32: As they were coming out, they found a man of Cyrene named Simon, whom they pressed into service to bear His cross.
verses.Matthew 27:33: And when they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Place of a Skull,
verses.Matthew 27:34: they gave Him wine to drink mixed with gall; and after tasting it, He was unwilling to drink.
verses.Matthew 27:35: And when they had crucified Him, they divided up His garments among themselves by casting lots.
verses.Matthew 27:36: And sitting down, they began to keep watch over Him there.
verses.Matthew 27:37: And above His head they put up the charge against Him which read, “THIS IS JESUS THE KING OF THE JEWS.”
verses.Matthew 27:38: At that time two robbers were crucified with Him, one on the right and one on the left.
verses.Matthew 27:39: And those passing by were hurling abuse at Him, wagging their heads
verses.Matthew 27:40: and saying, “You who are going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save Yourself! If You are the Son of God, come down from the cross.”
verses.Matthew 27:41: In the same way the chief priests also, along with the scribes and elders, were mocking Him and saying,
verses.Matthew 27:42: “He saved others; He cannot save Himself. He is the King of Israel; let Him now come down from the cross, and we will believe in Him.
verses.Matthew 27:43: He trusts in God;let God rescue Him now, if He delights in Him; for He said, ‘I am the Son of God.’ ”
verses.Matthew 27:44: The robbers who had been crucified with Him were also insulting Him with the same words.
verses.Matthew 27:45: Now from the sixth hour darkness fell upon all the land until the ninth hour.
verses.Matthew 27:47: And some of those who were standing there, when they heard it, began saying, “This man is calling for Elijah.”
verses.Matthew 27:48: Immediately one of them ran, and taking a sponge, he filled it with sour wine and put it on a reed, and gave Him a drink.
verses.Matthew 27:49: But the rest of them said, “Let us see whether Elijah will come to save Him.”
verses.Matthew 27:50: And Jesus cried out again with a loud voice, and yielded up His spirit.
verses.Matthew 27:51: And behold, the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom; and the earth shook and the rocks were split.
verses.Matthew 27:52: The tombs were opened, and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised;
verses.Matthew 27:53: and coming out of the tombs after His resurrection they entered the holy city and appeared to many.
verses.Matthew 27:54: Now the centurion, and those who were with him keeping guard over Jesus, when they saw the earthquake and the things that were happening, became very frightened and said, “Truly this was the Son of God!”
verses.Matthew 27:55: Many women were there looking on from a distance, who had followed Jesus from Galilee while ministering to Him.
verses.Matthew 27:56: Among them was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
verses.Matthew 27:57: When it was evening, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who himself had also become a disciple of Jesus.
verses.Matthew 27:58: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate ordered it to be given to him.
verses.Matthew 27:59: And Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
verses.Matthew 27:60: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock; and he rolled a large stone against the entrance of the tomb and went away.
verses.Matthew 27:61: And Mary Magdalene was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the grave.
verses.Matthew 27:62: Now on the next day, the day after the preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered together with Pilate,
verses.Matthew 27:63: and said, “Sir, we remember that when He was still alive that deceiver said, ‘After three days I am to rise again.’
verses.Matthew 27:64: Therefore, give orders for the grave to be made secure until the third day, otherwise His disciples may come and steal Him away and say to the people, ‘He has risen from the dead,’ and the last deception will be worse than the first.”
verses.Matthew 27:65: Pilate said to them, “You have a guard; go, make it as secure as you know how.”
verses.Matthew 27:66: And they went and made the grave secure, and along with the guard they set a seal on the stone.
verses.Matthew 28:1: Now after the Sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the grave.
verses.Matthew 28:2: And behold, a severe earthquake had occurred, for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven and came and rolled away the stone and sat upon it.
verses.Matthew 28:3: And his appearance was like lightning, and his clothing as white as snow.
verses.Matthew 28:4: The guards shook for fear of him and became like dead men.
verses.Matthew 28:5: The angel said to the women, “Do not be afraid; for I know that you are looking for Jesus who has been crucified.
verses.Matthew 28:6: He is not here, for He has risen, just as He said. Come, see the place where He was lying.
verses.Matthew 28:7: Go quickly and tell His disciples that He has risen from the dead; and behold, He is going ahead of you into Galilee, there you will see Him; behold, I have told you.”
verses.Matthew 28:8: And they left the tomb quickly with fear and great joy and ran to report it to His disciples.
verses.Matthew 28:9: And behold, Jesus met them and greeted them. And they came up and took hold of His feet and worshiped Him.
verses.Matthew 28:11: Now while they were on their way, some of the guard came into the city and reported to the chief priests all that had happened.
verses.Matthew 28:12: And when they had assembled with the elders and consulted together, they gave a large sum of money to the soldiers,
verses.Matthew 28:13: and said, “You are to say, ‘His disciples came by night and stole Him away while we were asleep.’
verses.Matthew 28:14: And if this should come to the governor’s ears, we will win him over and keep you out of trouble.”
verses.Matthew 28:15: And they took the money and did as they had been instructed; and this story was widely spread among the Jews, and is to this day.
verses.Matthew 28:16: But the eleven disciples proceeded to Galilee, to the mountain which Jesus had designated.
verses.Matthew 28:17: When they saw Him, they worshiped Him; but some were doubtful.
verses.Mark 1:1: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
verses.Mark 1:2: As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: “Behold, I send My messenger ahead of You, Who will prepare Your way;
verses.Mark 1:3: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make ready the way of the Lord, Make His paths straight.’ ”
verses.Mark 1:4: John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
verses.Mark 1:5: And all the country of Judea was going out to him, and all the people of Jerusalem; and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.
verses.Mark 1:6: John was clothed with camel’s hair and wore a leather belt around his waist, and his diet was locusts and wild honey.
verses.Mark 1:7: And he was preaching, and saying, “After me One is coming who is mightier than I, and I am not fit to stoop down and untie the thong of His sandals.
verses.Mark 1:8: I baptized you with water; but He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
verses.Mark 1:9: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
verses.Mark 1:10: Immediately coming up out of the water, He saw the heavens opening, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon Him;
verses.Mark 1:11: and a voice came out of the heavens: “You are My beloved Son, in You I am well-pleased.”
verses.Mark 1:12: Immediately the Spirit impelled Him to go out into the wilderness.
verses.Mark 1:13: And He was in the wilderness forty days being tempted by Satan; and He was with the wild beasts, and the angels were ministering to Him.
verses.Mark 1:14: Now after John had been taken into custody, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of God,
verses.Mark 1:16: As He was going along by the Sea of Galilee, He saw Simon and Andrew, the brother of Simon, casting a net in the sea; for they were fishermen.
verses.Mark 1:18: Immediately they left their nets and followed Him.
verses.Mark 1:19: Going on a little farther, He saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who were also in the boat mending the nets.
verses.Mark 1:20: Immediately He called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants, and went away to follow Him.
verses.Mark 1:21: They went into Capernaum; and immediately on the Sabbath He entered the synagogue and began to teach.
verses.Mark 1:22: They were amazed at His teaching; for He was teaching them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
verses.Mark 1:23: Just then there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
verses.Mark 1:24: saying, “What business do we have with each other, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God!”
verses.Mark 1:26: Throwing him into convulsions, the unclean spirit cried out with a loud voice and came out of him.
verses.Mark 1:27: They were all amazed, so that they debated among themselves, saying, “What is this? A new teaching with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey Him.”
verses.Mark 1:28: Immediately the news about Him spread everywhere into all the surrounding district of Galilee.
verses.Mark 1:29: And immediately after they came out of the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
verses.Mark 1:30: Now Simon’s mother-in-law was lying sick with a fever; and immediately they spoke to Jesus about her.
verses.Mark 1:31: And He came to her and raised her up, taking her by the hand, and the fever left her, and she waited on them.
verses.Mark 1:32: When evening came, after the sun had set, they began bringing to Him all who were ill and those who were demon-possessed.
verses.Mark 1:33: And the whole city had gathered at the door.
verses.Mark 1:34: And He healed many who were ill with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and He was not permitting the demons to speak, because they knew who He was.
verses.Mark 1:35: In the early morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house, and went away to a secluded place, and was praying there.
verses.Mark 1:36: Simon and his companions searched for Him;
verses.Mark 1:37: they found Him, and said to Him, “Everyone is looking for You.”
verses.Mark 1:39: And He went into their synagogues throughout all Galilee, preaching and casting out the demons.
verses.Mark 1:40: And a leper came to Jesus, beseeching Him and falling on his knees before Him, and saying, “If You are willing, You can make me clean.”
verses.Mark 1:42: Immediately the leprosy left him and he was cleansed.
verses.Mark 1:43: And He sternly warned him and immediately sent him away,
verses.Mark 1:45: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely and to spread the news around, to such an extent that Jesus could no longer publicly enter a city, but stayed out in unpopulated areas; and they were coming to Him from everywhere.
verses.Mark 2:1: When He had come back to Capernaum several days afterward, it was heard that He was at home.
verses.Mark 2:2: And many were gathered together, so that there was no longer room, not even near the door; and He was speaking the word to them.
verses.Mark 2:3: And they came, bringing to Him a paralytic, carried by four men.
verses.Mark 2:4: Being unable to get to Him because of the crowd, they removed the roof above Him; and when they had dug an opening, they let down the pallet on which the paralytic was lying.
verses.Mark 2:6: But some of the scribes were sitting there and reasoning in their hearts,
verses.Mark 2:7: “Why does this man speak that way? He is blaspheming; who can forgive sins but God alone?”
verses.Mark 2:12: And he got up and immediately picked up the pallet and went out in the sight of everyone, so that they were all amazed and were glorifying God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this.”
verses.Mark 2:13: And He went out again by the seashore; and all the people were coming to Him, and He was teaching them.
verses.Mark 2:15: And it happened that He was reclining at the table in his house, and many tax collectors and sinners were dining with Jesus and His disciples; for there were many of them, and they were following Him.
verses.Mark 2:16: When the scribes of the Pharisees saw that He was eating with the sinners and tax collectors, they said to His disciples, “Why is He eating and drinking with tax collectors and sinners?”
verses.Mark 2:18: John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting; and they came and said to Him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but Your disciples do not fast?”
verses.Mark 2:23: And it happened that He was passing through the grainfields on the Sabbath, and His disciples began to make their way along while picking the heads of grain.
verses.Mark 2:24: The Pharisees were saying to Him, “Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?”
verses.Mark 3:1: He entered again into a synagogue; and a man was there whose hand was withered.
verses.Mark 3:2: They were watching Him to see if He would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse Him.
verses.Mark 3:6: The Pharisees went out and immediately began conspiring with the Herodians against Him, as to how they might destroy Him.
verses.Mark 3:7: Jesus withdrew to the sea with His disciples; and a great multitude from Galilee followed; and also from Judea,
verses.Mark 3:8: and from Jerusalem, and from Idumea, and beyond the Jordan, and the vicinity of Tyre and Sidon, a great number of people heard of all that He was doing and came to Him.
verses.Mark 3:9: And He told His disciples that a boat should stand ready for Him because of the crowd, so that they would not crowd Him;
verses.Mark 3:10: for He had healed many, with the result that all those who had afflictions pressed around Him in order to touch Him.
verses.Mark 3:11: Whenever the unclean spirits saw Him, they would fall down before Him and shout, “You are the Son of God!”
verses.Mark 3:12: And He earnestly warned them not to tell who He was.
verses.Mark 3:13: And He went up on the mountain and summoned those whom He Himself wanted, and they came to Him.
verses.Mark 3:14: And He appointed twelve, so that they would be with Him and that He could send them out to preach,
verses.Mark 3:15: and to have authority to cast out the demons.
verses.Mark 3:16: And He appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom He gave the name Peter),
verses.Mark 3:17: and James, the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James (to them He gave the name Boanerges, which means, “Sons of Thunder”);
verses.Mark 3:18: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Zealot;
verses.Mark 3:19: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Him.
verses.Mark 3:20: And He came home, and the crowd gathered again, to such an extent that they could not even eat a meal.
verses.Mark 3:21: When His own people heard of this, they went out to take custody of Him; for they were saying, “He has lost His senses.”
verses.Mark 3:22: The scribes who came down from Jerusalem were saying, “He is possessed by Beelzebul,” and “He casts out the demons by the ruler of the demons.”
verses.Mark 3:30: because they were saying, “He has an unclean spirit.”
verses.Mark 3:31: Then His mother and His brothers arrived, and standing outside they sent word to Him and called Him.
verses.Mark 3:32: A crowd was sitting around Him, and they said to Him, “Behold, Your mother and Your brothers are outside looking for You.”
verses.Mark 4:1: He began to teach again by the sea. And such a very large crowd gathered to Him that He got into a boat in the sea and sat down; and the whole crowd was by the sea on the land.
verses.Mark 4:2: And He was teaching them many things in parables, and was saying to them in His teaching,
verses.Mark 4:10: As soon as He was alone, His followers, along with the twelve, began asking Him about the parables.
verses.Mark 4:33: With many such parables He was speaking the word to them, so far as they were able to hear it;
verses.Mark 4:34: and He did not speak to them without a parable; but He was explaining everything privately to His own disciples.
verses.Mark 4:36: Leaving the crowd, they took Him along with them in the boat, just as He was; and other boats were with Him.
verses.Mark 4:37: And there arose a fierce gale of wind, and the waves were breaking over the boat so much that the boat was already filling up.
verses.Mark 4:38: Jesus Himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion; and they woke Him and said to Him, “Teacher, do You not care that we are perishing?”
verses.Mark 4:41: They became very much afraid and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey Him?”
verses.Mark 5:1: They came to the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gerasenes.
verses.Mark 5:2: When He got out of the boat, immediately a man from the tombs with an unclean spirit met Him,
verses.Mark 5:3: and he had his dwelling among the tombs. And no one was able to bind him anymore, even with a chain;
verses.Mark 5:4: because he had often been bound with shackles and chains, and the chains had been torn apart by him and the shackles broken in pieces, and no one was strong enough to subdue him.
verses.Mark 5:5: Constantly, night and day, he was screaming among the tombs and in the mountains, and gashing himself with stones.
verses.Mark 5:6: Seeing Jesus from a distance, he ran up and bowed down before Him;
verses.Mark 5:7: and shouting with a loud voice, he said, “What business do we have with each other, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I implore You by God, do not torment me!”
verses.Mark 5:10: And he began to implore Him earnestly not to send them out of the country.
verses.Mark 5:11: Now there was a large herd of swine feeding nearby on the mountain.
verses.Mark 5:12: The demons implored Him, saying, “Send us into the swine so that we may enter them.”
verses.Mark 5:13: Jesus gave them permission. And coming out, the unclean spirits entered the swine; and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea, about two thousand of them; and they were drowned in the sea.
verses.Mark 5:14: Their herdsmen ran away and reported it in the city and in the country. And the people came to see what it was that had happened.
verses.Mark 5:15: They came to Jesus and observed the man who had been demon-possessed sitting down, clothed and in his right mind, the very man who had had the “legion”; and they became frightened.
verses.Mark 5:16: Those who had seen it described to them how it had happened to the demon-possessed man, and all about the swine.
verses.Mark 5:17: And they began to implore Him to leave their region.
verses.Mark 5:18: As He was getting into the boat, the man who had been demon-possessed was imploring Him that he might accompany Him.
verses.Mark 5:20: And he went away and began to proclaim in Decapolis what great things Jesus had done for him; and everyone was amazed.
verses.Mark 5:21: When Jesus had crossed over again in the boat to the other side, a large crowd gathered around Him; and so He stayed by the seashore.
verses.Mark 5:22: One of the synagogue officials named Jairus came up, and on seeing Him, fell at His feet
verses.Mark 5:23: and implored Him earnestly, saying, “My little daughter is at the point of death; please come and lay Your hands on her, so that she will get well and live.”
verses.Mark 5:24: And He went off with him; and a large crowd was following Him and pressing in on Him.
verses.Mark 5:25: A woman who had had a hemorrhage for twelve years,
verses.Mark 5:26: and had endured much at the hands of many physicians, and had spent all that she had and was not helped at all, but rather had grown worse—
verses.Mark 5:27: after hearing about Jesus, she came up in the crowd behind Him and touched His cloak.
verses.Mark 5:28: For she thought, “If I just touch His garments, I will get well.”
verses.Mark 5:29: Immediately the flow of her blood was dried up; and she felt in her body that she was healed of her affliction.
verses.Mark 5:31: And His disciples said to Him, “You see the crowd pressing in on You, and You say, ‘Who touched Me?’ ”
verses.Mark 5:32: And He looked around to see the woman who had done this.
verses.Mark 5:33: But the woman fearing and trembling, aware of what had happened to her, came and fell down before Him and told Him the whole truth.
verses.Mark 5:35: While He was still speaking, they came from the house of the synagogue official, saying, “Your daughter has died; why trouble the Teacher anymore?”
verses.Mark 5:37: And He allowed no one to accompany Him, except Peter and James and John the brother of James.
verses.Mark 5:38: They came to the house of the synagogue official; and He saw a commotion, and people loudly weeping and wailing.
verses.Mark 5:40: They began laughing at Him. But putting them all out, He took along the child’s father and mother and His own companions, and entered the room where the child was.
verses.Mark 5:42: Immediately the girl got up and began to walk, for she was twelve years old. And immediately they were completely astounded.
verses.Mark 5:43: And He gave them strict orders that no one should know about this, and He said that something should be given her to eat.
verses.Mark 6:1: Jesus went out from there and came into His hometown; and His disciples followed Him.
verses.Mark 6:2: When the Sabbath came, He began to teach in the synagogue; and the many listeners were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things, and what is this wisdom given to Him, and such miracles as these performed by His hands?
verses.Mark 6:3: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon? Are not His sisters here with us?” And they took offense at Him.
verses.Mark 6:5: And He could do no miracle there except that He laid His hands on a few sick people and healed them.
verses.Mark 6:6: And He wondered at their unbelief. And He was going around the villages teaching.
verses.Mark 6:7: And He summoned the twelve and began to send them out in pairs, and gave them authority over the unclean spirits;
verses.Mark 6:8: and He instructed them that they should take nothing for their journey, except a mere staff—no bread, no bag, no money in their belt—
verses.Mark 6:12: They went out and preached that men should repent.
verses.Mark 6:13: And they were casting out many demons and were anointing with oil many sick people and healing them.
verses.Mark 6:14: And King Herod heard of it, for His name had become well known; and people were saying, “John the Baptist has risen from the dead, and that is why these miraculous powers are at work in Him.”
verses.Mark 6:15: But others were saying, “He is Elijah.” And others were saying, “He is a prophet, like one of the prophets of old.”
verses.Mark 6:16: But when Herod heard of it, he kept saying, “John, whom I beheaded, has risen!”
verses.Mark 6:17: For Herod himself had sent and had John arrested and bound in prison on account of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip, because he had married her.
verses.Mark 6:18: For John had been saying to Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”
verses.Mark 6:19: Herodias had a grudge against him and wanted to put him to death and could not do so;
verses.Mark 6:20: for Herod was afraid of John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and he kept him safe. And when he heard him, he was very perplexed; but he used to enjoy listening to him.
verses.Mark 6:21: A strategic day came when Herod on his birthday gave a banquet for his lords and military commanders and the leading men of Galilee;
verses.Mark 6:22: and when the daughter of Herodias herself came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his dinner guests; and the king said to the girl, “Ask me for whatever you want and I will give it to you.”
verses.Mark 6:23: And he swore to her, “Whatever you ask of me, I will give it to you; up to half of my kingdom.”
verses.Mark 6:24: And she went out and said to her mother, “What shall I ask for?” And she said, “The head of John the Baptist.”
verses.Mark 6:25: Immediately she came in a hurry to the king and asked, saying, “I want you to give me at once the head of John the Baptist on a platter.”
verses.Mark 6:26: And although the king was very sorry, yet because of his oaths and because of his dinner guests, he was unwilling to refuse her.
verses.Mark 6:27: Immediately the king sent an executioner and commanded him to bring back his head. And he went and had him beheaded in the prison,
verses.Mark 6:28: and brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the girl; and the girl gave it to her mother.
verses.Mark 6:29: When his disciples heard about this, they came and took away his body and laid it in a tomb.
verses.Mark 6:30: The apostles gathered together with Jesus; and they reported to Him all that they had done and taught.
verses.Mark 6:32: They went away in the boat to a secluded place by themselves.
verses.Mark 6:33: The people saw them going, and many recognized them and ran there together on foot from all the cities, and got there ahead of them.
verses.Mark 6:34: When Jesus went ashore, He saw a large crowd, and He felt compassion for them because they were like sheep without a shepherd; and He began to teach them many things.
verses.Mark 6:35: When it was already quite late, His disciples came to Him and said, “This place is desolate and it is already quite late;
verses.Mark 6:36: send them away so that they may go into the surrounding countryside and villages and buy themselves something to eat.”
verses.Mark 6:39: And He commanded them all to sit down by groups on the green grass.
verses.Mark 6:40: They sat down in groups of hundreds and of fifties.
verses.Mark 6:41: And He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up toward heaven, He blessed the food and broke the loaves and He kept giving them to the disciples to set before them; and He divided up the two fish among them all.
verses.Mark 6:42: They all ate and were satisfied,
verses.Mark 6:43: and they picked up twelve full baskets of the broken pieces, and also of the fish.
verses.Mark 6:44: There were five thousand men who ate the loaves.
verses.Mark 6:45: Immediately Jesus made His disciples get into the boat and go ahead of Him to the other side to Bethsaida, while He Himself was sending the crowd away.
verses.Mark 6:46: After bidding them farewell, He left for the mountain to pray.
verses.Mark 6:47: When it was evening, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and He was alone on the land.
verses.Mark 6:48: Seeing them straining at the oars, for the wind was against them, at about the fourth watch of the night He came to them, walking on the sea; and He intended to pass by them.
verses.Mark 6:49: But when they saw Him walking on the sea, they supposed that it was a ghost, and cried out;
verses.Mark 6:51: Then He got into the boat with them, and the wind stopped; and they were utterly astonished,
verses.Mark 6:52: for they had not gained any insight from the incident of the loaves, but their heart was hardened.
verses.Mark 6:53: When they had crossed over they came to land at Gennesaret, and moored to the shore.
verses.Mark 6:54: When they got out of the boat, immediately the people recognized Him,
verses.Mark 6:55: and ran about that whole country and began to carry here and there on their pallets those who were sick, to the place they heard He was.
verses.Mark 6:56: Wherever He entered villages, or cities, or countryside, they were laying the sick in the market places, and imploring Him that they might just touch the fringe of His cloak; and as many as touched it were being cured.
verses.Mark 7:1: The Pharisees and some of the scribes gathered around Him when they had come from Jerusalem,
verses.Mark 7:2: and had seen that some of His disciples were eating their bread with impure hands, that is, unwashed.
verses.Mark 7:3: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they carefully wash their hands, thus observing the traditions of the elders;
verses.Mark 7:4: and when they come from the market place, they do not eat unless they cleanse themselves; and there are many other things which they have received in order to observe, such as the washing of cups and pitchers and copper pots.)
verses.Mark 7:5: The Pharisees and the scribes asked Him, “Why do Your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat their bread with impure hands?”
verses.Mark 7:17: When he had left the crowd and entered the house, His disciples questioned Him about the parable.
verses.Mark 7:24: Jesus got up and went away from there to the region of Tyre. And when He had entered a house, He wanted no one to know of it; yet He could not escape notice.
verses.Mark 7:25: But after hearing of Him, a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit immediately came and fell at His feet.
verses.Mark 7:26: Now the woman was a Gentile, of the Syrophoenician race. And she kept asking Him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
verses.Mark 7:28: But she answered and said to Him, “Yes, Lord, but even the dogs under the table feed on the children’s crumbs.”
verses.Mark 7:30: And going back to her home, she found the child lying on the bed, the demon having left.
verses.Mark 7:31: Again He went out from the region of Tyre, and came through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, within the region of Decapolis.
verses.Mark 7:32: They brought to Him one who was deaf and spoke with difficulty, and they implored Him to lay His hand on him.
verses.Mark 7:33: Jesus took him aside from the crowd, by himself, and put His fingers into his ears, and after spitting, He touched his tongue with the saliva;
verses.Mark 7:35: And his ears were opened, and the impediment of his tongue was removed, and he began speaking plainly.
verses.Mark 7:36: And He gave them orders not to tell anyone; but the more He ordered them, the more widely they continued to proclaim it.
verses.Mark 7:37: They were utterly astonished, saying, “He has done all things well; He makes even the deaf to hear and the mute to speak.”
verses.Mark 8:1: In those days, when there was again a large crowd and they had nothing to eat, Jesus called His disciples and said to them,
verses.Mark 8:4: And His disciples answered Him, “Where will anyone be able to find enough bread here in this desolate place to satisfy these people?”
verses.Mark 8:6: And He directed the people to sit down on the ground; and taking the seven loaves, He gave thanks and broke them, and started giving them to His disciples to serve to them, and they served them to the people.
verses.Mark 8:7: They also had a few small fish; and after He had blessed them, He ordered these to be served as well.
verses.Mark 8:8: And they ate and were satisfied; and they picked up seven large baskets full of what was left over of the broken pieces.
verses.Mark 8:9: About four thousand were there; and He sent them away.
verses.Mark 8:10: And immediately He entered the boat with His disciples and came to the district of Dalmanutha.
verses.Mark 8:11: The Pharisees came out and began to argue with Him, seeking from Him a sign from heaven, to test Him.
verses.Mark 8:13: Leaving them, He again embarked and went away to the other side.
verses.Mark 8:14: And they had forgotten to take bread, and did not have more than one loaf in the boat with them.
verses.Mark 8:16: They began to discuss with one another the fact that they had no bread.
verses.Mark 8:22: And they came to Bethsaida. And they brought a blind man to Jesus and implored Him to touch him.
verses.Mark 8:24: And he looked up and said, “I see men, for I see them like trees, walking around.”
verses.Mark 8:25: Then again He laid His hands on his eyes; and he looked intently and was restored, and began to see everything clearly.
verses.Mark 8:28: They told Him, saying, “John the Baptist; and others say Elijah; but others, one of the prophets.”
verses.Mark 8:30: And He warned them to tell no one about Him.
verses.Mark 8:31: And He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders and the chief priests and the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
verses.Mark 8:32: And He was stating the matter plainly. And Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him.
verses.Mark 9:2: Six days later, Jesus took with Him Peter and James and John, and brought them up on a high mountain by themselves. And He was transfigured before them;
verses.Mark 9:3: and His garments became radiant and exceedingly white, as no launderer on earth can whiten them.
verses.Mark 9:4: Elijah appeared to them along with Moses; and they were talking with Jesus.
verses.Mark 9:5: Peter said to Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good for us to be here; let us make three tabernacles, one for You, and one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
verses.Mark 9:6: For he did not know what to answer; for they became terrified.
verses.Mark 9:7: Then a cloud formed, overshadowing them, and a voice came out of the cloud, “This is My beloved Son, listen to Him!”
verses.Mark 9:8: All at once they looked around and saw no one with them anymore, except Jesus alone.
verses.Mark 9:9: As they were coming down from the mountain, He gave them orders not to relate to anyone what they had seen, until the Son of Man rose from the dead.
verses.Mark 9:10: They seized upon that statement, discussing with one another what rising from the dead meant.
verses.Mark 9:11: They asked Him, saying, “Why is it that the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
verses.Mark 9:14: When they came back to the disciples, they saw a large crowd around them, and some scribes arguing with them.
verses.Mark 9:15: Immediately, when the entire crowd saw Him, they were amazed and began running up to greet Him.
verses.Mark 9:17: And one of the crowd answered Him, “Teacher, I brought You my son, possessed with a spirit which makes him mute;
verses.Mark 9:18: and whenever it seizes him, it slams him to the ground and he foams at the mouth, and grinds his teeth and stiffens out. I told Your disciples to cast it out, and they could not do it.”
verses.Mark 9:20: They brought the boy to Him. When he saw Him, immediately the spirit threw him into a convulsion, and falling to the ground, he began rolling around and foaming at the mouth.
verses.Mark 9:22: It has often thrown him both into the fire and into the water to destroy him. But if You can do anything, take pity on us and help us!”
verses.Mark 9:24: Immediately the boy’s father cried out and said, “I do believe; help my unbelief.”
verses.Mark 9:26: After crying out and throwing him into terrible convulsions, it came out; and the boy became so much like a corpse that most of them said, “He is dead!”
verses.Mark 9:27: But Jesus took him by the hand and raised him; and he got up.
verses.Mark 9:28: When He came into the house, His disciples began questioning Him privately, “Why could we not drive it out?”
verses.Mark 9:30: From there they went out and began to go through Galilee, and He did not want anyone to know about it.
verses.Mark 9:32: But they did not understand this statement, and they were afraid to ask Him.
verses.Mark 9:34: But they kept silent, for on the way they had discussed with one another which of them was the greatest.
verses.Mark 9:36: Taking a child, He set him before them, and taking him in His arms, He said to them,
verses.Mark 9:38: John said to Him, “Teacher, we saw someone casting out demons in Your name, and we tried to prevent him because he was not following us.”
verses.Mark 10:1: Getting up, He went from there to the region of Judea and beyond the Jordan; crowds gathered around Him again, and, according to His custom, He once more began to teach them.
verses.Mark 10:2: Some Pharisees came up to Jesus, testing Him, and began to question Him whether it was lawful for a man to divorce a wife.
verses.Mark 10:4: They said, “Moses permitted a man to write a certificate of divorce and send her away.”
verses.Mark 10:10: In the house the disciples began questioning Him about this again.
verses.Mark 10:13: And they were bringing children to Him so that He might touch them; but the disciples rebuked them.
verses.Mark 10:16: And He took them in His arms and began blessing them, laying His hands on them.
verses.Mark 10:17: As He was setting out on a journey, a man ran up to Him and knelt before Him, and asked Him, “Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
verses.Mark 10:20: And he said to Him, “Teacher, I have kept all these things from my youth up.”
verses.Mark 10:22: But at these words he was saddened, and he went away grieving, for he was one who owned much property.
verses.Mark 10:26: They were even more astonished and said to Him, “Then who can be saved?”
verses.Mark 10:28: Peter began to say to Him, “Behold, we have left everything and followed You.”
verses.Mark 10:32: They were on the road going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was walking on ahead of them; and they were amazed, and those who followed were fearful. And again He took the twelve aside and began to tell them what was going to happen to Him,
verses.Mark 10:35: James and John, the two sons of Zebedee, came up to Jesus, saying, “Teacher, we want You to do for us whatever we ask of You.”
verses.Mark 10:37: They said to Him, “Grant that we may sit, one on Your right and one on Your left, in Your glory.”
verses.Mark 10:41: Hearing this, the ten began to feel indignant with James and John.
verses.Mark 10:46: Then they came to Jericho. And as He was leaving Jericho with His disciples and a large crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, the son of Timaeus, was sitting by the road.
verses.Mark 10:47: When he heard that it was Jesus the Nazarene, he began to cry out and say, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
verses.Mark 10:48: Many were sternly telling him to be quiet, but he kept crying out all the more, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
verses.Mark 10:50: Throwing aside his cloak, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
verses.Mark 11:1: As they approached Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany, near the Mount of Olives, He sent two of His disciples,
verses.Mark 11:4: They went away and found a colt tied at the door, outside in the street; and they untied it.
verses.Mark 11:5: Some of the bystanders were saying to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
verses.Mark 11:6: They spoke to them just as Jesus had told them, and they gave them permission.
verses.Mark 11:7: They brought the colt to Jesus and put their coats on it; and He sat on it.
verses.Mark 11:8: And many spread their coats in the road, and others spread leafy branches which they had cut from the fields.
verses.Mark 11:9: Those who went in front and those who followed were shouting: “Hosanna! Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord;
verses.Mark 11:10: Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David; Hosanna in the highest!”
verses.Mark 11:11: Jesus entered Jerusalem and came into the temple; and after looking around at everything, He left for Bethany with the twelve, since it was already late.
verses.Mark 11:12: On the next day, when they had left Bethany, He became hungry.
verses.Mark 11:13: Seeing at a distance a fig tree in leaf, He went to see if perhaps He would find anything on it; and when He came to it, He found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
verses.Mark 11:15: Then they came to Jerusalem. And He entered the temple and began to drive out those who were buying and selling in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling doves;
verses.Mark 11:16: and He would not permit anyone to carry merchandise through the temple.
verses.Mark 11:18: The chief priests and the scribes heard this, and began seeking how to destroy Him; for they were afraid of Him, for the whole crowd was astonished at His teaching.
verses.Mark 11:19: When evening came, they would go out of the city.
verses.Mark 11:20: As they were passing by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered from the roots up.
verses.Mark 11:21: Being reminded, Peter said to Him, “Rabbi, look, the fig tree which You cursed has withered.”
verses.Mark 11:27: They came again to Jerusalem. And as He was walking in the temple, the chief priests and the scribes and the elders came to Him,
verses.Mark 11:28: and began saying to Him, “By what authority are You doing these things, or who gave You this authority to do these things?”
verses.Mark 11:31: They began reasoning among themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ He will say, ‘Then why did you not believe him?’
verses.Mark 11:32: But shall we say, ‘From men’?”—they were afraid of the people, for everyone considered John to have been a real prophet.
verses.Mark 12:12: And they were seeking to seize Him, and yet they feared the people, for they understood that He spoke the parable against them. And so they left Him and went away.
verses.Mark 12:13: Then they sent some of the Pharisees and Herodians to Him in order to trap Him in a statement.
verses.Mark 12:14: They came and said to Him, “Teacher, we know that You are truthful and defer to no one; for You are not partial to any, but teach the way of God in truth. Is it lawful to pay a poll-tax to Caesar, or not?
verses.Mark 12:18: Some Sadducees (who say that there is no resurrection) came to Jesus, and began questioning Him, saying,
verses.Mark 12:19: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies and leaves behind a wife and leaves no child, his brother should marry the wife and raise up children to his brother.
verses.Mark 12:20: There were seven brothers; and the first took a wife, and died leaving no children.
verses.Mark 12:21: The second one married her, and died leaving behind no children; and the third likewise;
verses.Mark 12:22: and so all seven left no children. Last of all the woman died also.
verses.Mark 12:23: In the resurrection, when they rise again, which one’s wife will she be? For all seven had married her.”
verses.Mark 12:28: One of the scribes came and heard them arguing, and recognizing that He had answered them well, asked Him, “What commandment is the foremost of all?”
verses.Mark 12:32: The scribe said to Him, “Right, Teacher; You have truly stated that He is One, and there is no one else besides Him;
verses.Mark 12:33: and to love Him with all the heart and with all the understanding and with all the strength, and to love one’s neighbor as himself, is much more than all burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
verses.Mark 12:41: And He sat down opposite the treasury, and began observing how the people were putting money into the treasury; and many rich people were putting in large sums.
verses.Mark 12:42: A poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which amount to a cent.
verses.Mark 13:1: As He was going out of the temple, one of His disciples said to Him, “Teacher, behold what wonderful stones and what wonderful buildings!”
verses.Mark 13:3: As He was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew were questioning Him privately,
verses.Mark 13:4: “Tell us, when will these things be, and what will be the sign when all these things are going to be fulfilled?”
verses.Mark 14:1: Now the Passover and Unleavened Bread were two days away; and the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how to seize Him by stealth and kill Him;
verses.Mark 14:2: for they were saying, “Not during the festival, otherwise there might be a riot of the people.”
verses.Mark 14:3: While He was in Bethany at the home of Simon the leper, and reclining at the table, there came a woman with an alabaster vial of very costly perfume of pure nard; and she broke the vial and poured it over His head.
verses.Mark 14:4: But some were indignantly remarking to one another, “Why has this perfume been wasted?
verses.Mark 14:5: For this perfume might have been sold for over three hundred denarii, and the money given to the poor.” And they were scolding her.
verses.Mark 14:10: Then Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went off to the chief priests in order to betray Him to them.
verses.Mark 14:11: They were glad when they heard this, and promised to give him money. And he began seeking how to betray Him at an opportune time.
verses.Mark 14:12: On the first day of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was being sacrificed, His disciples said to Him, “Where do You want us to go and prepare for You to eat the Passover?”
verses.Mark 14:16: The disciples went out and came to the city, and found it just as He had told them; and they prepared the Passover.
verses.Mark 14:17: When it was evening He came with the twelve.
verses.Mark 14:19: They began to be grieved and to say to Him one by one, “Surely not I?”
verses.Mark 14:23: And when He had taken a cup and given thanks, He gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
verses.Mark 14:26: After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
verses.Mark 14:29: But Peter said to Him, “Even though all may fall away, yet I will not.”
verses.Mark 14:31: But Peter kept saying insistently, “Even if I have to die with You, I will not deny You!” And they all were saying the same thing also.
verses.Mark 14:33: And He took with Him Peter and James and John, and began to be very distressed and troubled.
verses.Mark 14:35: And He went a little beyond them, and fell to the ground and began to pray that if it were possible, the hour might pass Him by.
verses.Mark 14:39: Again He went away and prayed, saying the same words.
verses.Mark 14:40: And again He came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy; and they did not know what to answer Him.
verses.Mark 14:43: Immediately while He was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, came up accompanied by a crowd with swords and clubs, who were from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
verses.Mark 14:44: Now he who was betraying Him had given them a signal, saying, “Whomever I kiss, He is the one; seize Him and lead Him away under guard.”
verses.Mark 14:45: After coming, Judas immediately went to Him, saying, “Rabbi!” and kissed Him.
verses.Mark 14:46: They laid hands on Him and seized Him.
verses.Mark 14:47: But one of those who stood by drew his sword, and struck the slave of the high priest and cut off his ear.
verses.Mark 14:50: And they all left Him and fled.
verses.Mark 14:51: A young man was following Him, wearing nothing but a linen sheet over his naked body; and they seized him.
verses.Mark 14:52: But he pulled free of the linen sheet and escaped naked.
verses.Mark 14:53: They led Jesus away to the high priest; and all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes gathered together.
verses.Mark 14:54: Peter had followed Him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest; and he was sitting with the officers and warming himself at the fire.
verses.Mark 14:55: Now the chief priests and the whole Council kept trying to obtain testimony against Jesus to put Him to death, and they were not finding any.
verses.Mark 14:56: For many were giving false testimony against Him, but their testimony was not consistent.
verses.Mark 14:57: Some stood up and began to give false testimony against Him, saying,
verses.Mark 14:58: “We heard Him say, ‘I will destroy this temple made with hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands.’ ”
verses.Mark 14:59: Not even in this respect was their testimony consistent.
verses.Mark 14:60: The high priest stood up and came forward and questioned Jesus, saying, “Do You not answer? What is it that these men are testifying against You?”
verses.Mark 14:61: But He kept silent and did not answer. Again the high priest was questioning Him, and saying to Him, “Are You the Christ, the Son of the Blessed One?”
verses.Mark 14:63: Tearing his clothes, the high priest said, “What further need do we have of witnesses?
verses.Mark 14:64: You have heard the blasphemy; how does it seem to you?” And they all condemned Him to be deserving of death.
verses.Mark 14:65: Some began to spit at Him, and to blindfold Him, and to beat Him with their fists, and to say to Him, “Prophesy!” And the officers received Him with slaps in the face.
verses.Mark 14:66: As Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant-girls of the high priest came,
verses.Mark 14:67: and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, “You also were with Jesus the Nazarene.”
verses.Mark 14:68: But he denied it, saying, “I neither know nor understand what you are talking about.” And he went out onto the porch.
verses.Mark 14:69: The servant-girl saw him, and began once more to say to the bystanders, “This is one of them!”
verses.Mark 14:70: But again he denied it. And after a little while the bystanders were again saying to Peter, “Surely you are one of them, for you are a Galilean too.”
verses.Mark 14:71: But he began to curse and swear, “I do not know this man you are talking about!”
verses.Mark 15:1: Early in the morning the chief priests with the elders and scribes and the whole Council, immediately held a consultation; and binding Jesus, they led Him away and delivered Him to Pilate.
verses.Mark 15:3: The chief priests began to accuse Him harshly.
verses.Mark 15:4: Then Pilate questioned Him again, saying, “Do You not answer? See how many charges they bring against You!”
verses.Mark 15:5: But Jesus made no further answer; so Pilate was amazed.
verses.Mark 15:6: Now at the feast he used to release for them any one prisoner whom they requested.
verses.Mark 15:7: The man named Barabbas had been imprisoned with the insurrectionists who had committed murder in the insurrection.
verses.Mark 15:8: The crowd went up and began asking him to do as he had been accustomed to do for them.
verses.Mark 15:9: Pilate answered them, saying, “Do you want me to release for you the King of the Jews?”
verses.Mark 15:10: For he was aware that the chief priests had handed Him over because of envy.
verses.Mark 15:11: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to ask him to release Barabbas for them instead.
verses.Mark 15:12: Answering again, Pilate said to them, “Then what shall I do with Him whom you call the King of the Jews?”
verses.Mark 15:14: But Pilate said to them, “Why, what evil has He done?” But they shouted all the more, “Crucify Him!”
verses.Mark 15:15: Wishing to satisfy the crowd, Pilate released Barabbas for them, and after having Jesus scourged, he handed Him over to be crucified.
verses.Mark 15:16: The soldiers took Him away into the palace (that is, the Praetorium), and they called together the whole Roman cohort.
verses.Mark 15:17: They dressed Him up in purple, and after twisting a crown of thorns, they put it on Him;
verses.Mark 15:18: and they began to acclaim Him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
verses.Mark 15:19: They kept beating His head with a reed, and spitting on Him, and kneeling and bowing before Him.
verses.Mark 15:20: After they had mocked Him, they took the purple robe off Him and put His own garments on Him. And they led Him out to crucify Him.
verses.Mark 15:21: They pressed into service a passer-by coming from the country, Simon of Cyrene (the father of Alexander and Rufus), to bear His cross.
verses.Mark 15:22: Then they brought Him to the place Golgotha, which is translated, Place of a Skull.
verses.Mark 15:23: They tried to give Him wine mixed with myrrh; but He did not take it.
verses.Mark 15:24: And they crucified Him, and divided up His garments among themselves, casting lots for them to decide what each man should take.
verses.Mark 15:25: It was the third hour when they crucified Him.
verses.Mark 15:26: The inscription of the charge against Him read, “THE KING OF THE JEWS.”
verses.Mark 15:27: They crucified two robbers with Him, one on His right and one on His left.
verses.Mark 15:28: [And the Scripture was fulfilled which says, “And He was numbered with transgressors.”]
verses.Mark 15:29: Those passing by were hurling abuse at Him, wagging their heads, and saying, “Ha! You who are going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days,
verses.Mark 15:30: save Yourself, and come down from the cross!”
verses.Mark 15:31: In the same way the chief priests also, along with the scribes, were mocking Him among themselves and saying, “He saved others; He cannot save Himself.
verses.Mark 15:32: Let this Christ, the King of Israel, now come down from the cross, so that we may see and believe!” Those who were crucified with Him were also insulting Him.
verses.Mark 15:33: When the sixth hour came, darkness fell over the whole land until the ninth hour.
verses.Mark 15:35: When some of the bystanders heard it, they began saying, “Behold, He is calling for Elijah.”
verses.Mark 15:36: Someone ran and filled a sponge with sour wine, put it on a reed, and gave Him a drink, saying, “Let us see whether Elijah will come to take Him down.”
verses.Mark 15:37: And Jesus uttered a loud cry, and breathed His last.
verses.Mark 15:38: And the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.
verses.Mark 15:39: When the centurion, who was standing right in front of Him, saw the way He breathed His last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”
verses.Mark 15:40: There were also some women looking on from a distance, among whom were Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the Less and Joses, and Salome.
verses.Mark 15:41: When He was in Galilee, they used to follow Him and minister to Him; and there were many other women who came up with Him to Jerusalem.
verses.Mark 15:42: When evening had already come, because it was the preparation day, that is, the day before the Sabbath,
verses.Mark 15:43: Joseph of Arimathea came, a prominent member of the Council, who himself was waiting for the kingdom of God; and he gathered up courage and went in before Pilate, and asked for the body of Jesus.
verses.Mark 15:44: Pilate wondered if He was dead by this time, and summoning the centurion, he questioned him as to whether He was already dead.
verses.Mark 15:45: And ascertaining this from the centurion, he granted the body to Joseph.
verses.Mark 15:46: Joseph bought a linen cloth, took Him down, wrapped Him in the linen cloth and laid Him in a tomb which had been hewn out in the rock; and he rolled a stone against the entrance of the tomb.
verses.Mark 15:47: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses were looking on to see where He was laid.
verses.Mark 16:1: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, bought spices, so that they might come and anoint Him.
verses.Mark 16:2: Very early on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen.
verses.Mark 16:3: They were saying to one another, “Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance of the tomb?”
verses.Mark 16:4: Looking up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away, although it was extremely large.
verses.Mark 16:5: Entering the tomb, they saw a young man sitting at the right, wearing a white robe; and they were amazed.
verses.Mark 16:6: And he said to them, “Do not be amazed; you are looking for Jesus the Nazarene, who has been crucified. He has risen; He is not here; behold, here is the place where they laid Him.
verses.Mark 16:7: But go, tell His disciples and Peter, ‘He is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see Him, just as He told you.’ ”
verses.Mark 16:8: They went out and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had gripped them; and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.
verses.Mark 16:9: [Now after He had risen early on the first day of the week, He first appeared to Mary Magdalene, from whom He had cast out seven demons.
verses.Mark 16:10: She went and reported to those who had been with Him, while they were mourning and weeping.
verses.Mark 16:11: When they heard that He was alive and had been seen by her, they refused to believe it.
verses.Mark 16:12: After that, He appeared in a different form to two of them while they were walking along on their way to the country.
verses.Mark 16:13: They went away and reported it to the others, but they did not believe them either.
verses.Mark 16:14: Afterward He appeared to the eleven themselves as they were reclining at the table; and He reproached them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they had not believed those who had seen Him after He had risen.
verses.Mark 16:19: So then, when the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, He was received up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
verses.Mark 16:20: And they went out and preached everywhere, while the Lord worked with them, and confirmed the word by the signs that followed.] [And they promptly reported all these instructions to Peter and his companions. And after that, Jesus Himself sent out through them from east to west the sacred and imperishable proclamation of eternal salvation.]
verses.Luke 1:1: Inasmuch as many have undertaken to compile an account of the things accomplished among us,
verses.Luke 1:2: just as they were handed down to us by those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word,
verses.Luke 1:3: it seemed fitting for me as well, having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, to write it out for you in consecutive order, most excellent Theophilus;
verses.Luke 1:4: so that you may know the exact truth about the things you have been taught.
verses.Luke 1:5: In the days of Herod, king of Judea, there was a priest named Zacharias, of the division of Abijah; and he had a wife from the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
verses.Luke 1:6: They were both righteous in the sight of God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and requirements of the Lord.
verses.Luke 1:7: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they were both advanced in years.
verses.Luke 1:8: Now it happened that while he was performing his priestly service before God in the appointed order of his division,
verses.Luke 1:9: according to the custom of the priestly office, he was chosen by lot to enter the temple of the Lord and burn incense.
verses.Luke 1:10: And the whole multitude of the people were in prayer outside at the hour of the incense offering.
verses.Luke 1:11: And an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
verses.Luke 1:12: Zacharias was troubled when he saw the angel, and fear gripped him.
verses.Luke 1:13: But the angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zacharias, for your petition has been heard, and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will give him the name John.
verses.Luke 1:14: You will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth.
verses.Luke 1:15: For he will be great in the sight of the Lord; and he will drink no wine or liquor, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit while yet in his mother’s womb.
verses.Luke 1:16: And he will turn many of the sons of Israel back to the Lord their God.
verses.Luke 1:17: It is he who will go as a forerunner before Him in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the fathers back to the children, and the disobedient to the attitude of the righteous, so as to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”
verses.Luke 1:18: Zacharias said to the angel, “How will I know this for certain? For I am an old man and my wife is advanced in years.”
verses.Luke 1:19: The angel answered and said to him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.
verses.Luke 1:20: And behold, you shall be silent and unable to speak until the day when these things take place, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their proper time.”
verses.Luke 1:21: The people were waiting for Zacharias, and were wondering at his delay in the temple.
verses.Luke 1:22: But when he came out, he was unable to speak to them; and they realized that he had seen a vision in the temple; and he kept making signs to them, and remained mute.
verses.Luke 1:23: When the days of his priestly service were ended, he went back home.
verses.Luke 1:24: After these days Elizabeth his wife became pregnant, and she kept herself in seclusion for five months, saying,
verses.Luke 1:25: “This is the way the Lord has dealt with me in the days when He looked with favor upon me, to take away my disgrace among men.”
verses.Luke 1:26: Now in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city in Galilee called Nazareth,
verses.Luke 1:27: to a virgin engaged to a man whose name was Joseph, of the descendants of David; and the virgin’s name was Mary.
verses.Luke 1:28: And coming in, he said to her, “Greetings, favored one! The Lord is with you.”
verses.Luke 1:29: But she was very perplexed at this statement, and kept pondering what kind of salutation this was.
verses.Luke 1:30: The angel said to her, “Do not be afraid, Mary; for you have found favor with God.
verses.Luke 1:31: And behold, you will conceive in your womb and bear a son, and you shall name Him Jesus.
verses.Luke 1:32: He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High; and the Lord God will give Him the throne of His father David;
verses.Luke 1:33: and He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and His kingdom will have no end.”
verses.Luke 1:34: Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, since I am a virgin?”
verses.Luke 1:35: The angel answered and said to her, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you; and for that reason the holy Child shall be called the Son of God.
verses.Luke 1:36: And behold, even your relative Elizabeth has also conceived a son in her old age; and she who was called barren is now in her sixth month.
verses.Luke 1:37: For nothing will be impossible with God.”
verses.Luke 1:38: And Mary said, “Behold, the bondslave of the Lord; may it be done to me according to your word.” And the angel departed from her.
verses.Luke 1:39: Now at this time Mary arose and went in a hurry to the hill country, to a city of Judah,
verses.Luke 1:40: and entered the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.
verses.Luke 1:41: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb; and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
verses.Luke 1:42: And she cried out with a loud voice and said, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!
verses.Luke 1:43: And how has it happened to me, that the mother of my Lord would come to me?
verses.Luke 1:44: For behold, when the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby leaped in my womb for joy.
verses.Luke 1:45: And blessed is she who believed that there would be a fulfillment of what had been spoken to her by the Lord.”
verses.Luke 1:46: And Mary said: “My soul exalts the Lord,
verses.Luke 1:47: And my spirit has rejoiced in God my Savior.
verses.Luke 1:48: For He has had regard for the humble state of His bondslave; For behold, from this time on all generations will count me blessed.
verses.Luke 1:49: For the Mighty One has done great things for me; And holy is His name.
verses.Luke 1:50: And His mercy is upon generation after generation Toward those who fear Him.
verses.Luke 1:51: He has done mighty deeds with His arm; He has scattered those who were proud in the thoughts of their heart.
verses.Luke 1:52: He has brought down rulers from their thrones, And has exalted those who were humble.
verses.Luke 1:53: He has filled the hungry with good things; And sent away the rich empty-handed.
verses.Luke 1:54: He has given help to Israel His servant, In remembrance of His mercy,
verses.Luke 1:55: As He spoke to our fathers, To Abraham and his descendants forever.”
verses.Luke 1:56: And Mary stayed with her about three months, and then returned to her home.
verses.Luke 1:57: Now the time had come for Elizabeth to give birth, and she gave birth to a son.
verses.Luke 1:58: Her neighbors and her relatives heard that the Lord had displayed His great mercy toward her; and they were rejoicing with her.
verses.Luke 1:59: And it happened that on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they were going to call him Zacharias, after his father.
verses.Luke 1:60: But his mother answered and said, “No indeed; but he shall be called John.”
verses.Luke 1:61: And they said to her, “There is no one among your relatives who is called by that name.”
verses.Luke 1:62: And they made signs to his father, as to what he wanted him called.
verses.Luke 1:63: And he asked for a tablet and wrote as follows, “His name is John.” And they were all astonished.
verses.Luke 1:64: And at once his mouth was opened and his tongue loosed, and he began to speak in praise of God.
verses.Luke 1:65: Fear came on all those living around them; and all these matters were being talked about in all the hill country of Judea.
verses.Luke 1:66: All who heard them kept them in mind, saying, “What then will this child turn out to be?” For the hand of the Lord was certainly with him.
verses.Luke 1:67: And his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying:
verses.Luke 1:68: “Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, For He has visited us and accomplished redemption for His people,
verses.Luke 1:69: And has raised up a horn of salvation for us In the house of David His servant—
verses.Luke 1:70: As He spoke by the mouth of His holy prophets from of old—
verses.Luke 1:71: Salvation from our enemies, And from the hand of all who hate us;
verses.Luke 1:72: To show mercy toward our fathers, And to remember His holy covenant,
verses.Luke 1:73: The oath which He swore to Abraham our father,
verses.Luke 1:74: To grant us that we, being rescued from the hand of our enemies, Might serve Him without fear,
verses.Luke 1:75: In holiness and righteousness before Him all our days.
verses.Luke 1:76: And you, child, will be called the prophet of the Most High; For you will go on before the Lord to prepare His ways;
verses.Luke 1:77: To give to His people the knowledge of salvation By the forgiveness of their sins,
verses.Luke 1:78: Because of the tender mercy of our God, With which the Sunrise from on high will visit us,
verses.Luke 1:79: To shine upon those who sit in darkness and the shadow of death, To guide our feet into the way of peace.”
verses.Luke 1:80: And the child continued to grow and to become strong in spirit, and he lived in the deserts until the day of his public appearance to Israel.
verses.Luke 2:1: Now in those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus, that a census be taken of all the inhabited earth.
verses.Luke 2:2: This was the first census taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria.
verses.Luke 2:3: And everyone was on his way to register for the census, each to his own city.
verses.Luke 2:4: Joseph also went up from Galilee, from the city of Nazareth, to Judea, to the city of David which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David,
verses.Luke 2:5: in order to register along with Mary, who was engaged to him, and was with child.
verses.Luke 2:6: While they were there, the days were completed for her to give birth.
verses.Luke 2:7: And she gave birth to her firstborn son; and she wrapped Him in cloths, and laid Him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.
verses.Luke 2:8: In the same region there were some shepherds staying out in the fields and keeping watch over their flock by night.
verses.Luke 2:9: And an angel of the Lord suddenly stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them; and they were terribly frightened.
verses.Luke 2:10: But the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid; for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be for all the people;
verses.Luke 2:11: for today in the city of David there has been born for you a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
verses.Luke 2:12: This will be a sign for you: you will find a baby wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger.”
verses.Luke 2:13: And suddenly there appeared with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God and saying,
verses.Luke 2:14: “Glory to God in the highest, And on earth peace among men with whom He is pleased.”
verses.Luke 2:15: When the angels had gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds began saying to one another, “Let us go straight to Bethlehem then, and see this thing that has happened which the Lord has made known to us.”
verses.Luke 2:16: So they came in a hurry and found their way to Mary and Joseph, and the baby as He lay in the manger.
verses.Luke 2:17: When they had seen this, they made known the statement which had been told them about this Child.
verses.Luke 2:18: And all who heard it wondered at the things which were told them by the shepherds.
verses.Luke 2:19: But Mary treasured all these things, pondering them in her heart.
verses.Luke 2:20: The shepherds went back, glorifying and praising God for all that they had heard and seen, just as had been told them.
verses.Luke 2:21: And when eight days had passed, before His circumcision, His name was then called Jesus, the name given by the angel before He was conceived in the womb.
verses.Luke 2:22: And when the days for their purification according to the law of Moses were completed, they brought Him up to Jerusalem to present Him to the Lord
verses.Luke 2:23: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, “Every firstborn male that opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord”),
verses.Luke 2:24: and to offer a sacrifice according to what was said in the Law of the Lord, “A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons.”
verses.Luke 2:25: And there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; and this man was righteous and devout, looking for the consolation of Israel; and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
verses.Luke 2:26: And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
verses.Luke 2:27: And he came in the Spirit into the temple; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to carry out for Him the custom of the Law,
verses.Luke 2:28: then he took Him into his arms, and blessed God, and said,
verses.Luke 2:29: “Now Lord, You are releasing Your bond-servant to depart in peace, According to Your word;
verses.Luke 2:30: For my eyes have seen Your salvation,
verses.Luke 2:31: Which You have prepared in the presence of all peoples,
verses.Luke 2:32: A Light of revelation to the Gentiles, And the glory of Your people Israel.”
verses.Luke 2:33: And His father and mother were amazed at the things which were being said about Him.
verses.Luke 2:34: And Simeon blessed them and said to Mary His mother, “Behold, this Child is appointed for the fall and rise of many in Israel, and for a sign to be opposed—
verses.Luke 2:35: and a sword will pierce even your own soul—to the end that thoughts from many hearts may be revealed.”
verses.Luke 2:36: And there was a prophetess, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was advanced in years and had lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
verses.Luke 2:37: and then as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the temple, serving night and day with fastings and prayers.
verses.Luke 2:38: At that very moment she came up and began giving thanks to God, and continued to speak of Him to all those who were looking for the redemption of Jerusalem.
verses.Luke 2:39: When they had performed everything according to the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own city of Nazareth.
verses.Luke 2:40: The Child continued to grow and become strong, increasing in wisdom; and the grace of God was upon Him.
verses.Luke 2:41: Now His parents went to Jerusalem every year at the Feast of the Passover.
verses.Luke 2:42: And when He became twelve, they went up there according to the custom of the Feast;
verses.Luke 2:43: and as they were returning, after spending the full number of days, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. But His parents were unaware of it,
verses.Luke 2:44: but supposed Him to be in the caravan, and went a day’s journey; and they began looking for Him among their relatives and acquaintances.
verses.Luke 2:45: When they did not find Him, they returned to Jerusalem looking for Him.
verses.Luke 2:46: Then, after three days they found Him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers, both listening to them and asking them questions.
verses.Luke 2:47: And all who heard Him were amazed at His understanding and His answers.
verses.Luke 2:48: When they saw Him, they were astonished; and His mother said to Him, “Son, why have You treated us this way? Behold, Your father and I have been anxiously looking for You.”
verses.Luke 2:50: But they did not understand the statement which He had made to them.
verses.Luke 2:51: And He went down with them and came to Nazareth, and He continued in subjection to them; and His mother treasured all these things in her heart.
verses.Luke 2:52: And Jesus kept increasing in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.
verses.Luke 3:1: Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, when Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, and Herod was tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip was tetrarch of the region of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was tetrarch of Abilene,
verses.Luke 3:2: in the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John, the son of Zacharias, in the wilderness.
verses.Luke 3:3: And he came into all the district around the Jordan, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins;
verses.Luke 3:4: as it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make ready the way of the Lord, Make His paths straight.
verses.Luke 3:5: Every ravine will be filled, And every mountain and hill will be brought low; The crooked will become straight, And the rough roads smooth;
verses.Luke 3:6: And all flesh will see the salvation of God.’ ”
verses.Luke 3:7: So he began saying to the crowds who were going out to be baptized by him, “You brood of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
verses.Luke 3:8: Therefore bear fruits in keeping with repentance, and do not begin to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham for our father,’ for I say to you that from these stones God is able to raise up children to Abraham.
verses.Luke 3:9: Indeed the axe is already laid at the root of the trees; so every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.”
verses.Luke 3:10: And the crowds were questioning him, saying, “Then what shall we do?”
verses.Luke 3:11: And he would answer and say to them, “The man who has two tunics is to share with him who has none; and he who has food is to do likewise.”
