verses.Luke 3:12: And some tax collectors also came to be baptized, and they said to him, “Teacher, what shall we do?”
verses.Luke 3:13: And he said to them, “Collect no more than what you have been ordered to.”
verses.Luke 3:14: Some soldiers were questioning him, saying, “And what about us, what shall we do?” And he said to them, “Do not take money from anyone by force, or accuse anyone falsely, and be content with your wages.”
verses.Luke 3:15: Now while the people were in a state of expectation and all were wondering in their hearts about John, as to whether he was the Christ,
verses.Luke 3:16: John answered and said to them all, “As for me, I baptize you with water; but One is coming who is mightier than I, and I am not fit to untie the thong of His sandals; He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
verses.Luke 3:17: His winnowing fork is in His hand to thoroughly clear His threshing floor, and to gather the wheat into His barn; but He will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”
verses.Luke 3:18: So with many other exhortations he preached the gospel to the people.
verses.Luke 3:19: But when Herod the tetrarch was reprimanded by him because of Herodias, his brother’s wife, and because of all the wicked things which Herod had done,
verses.Luke 3:20: Herod also added this to them all: he locked John up in prison.
verses.Luke 3:21: Now when all the people were baptized, Jesus was also baptized, and while He was praying, heaven was opened,
verses.Luke 3:22: and the Holy Spirit descended upon Him in bodily form like a dove, and a voice came out of heaven, “You are My beloved Son, in You I am well-pleased.”
verses.Luke 3:23: When He began His ministry, Jesus Himself was about thirty years of age, being, as was supposed, the son of Joseph, the son of Eli,
verses.Luke 3:24: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,
verses.Luke 3:25: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Hesli, the son of Naggai,
verses.Luke 3:26: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Josech, the son of Joda,
verses.Luke 3:27: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,
verses.Luke 3:28: the son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmadam, the son of Er,
verses.Luke 3:29: the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,
verses.Luke 3:30: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim,
verses.Luke 3:31: the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,
verses.Luke 3:32: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon,
verses.Luke 3:33: the son of Amminadab, the son of Admin, the son of Ram, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,
verses.Luke 3:34: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,
verses.Luke 3:35: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Heber, the son of Shelah,
verses.Luke 3:36: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,
verses.Luke 3:37: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalaleel, the son of Cainan,
verses.Luke 3:38: the son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.
verses.Luke 4:1: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led around by the Spirit in the wilderness
verses.Luke 4:2: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. And He ate nothing during those days, and when they had ended, He became hungry.
verses.Luke 4:3: And the devil said to Him, “If You are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread.”
verses.Luke 4:5: And he led Him up and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
verses.Luke 4:6: And the devil said to Him, “I will give You all this domain and its glory; for it has been handed over to me, and I give it to whomever I wish.
verses.Luke 4:7: Therefore if You worship before me, it shall all be Yours.”
verses.Luke 4:9: And he led Him to Jerusalem and had Him stand on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to Him, “If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down from here;
verses.Luke 4:10: for it is written, ‘He will command His angels concerning You to guard You,’
verses.Luke 4:11: and, ‘On their hands they will bear You up, So that You will not strike Your foot against a stone.’ ”
verses.Luke 4:13: When the devil had finished every temptation, he left Him until an opportune time.
verses.Luke 4:14: And Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news about Him spread through all the surrounding district.
verses.Luke 4:15: And He began teaching in their synagogues and was praised by all.
verses.Luke 4:16: And He came to Nazareth, where He had been brought up; and as was His custom, He entered the synagogue on the Sabbath, and stood up to read.
verses.Luke 4:17: And the book of the prophet Isaiah was handed to Him. And He opened the book and found the place where it was written,
verses.Luke 4:20: And He closed the book, gave it back to the attendant and sat down; and the eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed on Him.
verses.Luke 4:22: And all were speaking well of Him, and wondering at the gracious words which were falling from His lips; and they were saying, “Is this not Joseph’s son?”
verses.Luke 4:28: And all the people in the synagogue were filled with rage as they heard these things;
verses.Luke 4:29: and they got up and drove Him out of the city, and led Him to the brow of the hill on which their city had been built, in order to throw Him down the cliff.
verses.Luke 4:30: But passing through their midst, He went His way.
verses.Luke 4:31: And He came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and He was teaching them on the Sabbath;
verses.Luke 4:32: and they were amazed at His teaching, for His message was with authority.
verses.Luke 4:33: In the synagogue there was a man possessed by the spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,
verses.Luke 4:34: “Let us alone! What business do we have with each other, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God!”
verses.Luke 4:36: And amazement came upon them all, and they began talking with one another saying, “What is this message? For with authority and power He commands the unclean spirits and they come out.”
verses.Luke 4:37: And the report about Him was spreading into every locality in the surrounding district.
verses.Luke 4:38: Then He got up and left the synagogue, and entered Simon’s home. Now Simon’s mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked Him to help her.
verses.Luke 4:39: And standing over her, He rebuked the fever, and it left her; and she immediately got up and waited on them.
verses.Luke 4:40: While the sun was setting, all those who had any who were sick with various diseases brought them to Him; and laying His hands on each one of them, He was healing them.
verses.Luke 4:41: Demons also were coming out of many, shouting, “You are the Son of God!” But rebuking them, He would not allow them to speak, because they knew Him to be the Christ.
verses.Luke 4:42: When day came, Jesus left and went to a secluded place; and the crowds were searching for Him, and came to Him and tried to keep Him from going away from them.
verses.Luke 4:44: So He kept on preaching in the synagogues of Judea.
verses.Luke 5:1: Now it happened that while the crowd was pressing around Him and listening to the word of God, He was standing by the lake of Gennesaret;
verses.Luke 5:2: and He saw two boats lying at the edge of the lake; but the fishermen had gotten out of them and were washing their nets.
verses.Luke 5:3: And He got into one of the boats, which was Simon’s, and asked him to put out a little way from the land. And He sat down and began teaching the people from the boat.
verses.Luke 5:5: Simon answered and said, “Master, we worked hard all night and caught nothing, but I will do as You say and let down the nets.”
verses.Luke 5:6: When they had done this, they enclosed a great quantity of fish, and their nets began to break;
verses.Luke 5:7: so they signaled to their partners in the other boat for them to come and help them. And they came and filled both of the boats, so that they began to sink.
verses.Luke 5:8: But when Simon Peter saw that, he fell down at Jesus’ feet, saying, “Go away from me Lord, for I am a sinful man!”
verses.Luke 5:9: For amazement had seized him and all his companions because of the catch of fish which they had taken;
verses.Luke 5:11: When they had brought their boats to land, they left everything and followed Him.
verses.Luke 5:12: While He was in one of the cities, behold, there was a man covered with leprosy; and when he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and implored Him, saying, “Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.”
verses.Luke 5:15: But the news about Him was spreading even farther, and large crowds were gathering to hear Him and to be healed of their sicknesses.
verses.Luke 5:16: But Jesus Himself would often slip away to the wilderness and pray.
verses.Luke 5:17: One day He was teaching; and there were some Pharisees and teachers of the law sitting there, who had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem; and the power of the Lord was present for Him to perform healing.
verses.Luke 5:18: And some men were carrying on a bed a man who was paralyzed; and they were trying to bring him in and to set him down in front of Him.
verses.Luke 5:19: But not finding any way to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and let him down through the tiles with his stretcher, into the middle of the crowd, in front of Jesus.
verses.Luke 5:21: The scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, “Who is this man who speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?”
verses.Luke 5:25: Immediately he got up before them, and picked up what he had been lying on, and went home glorifying God.
verses.Luke 5:26: They were all struck with astonishment and began glorifying God; and they were filled with fear, saying, “We have seen remarkable things today.”
verses.Luke 5:28: And he left everything behind, and got up and began to follow Him.
verses.Luke 5:29: And Levi gave a big reception for Him in his house; and there was a great crowd of tax collectors and other people who were reclining at the table with them.
verses.Luke 5:30: The Pharisees and their scribes began grumbling at His disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with the tax collectors and sinners?”
verses.Luke 5:33: And they said to Him, “The disciples of John often fast and offer prayers, the disciples of the Pharisees also do the same, but Yours eat and drink.”
verses.Luke 6:1: Now it happened that He was passing through some grainfields on a Sabbath; and His disciples were picking the heads of grain, rubbing them in their hands, and eating the grain.
verses.Luke 6:2: But some of the Pharisees said, “Why do you do what is not lawful on the Sabbath?”
verses.Luke 6:6: On another Sabbath He entered the synagogue and was teaching; and there was a man there whose right hand was withered.
verses.Luke 6:7: The scribes and the Pharisees were watching Him closely to see if He healed on the Sabbath, so that they might find reason to accuse Him.
verses.Luke 6:11: But they themselves were filled with rage, and discussed together what they might do to Jesus.
verses.Luke 6:12: It was at this time that He went off to the mountain to pray, and He spent the whole night in prayer to God.
verses.Luke 6:13: And when day came, He called His disciples to Him and chose twelve of them, whom He also named as apostles:
verses.Luke 6:14: Simon, whom He also named Peter, and Andrew his brother; and James and John; and Philip and Bartholomew;
verses.Luke 6:15: and Matthew and Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus, and Simon who was called the Zealot;
verses.Luke 6:16: Judas the son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
verses.Luke 6:17: Jesus came down with them and stood on a level place; and there was a large crowd of His disciples, and a great throng of people from all Judea and Jerusalem and the coastal region of Tyre and Sidon,
verses.Luke 6:18: who had come to hear Him and to be healed of their diseases; and those who were troubled with unclean spirits were being cured.
verses.Luke 6:19: And all the people were trying to touch Him, for power was coming from Him and healing them all.
verses.Luke 7:1: When He had completed all His discourse in the hearing of the people, He went to Capernaum.
verses.Luke 7:2: And a centurion’s slave, who was highly regarded by him, was sick and about to die.
verses.Luke 7:3: When he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders asking Him to come and save the life of his slave.
verses.Luke 7:4: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly implored Him, saying, “He is worthy for You to grant this to him;
verses.Luke 7:5: for he loves our nation and it was he who built us our synagogue.”
verses.Luke 7:6: Now Jesus started on His way with them; and when He was not far from the house, the centurion sent friends, saying to Him, “Lord, do not trouble Yourself further, for I am not worthy for You to come under my roof;
verses.Luke 7:7: for this reason I did not even consider myself worthy to come to You, but just say the word, and my servant will be healed.
verses.Luke 7:8: For I also am a man placed under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to this one, ‘Go!’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes, and to my slave, ‘Do this!’ and he does it.”
verses.Luke 7:10: When those who had been sent returned to the house, they found the slave in good health.
verses.Luke 7:11: Soon afterwards He went to a city called Nain; and His disciples were going along with Him, accompanied by a large crowd.
verses.Luke 7:12: Now as He approached the gate of the city, a dead man was being carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow; and a sizeable crowd from the city was with her.
verses.Luke 7:15: The dead man sat up and began to speak. And Jesus gave him back to his mother.
verses.Luke 7:16: Fear gripped them all, and they began glorifying God, saying, “A great prophet has arisen among us!” and, “God has visited His people!”
verses.Luke 7:17: This report concerning Him went out all over Judea and in all the surrounding district.
verses.Luke 7:18: The disciples of John reported to him about all these things.
verses.Luke 7:19: Summoning two of his disciples, John sent them to the Lord, saying, “Are You the Expected One, or do we look for someone else?”
verses.Luke 7:20: When the men came to Him, they said, “John the Baptist has sent us to You, to ask, ‘Are You the Expected One, or do we look for someone else?’ ”
verses.Luke 7:21: At that very time He cured many people of diseases and afflictions and evil spirits; and He gave sight to many who were blind.
verses.Luke 7:29: When all the people and the tax collectors heard this, they acknowledged God’s justice, having been baptized with the baptism of John.
verses.Luke 7:30: But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected God’s purpose for themselves, not having been baptized by John.
verses.Luke 7:36: Now one of the Pharisees was requesting Him to dine with him, and He entered the Pharisee’s house and reclined at the table.
verses.Luke 7:37: And there was a woman in the city who was a sinner; and when she learned that He was reclining at the table in the Pharisee’s house, she brought an alabaster vial of perfume,
verses.Luke 7:38: and standing behind Him at His feet, weeping, she began to wet His feet with her tears, and kept wiping them with the hair of her head, and kissing His feet and anointing them with the perfume.
verses.Luke 7:39: Now when the Pharisee who had invited Him saw this, he said to himself, “If this man were a prophet He would know who and what sort of person this woman is who is touching Him, that she is a sinner.”
verses.Luke 7:49: Those who were reclining at the table with Him began to say to themselves, “Who is this man who even forgives sins?”
verses.Luke 8:1: Soon afterwards, He began going around from one city and village to another, proclaiming and preaching the kingdom of God. The twelve were with Him,
verses.Luke 8:2: and also some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses: Mary who was called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,
verses.Luke 8:3: and Joanna the wife of Chuza, Herod’s steward, and Susanna, and many others who were contributing to their support out of their private means.
verses.Luke 8:4: When a large crowd was coming together, and those from the various cities were journeying to Him, He spoke by way of a parable:
verses.Luke 8:9: His disciples began questioning Him as to what this parable meant.
verses.Luke 8:19: And His mother and brothers came to Him, and they were unable to get to Him because of the crowd.
verses.Luke 8:20: And it was reported to Him, “Your mother and Your brothers are standing outside, wishing to see You.”
verses.Luke 8:23: But as they were sailing along He fell asleep; and a fierce gale of wind descended on the lake, and they began to be swamped and to be in danger.
verses.Luke 8:24: They came to Jesus and woke Him up, saying, “Master, Master, we are perishing!” And He got up and rebuked the wind and the surging waves, and they stopped, and it became calm.
verses.Luke 8:26: Then they sailed to the country of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.
verses.Luke 8:27: And when He came out onto the land, He was met by a man from the city who was possessed with demons; and who had not put on any clothing for a long time, and was not living in a house, but in the tombs.
verses.Luke 8:28: Seeing Jesus, he cried out and fell before Him, and said in a loud voice, “What business do we have with each other, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg You, do not torment me.”
verses.Luke 8:29: For He had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For it had seized him many times; and he was bound with chains and shackles and kept under guard, and yet he would break his bonds and be driven by the demon into the desert.
verses.Luke 8:31: They were imploring Him not to command them to go away into the abyss.
verses.Luke 8:32: Now there was a herd of many swine feeding there on the mountain; and the demons implored Him to permit them to enter the swine. And He gave them permission.
verses.Luke 8:33: And the demons came out of the man and entered the swine; and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and was drowned.
verses.Luke 8:34: When the herdsmen saw what had happened, they ran away and reported it in the city and out in the country.
verses.Luke 8:35: The people went out to see what had happened; and they came to Jesus, and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting down at the feet of Jesus, clothed and in his right mind; and they became frightened.
verses.Luke 8:36: Those who had seen it reported to them how the man who was demon-possessed had been made well.
verses.Luke 8:37: And all the people of the country of the Gerasenes and the surrounding district asked Him to leave them, for they were gripped with great fear; and He got into a boat and returned.
verses.Luke 8:38: But the man from whom the demons had gone out was begging Him that he might accompany Him; but He sent him away, saying,
verses.Luke 8:40: And as Jesus returned, the people welcomed Him, for they had all been waiting for Him.
verses.Luke 8:41: And there came a man named Jairus, and he was an official of the synagogue; and he fell at Jesus’ feet, and began to implore Him to come to his house;
verses.Luke 8:42: for he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, and she was dying. But as He went, the crowds were pressing against Him.
verses.Luke 8:43: And a woman who had a hemorrhage for twelve years, and could not be healed by anyone,
verses.Luke 8:44: came up behind Him and touched the fringe of His cloak, and immediately her hemorrhage stopped.
verses.Luke 8:47: When the woman saw that she had not escaped notice, she came trembling and fell down before Him, and declared in the presence of all the people the reason why she had touched Him, and how she had been immediately healed.
verses.Luke 8:49: While He was still speaking, someone came from the house of the synagogue official, saying, “Your daughter has died; do not trouble the Teacher anymore.”
verses.Luke 8:51: When He came to the house, He did not allow anyone to enter with Him, except Peter and John and James, and the girl’s father and mother.
verses.Luke 8:53: And they began laughing at Him, knowing that she had died.
verses.Luke 8:55: And her spirit returned, and she got up immediately; and He gave orders for something to be given her to eat.
verses.Luke 8:56: Her parents were amazed; but He instructed them to tell no one what had happened.
verses.Luke 9:1: And He called the twelve together, and gave them power and authority over all the demons and to heal diseases.
verses.Luke 9:2: And He sent them out to proclaim the kingdom of God and to perform healing.
verses.Luke 9:6: Departing, they began going throughout the villages, preaching the gospel and healing everywhere.
verses.Luke 9:7: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was happening; and he was greatly perplexed, because it was said by some that John had risen from the dead,
verses.Luke 9:8: and by some that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the prophets of old had risen again.
verses.Luke 9:9: Herod said, “I myself had John beheaded; but who is this man about whom I hear such things?” And he kept trying to see Him.
verses.Luke 9:10: When the apostles returned, they gave an account to Him of all that they had done. Taking them with Him, He withdrew by Himself to a city called Bethsaida.
verses.Luke 9:11: But the crowds were aware of this and followed Him; and welcoming them, He began speaking to them about the kingdom of God and curing those who had need of healing.
verses.Luke 9:12: Now the day was ending, and the twelve came and said to Him, “Send the crowd away, that they may go into the surrounding villages and countryside and find lodging and get something to eat; for here we are in a desolate place.”
verses.Luke 9:15: They did so, and had them all sit down.
verses.Luke 9:16: Then He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, He blessed them, and broke them, and kept giving them to the disciples to set before the people.
verses.Luke 9:17: And they all ate and were satisfied; and the broken pieces which they had left over were picked up, twelve baskets full.
verses.Luke 9:19: They answered and said, “John the Baptist, and others say Elijah; but others, that one of the prophets of old has risen again.”
verses.Luke 9:21: But He warned them and instructed them not to tell this to anyone,
verses.Luke 9:28: Some eight days after these sayings, He took along Peter and John and James, and went up on the mountain to pray.
verses.Luke 9:29: And while He was praying, the appearance of His face became different, and His clothing became white and gleaming.
verses.Luke 9:30: And behold, two men were talking with Him; and they were Moses and Elijah,
verses.Luke 9:31: who, appearing in glory, were speaking of His departure which He was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.
verses.Luke 9:32: Now Peter and his companions had been overcome with sleep; but when they were fully awake, they saw His glory and the two men standing with Him.
verses.Luke 9:33: And as these were leaving Him, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good for us to be here; let us make three tabernacles: one for You, and one for Moses, and one for Elijah”—not realizing what he was saying.
verses.Luke 9:34: While he was saying this, a cloud formed and began to overshadow them; and they were afraid as they entered the cloud.
verses.Luke 9:35: Then a voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is My Son, My Chosen One; listen to Him!”
verses.Luke 9:36: And when the voice had spoken, Jesus was found alone. And they kept silent, and reported to no one in those days any of the things which they had seen.
verses.Luke 9:37: On the next day, when they came down from the mountain, a large crowd met Him.
verses.Luke 9:38: And a man from the crowd shouted, saying, “Teacher, I beg You to look at my son, for he is my only boy,
verses.Luke 9:39: and a spirit seizes him, and he suddenly screams, and it throws him into a convulsion with foaming at the mouth; and only with difficulty does it leave him, mauling him as it leaves.
verses.Luke 9:40: I begged Your disciples to cast it out, and they could not.”
verses.Luke 9:42: While he was still approaching, the demon slammed him to the ground and threw him into a convulsion. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the boy and gave him back to his father.
verses.Luke 9:43: And they were all amazed at the greatness of God. But while everyone was marveling at all that He was doing, He said to His disciples,
verses.Luke 9:45: But they did not understand this statement, and it was concealed from them so that they would not perceive it; and they were afraid to ask Him about this statement.
verses.Luke 9:46: An argument started among them as to which of them might be the greatest.
verses.Luke 9:47: But Jesus, knowing what they were thinking in their heart, took a child and stood him by His side,
verses.Luke 9:49: John answered and said, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in Your name; and we tried to prevent him because he does not follow along with us.”
verses.Luke 9:51: When the days were approaching for His ascension, He was determined to go to Jerusalem;
verses.Luke 9:52: and He sent messengers on ahead of Him, and they went and entered a village of the Samaritans to make arrangements for Him.
verses.Luke 9:53: But they did not receive Him, because He was traveling toward Jerusalem.
verses.Luke 9:54: When His disciples James and John saw this, they said, “Lord, do You want us to command fire to come down from heaven and consume them?”
verses.Luke 9:57: As they were going along the road, someone said to Him, “I will follow You wherever You go.”
verses.Luke 9:61: Another also said, “I will follow You, Lord; but first permit me to say good-bye to those at home.”
verses.Luke 10:1: Now after this the Lord appointed seventy others, and sent them in pairs ahead of Him to every city and place where He Himself was going to come.
verses.Luke 10:17: The seventy returned with joy, saying, “Lord, even the demons are subject to us in Your name.”
verses.Luke 10:25: And a lawyer stood up and put Him to the test, saying, “Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
verses.Luke 10:27: And he answered, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your strength, and with all your mind;and your neighbor as yourself.”
verses.Luke 10:29: But wishing to justify himself, he said to Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”
verses.Luke 10:38: Now as they were traveling along, He entered a village; and a woman named Martha welcomed Him into her home.
verses.Luke 10:39: She had a sister called Mary, who was seated at the Lord’s feet, listening to His word.
verses.Luke 10:40: But Martha was distracted with all her preparations; and she came up to Him and said, “Lord, do You not care that my sister has left me to do all the serving alone? Then tell her to help me.”
verses.Luke 11:1: It happened that while Jesus was praying in a certain place, after He had finished, one of His disciples said to Him, “Lord, teach us to pray just as John also taught his disciples.”
verses.Luke 11:14: And He was casting out a demon, and it was mute; when the demon had gone out, the mute man spoke; and the crowds were amazed.
verses.Luke 11:15: But some of them said, “He casts out demons by Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.”
verses.Luke 11:16: Others, to test Him, were demanding of Him a sign from heaven.
verses.Luke 11:27: While Jesus was saying these things, one of the women in the crowd raised her voice and said to Him, “Blessed is the womb that bore You and the breasts at which You nursed.”
verses.Luke 11:37: Now when He had spoken, a Pharisee asked Him to have lunch with him; and He went in, and reclined at the table.
verses.Luke 11:38: When the Pharisee saw it, he was surprised that He had not first ceremonially washed before the meal.
verses.Luke 11:45: One of the lawyers said to Him in reply, “Teacher, when You say this, You insult us too.”
verses.Luke 11:53: When He left there, the scribes and the Pharisees began to be very hostile and to question Him closely on many subjects,
verses.Luke 11:54: plotting against Him to catch Him in something He might say.
verses.Luke 12:13: Someone in the crowd said to Him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the family inheritance with me.”
verses.Luke 12:41: Peter said, “Lord, are You addressing this parable to us, or to everyone else as well?”
verses.Luke 13:1: Now on the same occasion there were some present who reported to Him about the Galileans whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.
verses.Luke 13:10: And He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
verses.Luke 13:11: And there was a woman who for eighteen years had had a sickness caused by a spirit; and she was bent double, and could not straighten up at all.
verses.Luke 13:13: And He laid His hands on her; and immediately she was made erect again and began glorifying God.
verses.Luke 13:14: But the synagogue official, indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, began saying to the crowd in response, “There are six days in which work should be done; so come during them and get healed, and not on the Sabbath day.”
verses.Luke 13:17: As He said this, all His opponents were being humiliated; and the entire crowd was rejoicing over all the glorious things being done by Him.
verses.Luke 13:22: And He was passing through from one city and village to another, teaching, and proceeding on His way to Jerusalem.

verses.Luke 13:31: Just at that time some Pharisees approached, saying to Him, “Go away, leave here, for Herod wants to kill You.”
verses.Luke 14:1: It happened that when He went into the house of one of the leaders of the Pharisees on the Sabbath to eat bread, they were watching Him closely.
verses.Luke 14:2: And there in front of Him was a man suffering from dropsy.
verses.Luke 14:4: But they kept silent. And He took hold of him and healed him, and sent him away.
verses.Luke 14:6: And they could make no reply to this.
verses.Luke 14:7: And He began speaking a parable to the invited guests when He noticed how they had been picking out the places of honor at the table, saying to them,

verses.Luke 14:25: Now large crowds were going along with Him; and He turned and said to them,
verses.Luke 15:1: Now all the tax collectors and the sinners were coming near Him to listen to Him.
verses.Luke 15:2: Both the Pharisees and the scribes began to grumble, saying, “This man receives sinners and eats with them.”
verses.Luke 15:3: So He told them this parable, saying,
verses.Luke 16:14: Now the Pharisees, who were lovers of money, were listening to all these things and were scoffing at Him.
verses.Luke 17:5: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”
verses.Luke 17:11: While He was on the way to Jerusalem, He was passing between Samaria and Galilee.
verses.Luke 17:12: As He entered a village, ten leprous men who stood at a distance met Him;
verses.Luke 17:13: and they raised their voices, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”
verses.Luke 17:15: Now one of them, when he saw that he had been healed, turned back, glorifying God with a loud voice,
verses.Luke 17:16: and he fell on his face at His feet, giving thanks to Him. And he was a Samaritan.
verses.Luke 18:1: Now He was telling them a parable to show that at all times they ought to pray and not to lose heart,
verses.Luke 18:9: And He also told this parable to some people who trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and viewed others with contempt:
verses.Luke 18:15: And they were bringing even their babies to Him so that He would touch them, but when the disciples saw it, they began rebuking them.
verses.Luke 18:18: A ruler questioned Him, saying, “Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”

verses.Luke 18:34: But the disciples understood none of these things, and the meaning of this statement was hidden from them, and they did not comprehend the things that were said.
verses.Luke 18:35: As Jesus was approaching Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the road begging.
verses.Luke 18:36: Now hearing a crowd going by, he began to inquire what this was.
verses.Luke 18:37: They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.
verses.Luke 18:38: And he called out, saying, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
verses.Luke 18:39: Those who led the way were sternly telling him to be quiet; but he kept crying out all the more, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
