verses.Luke 18:43: Immediately he regained his sight and began following Him, glorifying God; and when all the people saw it, they gave praise to God.
verses.Luke 19:1: He entered Jericho and was passing through.
verses.Luke 19:2: And there was a man called by the name of Zaccheus; he was a chief tax collector and he was rich.
verses.Luke 19:3: Zaccheus was trying to see who Jesus was, and was unable because of the crowd, for he was small in stature.
verses.Luke 19:4: So he ran on ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree in order to see Him, for He was about to pass through that way.
verses.Luke 19:6: And he hurried and came down and received Him gladly.
verses.Luke 19:7: When they saw it, they all began to grumble, saying, “He has gone to be the guest of a man who is a sinner.”
verses.Luke 19:8: Zaccheus stopped and said to the Lord, “Behold, Lord, half of my possessions I will give to the poor, and if I have defrauded anyone of anything, I will give back four times as much.”
verses.Luke 19:11: While they were listening to these things, Jesus went on to tell a parable, because He was near Jerusalem, and they supposed that the kingdom of God was going to appear immediately.
verses.Luke 19:28: After He had said these things, He was going on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
verses.Luke 19:29: When He approached Bethphage and Bethany, near the mount that is called Olivet, He sent two of the disciples,
verses.Luke 19:32: So those who were sent went away and found it just as He had told them.
verses.Luke 19:33: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, “Why are you untying the colt?”
verses.Luke 19:34: They said, “The Lord has need of it.”
verses.Luke 19:35: They brought it to Jesus, and they threw their coats on the colt and put Jesus on it.
verses.Luke 19:36: As He was going, they were spreading their coats on the road.
verses.Luke 19:37: As soon as He was approaching, near the descent of the Mount of Olives, the whole crowd of the disciples began to praise God joyfully with a loud voice for all the miracles which they had seen,
verses.Luke 19:38: shouting: “Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord; Peace in heaven and glory in the highest!”
verses.Luke 19:39: Some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to Him, “Teacher, rebuke Your disciples.”
verses.Luke 19:41: When He approached Jerusalem, He saw the city and wept over it,
verses.Luke 19:45: Jesus entered the temple and began to drive out those who were selling,
verses.Luke 19:47: And He was teaching daily in the temple; but the chief priests and the scribes and the leading men among the people were trying to destroy Him,
verses.Luke 19:48: and they could not find anything that they might do, for all the people were hanging on to every word He said.
verses.Luke 20:1: On one of the days while He was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes with the elders confronted Him,
verses.Luke 20:2: and they spoke, saying to Him, “Tell us by what authority You are doing these things, or who is the one who gave You this authority?”
verses.Luke 20:5: They reasoned among themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ He will say, ‘Why did you not believe him?’
verses.Luke 20:6: But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us to death, for they are convinced that John was a prophet.”
verses.Luke 20:7: So they answered that they did not know where it came from.
verses.Luke 20:19: The scribes and the chief priests tried to lay hands on Him that very hour, and they feared the people; for they understood that He spoke this parable against them.
verses.Luke 20:20: So they watched Him, and sent spies who pretended to be righteous, in order that they might catch Him in some statement, so that they could deliver Him to the rule and the authority of the governor.
verses.Luke 20:21: They questioned Him, saying, “Teacher, we know that You speak and teach correctly, and You are not partial to any, but teach the way of God in truth.
verses.Luke 20:22: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”
verses.Luke 20:23: But He detected their trickery and said to them,
verses.Luke 20:26: And they were unable to catch Him in a saying in the presence of the people; and being amazed at His answer, they became silent.
verses.Luke 20:27: Now there came to Him some of the Sadducees (who say that there is no resurrection),
verses.Luke 20:28: and they questioned Him, saying, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies, having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should marry the wife and raise up children to his brother.
verses.Luke 20:29: Now there were seven brothers; and the first took a wife and died childless;
verses.Luke 20:31: and the third married her; and in the same way all seven died, leaving no children.
verses.Luke 20:33: In the resurrection therefore, which one’s wife will she be? For all seven had married her.”
verses.Luke 20:39: Some of the scribes answered and said, “Teacher, You have spoken well.”
verses.Luke 20:40: For they did not have courage to question Him any longer about anything.
verses.Luke 20:45: And while all the people were listening, He said to the disciples,
verses.Luke 21:1: And He looked up and saw the rich putting their gifts into the treasury.
verses.Luke 21:2: And He saw a poor widow putting in two small copper coins.
verses.Luke 21:5: And while some were talking about the temple, that it was adorned with beautiful stones and votive gifts, He said,
verses.Luke 21:7: They questioned Him, saying, “Teacher, when therefore will these things happen? And what will be the sign when these things are about to take place?”
verses.Luke 21:37: Now during the day He was teaching in the temple, but at evening He would go out and spend the night on the mount that is called Olivet.
verses.Luke 21:38: And all the people would get up early in the morning to come to Him in the temple to listen to Him.
verses.Luke 22:1: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was approaching.
verses.Luke 22:2: The chief priests and the scribes were seeking how they might put Him to death; for they were afraid of the people.
verses.Luke 22:3: And Satan entered into Judas who was called Iscariot, belonging to the number of the twelve.
verses.Luke 22:4: And he went away and discussed with the chief priests and officers how he might betray Him to them.
verses.Luke 22:5: They were glad and agreed to give him money.
verses.Luke 22:6: So he consented, and began seeking a good opportunity to betray Him to them apart from the crowd.
verses.Luke 22:7: Then came the first day of Unleavened Bread on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.
verses.Luke 22:9: They said to Him, “Where do You want us to prepare it?”
verses.Luke 22:13: And they left and found everything just as He had told them; and they prepared the Passover.
verses.Luke 22:14: When the hour had come, He reclined at the table, and the apostles with Him.
verses.Luke 22:23: And they began to discuss among themselves which one of them it might be who was going to do this thing.
verses.Luke 22:24: And there arose also a dispute among them as to which one of them was regarded to be greatest.
verses.Luke 22:33: But he said to Him, “Lord, with You I am ready to go both to prison and to death!”
verses.Luke 22:39: And He came out and proceeded as was His custom to the Mount of Olives; and the disciples also followed Him.
verses.Luke 22:41: And He withdrew from them about a stone’s throw, and He knelt down and began to pray,
verses.Luke 22:43: Now an angel from heaven appeared to Him, strengthening Him.
verses.Luke 22:44: And being in agony He was praying very fervently; and His sweat became like drops of blood, falling down upon the ground.
verses.Luke 22:45: When He rose from prayer, He came to the disciples and found them sleeping from sorrow,
verses.Luke 22:47: While He was still speaking, behold, a crowd came, and the one called Judas, one of the twelve, was preceding them; and he approached Jesus to kiss Him.
verses.Luke 22:49: When those who were around Him saw what was going to happen, they said, “Lord, shall we strike with the sword?”
verses.Luke 22:50: And one of them struck the slave of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
verses.Luke 22:54: Having arrested Him, they led Him away and brought Him to the house of the high priest; but Peter was following at a distance.
verses.Luke 22:55: After they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and had sat down together, Peter was sitting among them.
verses.Luke 22:56: And a servant-girl, seeing him as he sat in the firelight and looking intently at him, said, “This man was with Him too.”
verses.Luke 22:57: But he denied it, saying, “Woman, I do not know Him.”
verses.Luke 22:58: A little later, another saw him and said, “You are one of them too!” But Peter said, “Man, I am not!”
verses.Luke 22:59: After about an hour had passed, another man began to insist, saying, “Certainly this man also was with Him, for he is a Galilean too.”
verses.Luke 22:60: But Peter said, “Man, I do not know what you are talking about.” Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
verses.Luke 22:62: And he went out and wept bitterly.
verses.Luke 22:63: Now the men who were holding Jesus in custody were mocking Him and beating Him,
verses.Luke 22:64: and they blindfolded Him and were asking Him, saying, “Prophesy, who is the one who hit You?”
verses.Luke 22:65: And they were saying many other things against Him, blaspheming.
verses.Luke 22:66: When it was day, the Council of elders of the people assembled, both chief priests and scribes, and they led Him away to their council chamber, saying,
verses.Luke 22:71: Then they said, “What further need do we have of testimony? For we have heard it ourselves from His own mouth.”
verses.Luke 23:1: Then the whole body of them got up and brought Him before Pilate.
verses.Luke 23:2: And they began to accuse Him, saying, “We found this man misleading our nation and forbidding to pay taxes to Caesar, and saying that He Himself is Christ, a King.”
verses.Luke 23:4: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, “I find no guilt in this man.”
verses.Luke 23:5: But they kept on insisting, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching all over Judea, starting from Galilee even as far as this place.”
verses.Luke 23:6: When Pilate heard it, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.
verses.Luke 23:7: And when he learned that He belonged to Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent Him to Herod, who himself also was in Jerusalem at that time.
verses.Luke 23:8: Now Herod was very glad when he saw Jesus; for he had wanted to see Him for a long time, because he had been hearing about Him and was hoping to see some sign performed by Him.
verses.Luke 23:9: And he questioned Him at some length; but He answered him nothing.
verses.Luke 23:10: And the chief priests and the scribes were standing there, accusing Him vehemently.
verses.Luke 23:11: And Herod with his soldiers, after treating Him with contempt and mocking Him, dressed Him in a gorgeous robe and sent Him back to Pilate.
verses.Luke 23:12: Now Herod and Pilate became friends with one another that very day; for before they had been enemies with each other.
verses.Luke 23:13: Pilate summoned the chief priests and the rulers and the people,
verses.Luke 23:14: and said to them, “You brought this man to me as one who incites the people to rebellion, and behold, having examined Him before you, I have found no guilt in this man regarding the charges which you make against Him.
verses.Luke 23:15: No, nor has Herod, for he sent Him back to us; and behold, nothing deserving death has been done by Him.
verses.Luke 23:16: Therefore I will punish Him and release Him.”
verses.Luke 23:17: [Now he was obliged to release to them at the feast one prisoner.]
verses.Luke 23:18: But they cried out all together, saying, “Away with this man, and release for us Barabbas!”
verses.Luke 23:19: (He was one who had been thrown into prison for an insurrection made in the city, and for murder.)
verses.Luke 23:20: Pilate, wanting to release Jesus, addressed them again,
verses.Luke 23:21: but they kept on calling out, saying, “Crucify, crucify Him!”
verses.Luke 23:22: And he said to them the third time, “Why, what evil has this man done? I have found in Him no guilt demanding death; therefore I will punish Him and release Him.”
verses.Luke 23:23: But they were insistent, with loud voices asking that He be crucified. And their voices began to prevail.
verses.Luke 23:24: And Pilate pronounced sentence that their demand be granted.
verses.Luke 23:25: And he released the man they were asking for who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, but he delivered Jesus to their will.
verses.Luke 23:26: When they led Him away, they seized a man, Simon of Cyrene, coming in from the country, and placed on him the cross to carry behind Jesus.
verses.Luke 23:27: And following Him was a large crowd of the people, and of women who were mourning and lamenting Him.
verses.Luke 23:32: Two others also, who were criminals, were being led away to be put to death with Him.
verses.Luke 23:33: When they came to the place called The Skull, there they crucified Him and the criminals, one on the right and the other on the left.
verses.Luke 23:35: And the people stood by, looking on. And even the rulers were sneering at Him, saying, “He saved others; let Him save Himself if this is the Christ of God, His Chosen One.”
verses.Luke 23:36: The soldiers also mocked Him, coming up to Him, offering Him sour wine,
verses.Luke 23:37: and saying, “If You are the King of the Jews, save Yourself!”
verses.Luke 23:38: Now there was also an inscription above Him, “THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.”
verses.Luke 23:39: One of the criminals who were hanged there was hurling abuse at Him, saying, “Are You not the Christ? Save Yourself and us!”
verses.Luke 23:40: But the other answered, and rebuking him said, “Do you not even fear God, since you are under the same sentence of condemnation?
verses.Luke 23:41: And we indeed are suffering justly, for we are receiving what we deserve for our deeds; but this man has done nothing wrong.”
verses.Luke 23:42: And he was saying, “Jesus, remember me when You come in Your kingdom!”
verses.Luke 23:44: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness fell over the whole land until the ninth hour,
verses.Luke 23:45: because the sun was obscured; and the veil of the temple was torn in two.
verses.Luke 23:47: Now when the centurion saw what had happened, he began praising God, saying, “Certainly this man was innocent.”
verses.Luke 23:48: And all the crowds who came together for this spectacle, when they observed what had happened, began to return, beating their breasts.
verses.Luke 23:49: And all His acquaintances and the women who accompanied Him from Galilee were standing at a distance, seeing these things.
verses.Luke 23:50: And a man named Joseph, who was a member of the Council, a good and righteous man
verses.Luke 23:51: (he had not consented to their plan and action), a man from Arimathea, a city of the Jews, who was waiting for the kingdom of God;
verses.Luke 23:52: this man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
verses.Luke 23:53: And he took it down and wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid Him in a tomb cut into the rock, where no one had ever lain.
verses.Luke 23:54: It was the preparation day, and the Sabbath was about to begin.
verses.Luke 23:55: Now the women who had come with Him out of Galilee followed, and saw the tomb and how His body was laid.
verses.Luke 23:56: Then they returned and prepared spices and perfumes. And on the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.
verses.Luke 24:1: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they came to the tomb bringing the spices which they had prepared.
verses.Luke 24:2: And they found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
verses.Luke 24:3: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
verses.Luke 24:4: While they were perplexed about this, behold, two men suddenly stood near them in dazzling clothing;
verses.Luke 24:5: and as the women were terrified and bowed their faces to the ground, the men said to them, “Why do you seek the living One among the dead?
verses.Luke 24:6: He is not here, but He has risen. Remember how He spoke to you while He was still in Galilee,
verses.Luke 24:7: saying that the Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.”
verses.Luke 24:9: and returned from the tomb and reported all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.
verses.Luke 24:10: Now they were Mary Magdalene and Joanna and Mary the mother of James; also the other women with them were telling these things to the apostles.
verses.Luke 24:11: But these words appeared to them as nonsense, and they would not believe them.
verses.Luke 24:12: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb; stooping and looking in, he saw the linen wrappings only; and he went away to his home, marveling at what had happened.
verses.Luke 24:13: And behold, two of them were going that very day to a village named Emmaus, which was about seven miles from Jerusalem.
verses.Luke 24:14: And they were talking with each other about all these things which had taken place.
verses.Luke 24:15: While they were talking and discussing, Jesus Himself approached and began traveling with them.
verses.Luke 24:16: But their eyes were prevented from recognizing Him.
verses.Luke 24:18: One of them, named Cleopas, answered and said to Him, “Are You the only one visiting Jerusalem and unaware of the things which have happened here in these days?”
verses.Luke 24:20: and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered Him to the sentence of death, and crucified Him.
verses.Luke 24:21: But we were hoping that it was He who was going to redeem Israel. Indeed, besides all this, it is the third day since these things happened.
verses.Luke 24:22: But also some women among us amazed us. When they were at the tomb early in the morning,
verses.Luke 24:23: and did not find His body, they came, saying that they had also seen a vision of angels who said that He was alive.
verses.Luke 24:24: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just exactly as the women also had said; but Him they did not see.”
verses.Luke 24:27: Then beginning with Moses and with all the prophets, He explained to them the things concerning Himself in all the Scriptures.
verses.Luke 24:28: And they approached the village where they were going, and He acted as though He were going farther.
verses.Luke 24:29: But they urged Him, saying, “Stay with us, for it is getting toward evening, and the day is now nearly over.” So He went in to stay with them.
verses.Luke 24:30: When He had reclined at the table with them, He took the bread and blessed it, and breaking it, He began giving it to them.
verses.Luke 24:31: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized Him; and He vanished from their sight.
verses.Luke 24:32: They said to one another, “Were not our hearts burning within us while He was speaking to us on the road, while He was explaining the Scriptures to us?”
verses.Luke 24:33: And they got up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem, and found gathered together the eleven and those who were with them,
verses.Luke 24:34: saying, “The Lord has really risen and has appeared to Simon.”
verses.Luke 24:35: They began to relate their experiences on the road and how He was recognized by them in the breaking of the bread.
verses.Luke 24:37: But they were startled and frightened and thought that they were seeing a spirit.
verses.Luke 24:40: And when He had said this, He showed them His hands and His feet.
verses.Luke 24:42: They gave Him a piece of a broiled fish;
verses.Luke 24:43: and He took it and ate it before them.
verses.Luke 24:45: Then He opened their minds to understand the Scriptures,
verses.Luke 24:50: And He led them out as far as Bethany, and He lifted up His hands and blessed them.
verses.Luke 24:51: While He was blessing them, He parted from them and was carried up into heaven.
verses.Luke 24:52: And they, after worshiping Him, returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
verses.Luke 24:53: and were continually in the temple praising God.
verses.John 1:1: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
verses.John 1:2: He was in the beginning with God.
verses.John 1:3: All things came into being through Him, and apart from Him nothing came into being that has come into being.
verses.John 1:4: In Him was life, and the life was the Light of men.
verses.John 1:5: The Light shines in the darkness, and the darkness did not comprehend it.
verses.John 1:6: There came a man sent from God, whose name was John.
verses.John 1:7: He came as a witness, to testify about the Light, so that all might believe through him.
verses.John 1:8: He was not the Light, but he came to testify about the Light.
verses.John 1:9: There was the true Light which, coming into the world, enlightens every man.
verses.John 1:10: He was in the world, and the world was made through Him, and the world did not know Him.
verses.John 1:11: He came to His own, and those who were His own did not receive Him.
verses.John 1:12: But as many as received Him, to them He gave the right to become children of God, even to those who believe in His name,
verses.John 1:13: who were born, not of blood nor of the will of the flesh nor of the will of man, but of God.
verses.John 1:14: And the Word became flesh, and dwelt among us, and we saw His glory, glory as of the only begotten from the Father, full of grace and truth.
verses.John 1:15: John testified about Him and cried out, saying, “This was He of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has a higher rank than I, for He existed before me.’ ”
verses.John 1:16: For of His fullness we have all received, and grace upon grace.
verses.John 1:17: For the Law was given through Moses; grace and truth were realized through Jesus Christ.
verses.John 1:18: No one has seen God at any time; the only begotten God who is in the bosom of the Father, He has explained Him.
verses.John 1:19: This is the testimony of John, when the Jews sent to him priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
verses.John 1:20: And he confessed and did not deny, but confessed, “I am not the Christ.”
verses.John 1:21: They asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” And he said, “I am not.” “Are you the Prophet?” And he answered, “No.”
verses.John 1:22: Then they said to him, “Who are you, so that we may give an answer to those who sent us? What do you say about yourself?”
verses.John 1:23: He said, “I am a voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’ as Isaiah the prophet said.”
verses.John 1:24: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.
verses.John 1:25: They asked him, and said to him, “Why then are you baptizing, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?”
verses.John 1:26: John answered them saying, “I baptize in water, but among you stands One whom you do not know.
verses.John 1:27: It is He who comes after me, the thong of whose sandal I am not worthy to untie.”
verses.John 1:28: These things took place in Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
verses.John 1:29: The next day he saw Jesus coming to him and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
verses.John 1:30: This is He on behalf of whom I said, ‘After me comes a Man who has a higher rank than I, for He existed before me.’
verses.John 1:31: I did not recognize Him, but so that He might be manifested to Israel, I came baptizing in water.”
verses.John 1:32: John testified saying, “I have seen the Spirit descending as a dove out of heaven, and He remained upon Him.
verses.John 1:33: I did not recognize Him, but He who sent me to baptize in water said to me, ‘He upon whom you see the Spirit descending and remaining upon Him, this is the One who baptizes in the Holy Spirit.’
verses.John 1:34: I myself have seen, and have testified that this is the Son of God.”
verses.John 1:35: Again the next day John was standing with two of his disciples,
verses.John 1:36: and he looked at Jesus as He walked, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God!”
verses.John 1:37: The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
verses.John 1:40: One of the two who heard John speak and followed Him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
verses.John 1:41: He found first his own brother Simon and said to him, “We have found the Messiah” (which translated means Christ).
verses.John 1:44: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.
verses.John 1:45: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, “We have found Him of whom Moses in the Law and also the Prophets wrote—Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”
verses.John 1:46: Nathanael said to him, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
verses.John 1:49: Nathanael answered Him, “Rabbi, You are the Son of God; You are the King of Israel.”
verses.John 2:1: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there;
verses.John 2:2: and both Jesus and His disciples were invited to the wedding.
verses.John 2:3: When the wine ran out, the mother of Jesus said to Him, “They have no wine.”
verses.John 2:5: His mother said to the servants, “Whatever He says to you, do it.”
verses.John 2:6: Now there were six stone waterpots set there for the Jewish custom of purification, containing twenty or thirty gallons each.
verses.John 2:9: When the headwaiter tasted the water which had become wine, and did not know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the headwaiter called the bridegroom,
verses.John 2:10: and said to him, “Every man serves the good wine first, and when the people have drunk freely, then he serves the poorer wine; but you have kept the good wine until now.”
verses.John 2:11: This beginning of His signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and manifested His glory, and His disciples believed in Him.
verses.John 2:12: After this He went down to Capernaum, He and His mother and His brothers and His disciples; and they stayed there a few days.
verses.John 2:13: The Passover of the Jews was near, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
verses.John 2:14: And He found in the temple those who were selling oxen and sheep and doves, and the money changers seated at their tables.
verses.John 2:15: And He made a scourge of cords, and drove them all out of the temple, with the sheep and the oxen; and He poured out the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables;
verses.John 2:17: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for Your house will consume me.”
verses.John 2:18: The Jews then said to Him, “What sign do You show us as your authority for doing these things?”
verses.John 2:20: The Jews then said, “It took forty-six years to build this temple, and will You raise it up in three days?”
verses.John 2:21: But He was speaking of the temple of His body.
verses.John 2:22: So when He was raised from the dead, His disciples remembered that He said this; and they believed the Scripture and the word which Jesus had spoken.
verses.John 2:23: Now when He was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in His name, observing His signs which He was doing.
verses.John 2:24: But Jesus, on His part, was not entrusting Himself to them, for He knew all men,
verses.John 2:25: and because He did not need anyone to testify concerning man, for He Himself knew what was in man.
verses.John 3:1: Now there was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews;
verses.John 3:2: this man came to Jesus by night and said to Him, “Rabbi, we know that You have come from God as a teacher; for no one can do these signs that You do unless God is with him.”
verses.John 3:4: Nicodemus said to Him, “How can a man be born when he is old? He cannot enter a second time into his mother’s womb and be born, can he?”
verses.John 3:9: Nicodemus said to Him, “How can these things be?”
verses.John 3:22: After these things Jesus and His disciples came into the land of Judea, and there He was spending time with them and baptizing.
verses.John 3:23: John also was baptizing in Aenon near Salim, because there was much water there; and people were coming and were being baptized—
verses.John 3:24: for John had not yet been thrown into prison.
verses.John 3:25: Therefore there arose a discussion on the part of John’s disciples with a Jew about purification.
verses.John 3:26: And they came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, He who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, He is baptizing and all are coming to Him.”
verses.John 3:27: John answered and said, “A man can receive nothing unless it has been given him from heaven.
verses.John 3:28: You yourselves are my witnesses that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but, ‘I have been sent ahead of Him.’
verses.John 3:29: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice. So this joy of mine has been made full.
verses.John 3:30: He must increase, but I must decrease.
verses.John 3:31: “He who comes from above is above all, he who is of the earth is from the earth and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.
verses.John 3:32: What He has seen and heard, of that He testifies; and no one receives His testimony.
verses.John 3:33: He who has received His testimony has set his seal to this, that God is true.
verses.John 3:34: For He whom God has sent speaks the words of God; for He gives the Spirit without measure.
verses.John 3:35: The Father loves the Son and has given all things into His hand.
verses.John 3:36: He who believes in the Son has eternal life; but he who does not obey the Son will not see life, but the wrath of God abides on him.”
verses.John 4:1: Therefore when the Lord knew that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John
verses.John 4:2: (although Jesus Himself was not baptizing, but His disciples were),
verses.John 4:3: He left Judea and went away again into Galilee.
verses.John 4:4: And He had to pass through Samaria.
verses.John 4:5: So He came to a city of Samaria called Sychar, near the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son Joseph;
verses.John 4:6: and Jacob’s well was there. So Jesus, being wearied from His journey, was sitting thus by the well. It was about the sixth hour.
verses.John 4:8: For His disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.
verses.John 4:9: Therefore the Samaritan woman said to Him, “How is it that You, being a Jew, ask me for a drink since I am a Samaritan woman?” (For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.)
verses.John 4:11: She said to Him, “Sir, You have nothing to draw with and the well is deep; where then do You get that living water?
verses.John 4:12: You are not greater than our father Jacob, are You, who gave us the well, and drank of it himself and his sons and his cattle?”
verses.John 4:15: The woman said to Him, “Sir, give me this water, so I will not be thirsty nor come all the way here to draw.”
verses.John 4:19: The woman said to Him, “Sir, I perceive that You are a prophet.
verses.John 4:20: Our fathers worshiped in this mountain, and you people say that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.”
verses.John 4:25: The woman said to Him, “I know that Messiah is coming (He who is called Christ); when that One comes, He will declare all things to us.”
verses.John 4:27: At this point His disciples came, and they were amazed that He had been speaking with a woman, yet no one said, “What do You seek?” or, “Why do You speak with her?”
verses.John 4:28: So the woman left her waterpot, and went into the city and said to the men,
verses.John 4:29: “Come, see a man who told me all the things that I have done; this is not the Christ, is it?”
verses.John 4:30: They went out of the city, and were coming to Him.
verses.John 4:31: Meanwhile the disciples were urging Him, saying, “Rabbi, eat.”
verses.John 4:33: So the disciples were saying to one another, “No one brought Him anything to eat, did he?”
verses.John 4:39: From that city many of the Samaritans believed in Him because of the word of the woman who testified, “He told me all the things that I have done.”
verses.John 4:40: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they were asking Him to stay with them; and He stayed there two days.
verses.John 4:41: Many more believed because of His word;
verses.John 4:42: and they were saying to the woman, “It is no longer because of what you said that we believe, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this One is indeed the Savior of the world.”
verses.John 4:43: After the two days He went forth from there into Galilee.
verses.John 4:44: For Jesus Himself testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country.
verses.John 4:45: So when He came to Galilee, the Galileans received Him, having seen all the things that He did in Jerusalem at the feast; for they themselves also went to the feast.
verses.John 4:46: Therefore He came again to Cana of Galilee where He had made the water wine. And there was a royal official whose son was sick at Capernaum.
verses.John 4:47: When he heard that Jesus had come out of Judea into Galilee, he went to Him and was imploring Him to come down and heal his son; for he was at the point of death.
verses.John 4:49: The royal official said to Him, “Sir, come down before my child dies.”
verses.John 4:51: As he was now going down, his slaves met him, saying that his son was living.
verses.John 4:52: So he inquired of them the hour when he began to get better. Then they said to him, “Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.”
verses.John 4:54: This is again a second sign that Jesus performed when He had come out of Judea into Galilee.
verses.John 5:1: After these things there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
verses.John 5:2: Now there is in Jerusalem by the sheep gate a pool, which is called in Hebrew Bethesda, having five porticoes.
verses.John 5:3: In these lay a multitude of those who were sick, blind, lame, and withered, [waiting for the moving of the waters;
verses.John 5:4: for an angel of the Lord went down at certain seasons into the pool and stirred up the water; whoever then first, after the stirring up of the water, stepped in was made well from whatever disease with which he was afflicted.]
verses.John 5:5: A man was there who had been ill for thirty-eight years.
verses.John 5:7: The sick man answered Him, “Sir, I have no man to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, but while I am coming, another steps down before me.”
verses.John 5:9: Immediately the man became well, and picked up his pallet and began to walk. Now it was the Sabbath on that day.
verses.John 5:10: So the Jews were saying to the man who was cured, “It is the Sabbath, and it is not permissible for you to carry your pallet.”
verses.John 5:13: But the man who was healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had slipped away while there was a crowd in that place.
verses.John 5:15: The man went away, and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
verses.John 5:16: For this reason the Jews were persecuting Jesus, because He was doing these things on the Sabbath.
verses.John 5:18: For this reason therefore the Jews were seeking all the more to kill Him, because He not only was breaking the Sabbath, but also was calling God His own Father, making Himself equal with God.
verses.John 6:1: After these things Jesus went away to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (or Tiberias).
verses.John 6:2: A large crowd followed Him, because they saw the signs which He was performing on those who were sick.
verses.John 6:3: Then Jesus went up on the mountain, and there He sat down with His disciples.
verses.John 6:4: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was near.
verses.John 6:6: This He was saying to test him, for He Himself knew what He was intending to do.
verses.John 6:7: Philip answered Him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, for everyone to receive a little.”
verses.John 6:8: One of His disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to Him,
verses.John 6:9: “There is a lad here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these for so many people?”
verses.John 6:11: Jesus then took the loaves, and having given thanks, He distributed to those who were seated; likewise also of the fish as much as they wanted.
verses.John 6:13: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with fragments from the five barley loaves which were left over by those who had eaten.
verses.John 6:14: Therefore when the people saw the sign which He had performed, they said, “This is truly the Prophet who is to come into the world.”
verses.John 6:15: So Jesus, perceiving that they were intending to come and take Him by force to make Him king, withdrew again to the mountain by Himself alone.
verses.John 6:16: Now when evening came, His disciples went down to the sea,
verses.John 6:17: and after getting into a boat, they started to cross the sea to Capernaum. It had already become dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
verses.John 6:18: The sea began to be stirred up because a strong wind was blowing.
verses.John 6:19: Then, when they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and drawing near to the boat; and they were frightened.
verses.John 6:21: So they were willing to receive Him into the boat, and immediately the boat was at the land to which they were going.
verses.John 6:22: The next day the crowd that stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other small boat there, except one, and that Jesus had not entered with His disciples into the boat, but that His disciples had gone away alone.
verses.John 6:23: There came other small boats from Tiberias near to the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
verses.John 6:24: So when the crowd saw that Jesus was not there, nor His disciples, they themselves got into the small boats, and came to Capernaum seeking Jesus.
verses.John 6:25: When they found Him on the other side of the sea, they said to Him, “Rabbi, when did You get here?”
verses.John 6:28: Therefore they said to Him, “What shall we do, so that we may work the works of God?”
verses.John 6:30: So they said to Him, “What then do You do for a sign, so that we may see, and believe You? What work do You perform?
verses.John 6:31: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread out of heaven to eat.’ ”
verses.John 6:34: Then they said to Him, “Lord, always give us this bread.”
verses.John 6:52: Then the Jews began to argue with one another, saying, “How can this man give us His flesh to eat?”
verses.John 6:59: These things He said in the synagogue as He taught in Capernaum.
verses.John 6:60: Therefore many of His disciples, when they heard this said, “This is a difficult statement; who can listen to it?”
verses.John 6:66: As a result of this many of His disciples withdrew and were not walking with Him anymore.
verses.John 6:68: Simon Peter answered Him, “Lord, to whom shall we go? You have words of eternal life.
verses.John 6:69: We have believed and have come to know that You are the Holy One of God.”
verses.John 6:71: Now He meant Judas the son of Simon Iscariot, for he, one of the twelve, was going to betray Him.
verses.John 7:1: After these things Jesus was walking in Galilee, for He was unwilling to walk in Judea because the Jews were seeking to kill Him.
verses.John 7:2: Now the feast of the Jews, the Feast of Booths, was near.
verses.John 7:3: Therefore His brothers said to Him, “Leave here and go into Judea, so that Your disciples also may see Your works which You are doing.
verses.John 7:4: For no one does anything in secret when he himself seeks to be known publicly. If You do these things, show Yourself to the world.”
verses.John 7:5: For not even His brothers were believing in Him.
verses.John 7:9: Having said these things to them, He stayed in Galilee.
verses.John 7:10: But when His brothers had gone up to the feast, then He Himself also went up, not publicly, but as if, in secret.
verses.John 7:11: So the Jews were seeking Him at the feast and were saying, “Where is He?”
verses.John 7:12: There was much grumbling among the crowds concerning Him; some were saying, “He is a good man”; others were saying, “No, on the contrary, He leads the people astray.”
verses.John 7:13: Yet no one was speaking openly of Him for fear of the Jews.
verses.John 7:14: But when it was now the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple, and began to teach.
verses.John 7:15: The Jews then were astonished, saying, “How has this man become learned, having never been educated?”
verses.John 7:20: The crowd answered, “You have a demon! Who seeks to kill You?”
verses.John 7:25: So some of the people of Jerusalem were saying, “Is this not the man whom they are seeking to kill?
verses.John 7:26: Look, He is speaking publicly, and they are saying nothing to Him. The rulers do not really know that this is the Christ, do they?
verses.John 7:27: However, we know where this man is from; but whenever the Christ may come, no one knows where He is from.”
verses.John 7:30: So they were seeking to seize Him; and no man laid his hand on Him, because His hour had not yet come.
verses.John 7:31: But many of the crowd believed in Him; and they were saying, “When the Christ comes, He will not perform more signs than those which this man has, will He?”
verses.John 7:32: The Pharisees heard the crowd muttering these things about Him, and the chief priests and the Pharisees sent officers to seize Him.
verses.John 7:35: The Jews then said to one another, “Where does this man intend to go that we will not find Him? He is not intending to go to the Dispersion among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks, is He?
verses.John 7:39: But this He spoke of the Spirit, whom those who believed in Him were to receive; for the Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus was not yet glorified.
verses.John 7:40: Some of the people therefore, when they heard these words, were saying, “This certainly is the Prophet.”
verses.John 7:41: Others were saying, “This is the Christ.” Still others were saying, “Surely the Christ is not going to come from Galilee, is He?
verses.John 7:42: Has not the Scripture said that the Christ comes from the descendants of David, and from Bethlehem, the village where David was?”
verses.John 7:43: So a division occurred in the crowd because of Him.
verses.John 7:44: Some of them wanted to seize Him, but no one laid hands on Him.
verses.John 7:45: The officers then came to the chief priests and Pharisees, and they said to them, “Why did you not bring Him?”
verses.John 7:46: The officers answered, “Never has a man spoken the way this man speaks.”
verses.John 7:47: The Pharisees then answered them, “You have not also been led astray, have you?
verses.John 7:48: No one of the rulers or Pharisees has believed in Him, has he?
verses.John 7:49: But this crowd which does not know the Law is accursed.”
verses.John 7:50: Nicodemus (he who came to Him before, being one of them) said to them,
verses.John 7:51: “Our Law does not judge a man unless it first hears from him and knows what he is doing, does it?”
verses.John 7:52: They answered him, “You are not also from Galilee, are you? Search, and see that no prophet arises out of Galilee.”
verses.John 8:1: But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
verses.John 8:2: Early in the morning He came again into the temple, and all the people were coming to Him; and He sat down and began to teach them.
verses.John 8:3: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery, and having set her in the center of the court,
verses.John 8:4: they said to Him, “Teacher, this woman has been caught in adultery, in the very act.
verses.John 8:5: Now in the Law Moses commanded us to stone such women; what then do You say?”
verses.John 8:6: They were saying this, testing Him, so that they might have grounds for accusing Him. But Jesus stooped down and with His finger wrote on the ground.
verses.John 8:8: Again He stooped down and wrote on the ground.
verses.John 8:9: When they heard it, they began to go out one by one, beginning with the older ones, and He was left alone, and the woman, where she was, in the center of the court.
verses.John 8:13: So the Pharisees said to Him, “You are testifying about Yourself; Your testimony is not true.”
verses.John 8:20: These words He spoke in the treasury, as He taught in the temple; and no one seized Him, because His hour had not yet come.
verses.John 8:27: They did not realize that He had been speaking to them about the Father.
verses.John 8:30: As He spoke these things, many came to believe in Him.
verses.John 8:48: The Jews answered and said to Him, “Do we not say rightly that You are a Samaritan and have a demon?”
verses.John 8:53: Surely You are not greater than our father Abraham, who died? The prophets died too; whom do You make Yourself out to be?”
verses.John 8:57: So the Jews said to Him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have You seen Abraham?”
verses.John 8:59: Therefore they picked up stones to throw at Him, but Jesus hid Himself and went out of the temple.
verses.John 9:1: As He passed by, He saw a man blind from birth.
verses.John 9:2: And His disciples asked Him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he would be born blind?”
verses.John 9:6: When He had said this, He spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and applied the clay to his eyes,
verses.John 9:8: Therefore the neighbors, and those who previously saw him as a beggar, were saying, “Is not this the one who used to sit and beg?”
verses.John 9:9: Others were saying, “This is he,” still others were saying, “No, but he is like him.” He kept saying, “I am the one.”
verses.John 9:10: So they were saying to him, “How then were your eyes opened?”
verses.John 9:12: They said to him, “Where is He?” He said, “I do not know.”
verses.John 9:13: They brought to the Pharisees the man who was formerly blind.
verses.John 9:14: Now it was a Sabbath on the day when Jesus made the clay and opened his eyes.
verses.John 9:15: Then the Pharisees also were asking him again how he received his sight. And he said to them, “He applied clay to my eyes, and I washed, and I see.”
verses.John 9:16: Therefore some of the Pharisees were saying, “This man is not from God, because He does not keep the Sabbath.” But others were saying, “How can a man who is a sinner perform such signs?” And there was a division among them.
verses.John 9:17: So they said to the blind man again, “What do you say about Him, since He opened your eyes?” And he said, “He is a prophet.”
verses.John 9:18: The Jews then did not believe it of him, that he had been blind and had received sight, until they called the parents of the very one who had received his sight,
verses.John 9:19: and questioned them, saying, “Is this your son, who you say was born blind? Then how does he now see?”
verses.John 9:20: His parents answered them and said, “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
verses.John 9:21: but how he now sees, we do not know; or who opened his eyes, we do not know. Ask him; he is of age, he will speak for himself.”
verses.John 9:22: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that if anyone confessed Him to be Christ, he was to be put out of the synagogue.
verses.John 9:23: For this reason his parents said, “He is of age; ask him.”
verses.John 9:24: So a second time they called the man who had been blind, and said to him, “Give glory to God; we know that this man is a sinner.”
verses.John 9:25: He then answered, “Whether He is a sinner, I do not know; one thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see.”
verses.John 9:26: So they said to him, “What did He do to you? How did He open your eyes?”
verses.John 9:27: He answered them, “I told you already and you did not listen; why do you want to hear it again? You do not want to become His disciples too, do you?”
verses.John 9:28: They reviled him and said, “You are His disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
verses.John 9:29: We know that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this man, we do not know where He is from.”
verses.John 9:30: The man answered and said to them, “Well, here is an amazing thing, that you do not know where He is from, and yet He opened my eyes.
verses.John 9:31: We know that God does not hear sinners; but if anyone is God-fearing and does His will, He hears him.
verses.John 9:32: Since the beginning of time it has never been heard that anyone opened the eyes of a person born blind.
verses.John 9:33: If this man were not from God, He could do nothing.”
verses.John 9:34: They answered him, “You were born entirely in sins, and are you teaching us?” So they put him out.
verses.John 9:36: He answered, “Who is He, Lord, that I may believe in Him?”
verses.John 9:38: And he said, “Lord, I believe.” And he worshiped Him.
verses.John 9:40: Those of the Pharisees who were with Him heard these things and said to Him, “We are not blind too, are we?”
verses.John 10:6: This figure of speech Jesus spoke to them, but they did not understand what those things were which He had been saying to them.
verses.John 10:19: A division occurred again among the Jews because of these words.
verses.John 10:20: Many of them were saying, “He has a demon and is insane. Why do you listen to Him?”
verses.John 10:21: Others were saying, “These are not the sayings of one demon-possessed. A demon cannot open the eyes of the blind, can he?”
verses.John 10:22: At that time the Feast of the Dedication took place at Jerusalem;
verses.John 10:23: it was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple in the portico of Solomon.
verses.John 10:24: The Jews then gathered around Him, and were saying to Him, “How long will You keep us in suspense? If You are the Christ, tell us plainly.”
verses.John 10:31: The Jews picked up stones again to stone Him.
verses.John 10:33: The Jews answered Him, “For a good work we do not stone You, but for blasphemy; and because You, being a man, make Yourself out to be God.”
verses.John 10:39: Therefore they were seeking again to seize Him, and He eluded their grasp.
verses.John 10:40: And He went away again beyond the Jordan to the place where John was first baptizing, and He was staying there.
verses.John 10:41: Many came to Him and were saying, “While John performed no sign, yet everything John said about this man was true.”
verses.John 11:1: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
verses.John 11:2: It was the Mary who anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped His feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.
verses.John 11:3: So the sisters sent word to Him, saying, “Lord, behold, he whom You love is sick.”
verses.John 11:5: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
verses.John 11:6: So when He heard that he was sick, He then stayed two days longer in the place where He was.
verses.John 11:8: The disciples said to Him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just now seeking to stone You, and are You going there again?”
verses.John 11:12: The disciples then said to Him, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.”
verses.John 11:13: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that He was speaking of literal sleep.
verses.John 11:16: Therefore Thomas, who is called Didymus, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, so that we may die with Him.”
verses.John 11:17: So when Jesus came, He found that he had already been in the tomb four days.
verses.John 11:18: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about two miles off;
verses.John 11:19: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary, to console them concerning their brother.
verses.John 11:20: Martha therefore, when she heard that Jesus was coming, went to meet Him, but Mary stayed at the house.
verses.John 11:21: Martha then said to Jesus, “Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.
verses.John 11:22: Even now I know that whatever You ask of God, God will give You.”
verses.John 11:24: Martha said to Him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection on the last day.”
verses.John 11:27: She said to Him, “Yes, Lord; I have believed that You are the Christ, the Son of God, even He who comes into the world.”
verses.John 11:28: When she had said this, she went away and called Mary her sister, saying secretly, “The Teacher is here and is calling for you.”
verses.John 11:29: And when she heard it, she got up quickly and was coming to Him.
verses.John 11:30: Now Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was still in the place where Martha met Him.
verses.John 11:31: Then the Jews who were with her in the house, and consoling her, when they saw that Mary got up quickly and went out, they followed her, supposing that she was going to the tomb to weep there.
verses.John 11:32: Therefore, when Mary came where Jesus was, she saw Him, and fell at His feet, saying to Him, “Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.”
verses.John 11:33: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her also weeping, He was deeply moved in spirit and was troubled,
verses.John 11:36: So the Jews were saying, “See how He loved him!”
verses.John 11:37: But some of them said, “Could not this man, who opened the eyes of the blind man, have kept this man also from dying?”
verses.John 11:38: So Jesus, again being deeply moved within, came to the tomb. Now it was a cave, and a stone was lying against it.
verses.John 11:45: Therefore many of the Jews who came to Mary, and saw what He had done, believed in Him.
verses.John 11:46: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them the things which Jesus had done.
verses.John 11:47: Therefore the chief priests and the Pharisees convened a council, and were saying, “What are we doing? For this man is performing many signs.
verses.John 11:48: If we let Him go on like this, all men will believe in Him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.”
verses.John 11:49: But one of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all,
verses.John 11:50: nor do you take into account that it is expedient for you that one man die for the people, and that the whole nation not perish.”
verses.John 11:51: Now he did not say this on his own initiative, but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus was going to die for the nation,
verses.John 11:52: and not for the nation only, but in order that He might also gather together into one the children of God who are scattered abroad.
verses.John 11:53: So from that day on they planned together to kill Him.
verses.John 11:54: Therefore Jesus no longer continued to walk publicly among the Jews, but went away from there to the country near the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim; and there He stayed with the disciples.
verses.John 11:55: Now the Passover of the Jews was near, and many went up to Jerusalem out of the country before the Passover to purify themselves.
verses.John 11:56: So they were seeking for Jesus, and were saying to one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think; that He will not come to the feast at all?”
verses.John 11:57: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where He was, he was to report it, so that they might seize Him.
verses.John 12:1: Jesus, therefore, six days before the Passover, came to Bethany where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
verses.John 12:2: So they made Him a supper there, and Martha was serving; but Lazarus was one of those reclining at the table with Him.
verses.John 12:3: Mary then took a pound of very costly perfume of pure nard, and anointed the feet of Jesus and wiped His feet with her hair; and the house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
verses.John 12:4: But Judas Iscariot, one of His disciples, who was intending to betray Him, said,
verses.John 12:5: “Why was this perfume not sold for three hundred denarii and given to poor people?”
verses.John 12:6: Now he said this, not because he was concerned about the poor, but because he was a thief, and as he had the money box, he used to pilfer what was put into it.
verses.John 12:9: The large crowd of the Jews then learned that He was there; and they came, not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might also see Lazarus, whom He raised from the dead.
verses.John 12:10: But the chief priests planned to put Lazarus to death also;
verses.John 12:11: because on account of him many of the Jews were going away and were believing in Jesus.
verses.John 12:12: On the next day the large crowd who had come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
verses.John 12:13: took the branches of the palm trees and went out to meet Him, and began to shout, “Hosanna! Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord, even the King of Israel.”
verses.John 12:14: Jesus, finding a young donkey, sat on it; as it is written,
verses.John 12:15: “Fear not, daughter of Zion;behold, your King is coming, seated on a donkey’s colt.”
verses.John 12:16: These things His disciples did not understand at the first; but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things were written of Him, and that they had done these things to Him.
verses.John 12:17: So the people, who were with Him when He called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead, continued to testify about Him.
verses.John 12:18: For this reason also the people went and met Him, because they heard that He had performed this sign.
verses.John 12:19: So the Pharisees said to one another, “You see that you are not doing any good; look, the world has gone after Him.”
verses.John 12:20: Now there were some Greeks among those who were going up to worship at the feast;
verses.John 12:21: these then came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida of Galilee, and began to ask him, saying, “Sir, we wish to see Jesus.”
verses.John 12:22: Philip came and told Andrew; Andrew and Philip came and told Jesus.
verses.John 12:29: So the crowd of people who stood by and heard it were saying that it had thundered; others were saying, “An angel has spoken to Him.”
verses.John 12:33: But He was saying this to indicate the kind of death by which He was to die.
verses.John 12:37: But though He had performed so many signs before them, yet they were not believing in Him.
verses.John 12:38: This was to fulfill the word of Isaiah the prophet which he spoke: “Lord, who has believed our report? And to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”
verses.John 12:39: For this reason they could not believe, for Isaiah said again,
verses.John 12:40: “He has blinded their eyes and He hardened their heart, so that they would not see with their eyes and perceive with their heart, and be converted and I heal them.”
verses.John 12:41: These things Isaiah said because he saw His glory, and he spoke of Him.
verses.John 12:42: Nevertheless many even of the rulers believed in Him, but because of the Pharisees they were not confessing Him, for fear that they would be put out of the synagogue;
verses.John 12:43: for they loved the approval of men rather than the approval of God.
verses.John 13:1: Now before the Feast of the Passover, Jesus knowing that His hour had come that He would depart out of this world to the Father, having loved His own who were in the world, He loved them to the end.
verses.John 13:2: During supper, the devil having already put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, the son of Simon, to betray Him,
verses.John 13:3: Jesus, knowing that the Father had given all things into His hands, and that He had come forth from God and was going back to God,
verses.John 13:4: got up from supper, and laid aside His garments; and taking a towel, He girded Himself.
verses.John 13:5: Then He poured water into the basin, and began to wash the disciples’ feet and to wipe them with the towel with which He was girded.
verses.John 13:6: So He came to Simon Peter. He said to Him, “Lord, do You wash my feet?”
verses.John 13:9: Simon Peter said to Him, “Lord, then wash not only my feet, but also my hands and my head.”
verses.John 13:22: The disciples began looking at one another, at a loss to know of which one He was speaking.
verses.John 13:23: There was reclining on Jesus’ bosom one of His disciples, whom Jesus loved.
verses.John 13:24: So Simon Peter gestured to him, and said to him, “Tell us who it is of whom He is speaking.”
verses.John 13:25: He, leaning back thus on Jesus’ bosom, said to Him, “Lord, who is it?”
verses.John 13:28: Now no one of those reclining at the table knew for what purpose He had said this to him.
verses.John 13:29: For some were supposing, because Judas had the money box, that Jesus was saying to him, “Buy the things we have need of for the feast”; or else, that he should give something to the poor.
verses.John 13:30: So after receiving the morsel he went out immediately; and it was night.
verses.John 13:37: Peter said to Him, “Lord, why can I not follow You right now? I will lay down my life for You.”
verses.John 14:5: Thomas said to Him, “Lord, we do not know where You are going, how do we know the way?”
verses.John 14:8: Philip said to Him, “Lord, show us the Father, and it is enough for us.”
verses.John 14:22: Judas (not Iscariot) said to Him, “Lord, what then has happened that You are going to disclose Yourself to us and not to the world?”
verses.John 16:29: His disciples said, “Lo, now You are speaking plainly and are not using a figure of speech.
verses.John 16:30: Now we know that You know all things, and have no need for anyone to question You; by this we believe that You came from God.”
verses.John 18:1: When Jesus had spoken these words, He went forth with His disciples over the ravine of the Kidron, where there was a garden, in which He entered with His disciples.
verses.John 18:2: Now Judas also, who was betraying Him, knew the place, for Jesus had often met there with His disciples.
verses.John 18:3: Judas then, having received the Roman cohort and officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees, came there with lanterns and torches and weapons.
verses.John 18:10: Simon Peter then, having a sword, drew it and struck the high priest’s slave, and cut off his right ear; and the slave’s name was Malchus.
verses.John 18:12: So the Roman cohort and the commander and the officers of the Jews, arrested Jesus and bound Him,
verses.John 18:13: and led Him to Annas first; for he was father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.
verses.John 18:14: Now Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jews that it was expedient for one man to die on behalf of the people.
verses.John 18:15: Simon Peter was following Jesus, and so was another disciple. Now that disciple was known to the high priest, and entered with Jesus into the court of the high priest,
verses.John 18:16: but Peter was standing at the door outside. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to the doorkeeper, and brought Peter in.
verses.John 18:17: Then the slave-girl who kept the door said to Peter, “You are not also one of this man’s disciples, are you?” He said, “I am not.”
verses.John 18:18: Now the slaves and the officers were standing there, having made a charcoal fire, for it was cold and they were warming themselves; and Peter was also with them, standing and warming himself.
verses.John 18:19: The high priest then questioned Jesus about His disciples, and about His teaching.
verses.John 18:22: When He had said this, one of the officers standing nearby struck Jesus, saying, “Is that the way You answer the high priest?”
verses.John 18:24: So Annas sent Him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.
verses.John 18:25: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. So they said to him, “You are not also one of His disciples, are you?” He denied it, and said, “I am not.”
verses.John 18:26: One of the slaves of the high priest, being a relative of the one whose ear Peter cut off, said, “Did I not see you in the garden with Him?”
verses.John 18:27: Peter then denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
verses.John 18:28: Then they led Jesus from Caiaphas into the Praetorium, and it was early; and they themselves did not enter into the Praetorium so that they would not be defiled, but might eat the Passover.
verses.John 18:29: Therefore Pilate went out to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this Man?”
verses.John 18:30: They answered and said to him, “If this Man were not an evildoer, we would not have delivered Him to you.”
verses.John 18:31: So Pilate said to them, “Take Him yourselves, and judge Him according to your law.” The Jews said to him, “We are not permitted to put anyone to death,”
verses.John 18:32: to fulfill the word of Jesus which He spoke, signifying by what kind of death He was about to die.
verses.John 18:33: Therefore Pilate entered again into the Praetorium, and summoned Jesus and said to Him, “Are You the King of the Jews?”
verses.John 18:35: Pilate answered, “I am not a Jew, am I? Your own nation and the chief priests delivered You to me; what have You done?”
verses.John 18:38: Pilate said to Him, “What is truth?” And when he had said this, he went out again to the Jews and said to them, “I find no guilt in Him.
verses.John 18:39: But you have a custom that I release someone for you at the Passover; do you wish then that I release for you the King of the Jews?”
verses.John 18:40: So they cried out again, saying, “Not this Man, but Barabbas.” Now Barabbas was a robber.
verses.John 19:1: Pilate then took Jesus and scourged Him.
verses.John 19:2: And the soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on His head, and put a purple robe on Him;
verses.John 19:3: and they began to come up to Him and say, “Hail, King of the Jews!” and to give Him slaps in the face.
verses.John 19:4: Pilate came out again and said to them, “Behold, I am bringing Him out to you so that you may know that I find no guilt in Him.”
verses.John 19:5: Jesus then came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said to them, “Behold, the Man!”
verses.John 19:6: So when the chief priests and the officers saw Him, they cried out saying, “Crucify, crucify!” Pilate said to them, “Take Him yourselves and crucify Him, for I find no guilt in Him.”
verses.John 19:7: The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and by that law He ought to die because He made Himself out to be the Son of God.”
verses.John 19:8: Therefore when Pilate heard this statement, he was even more afraid;
verses.John 19:9: and he entered into the Praetorium again and said to Jesus, “Where are You from?” But Jesus gave him no answer.
verses.John 19:10: So Pilate said to Him, “You do not speak to me? Do You not know that I have authority to release You, and I have authority to crucify You?”
verses.John 19:12: As a result of this Pilate made efforts to release Him, but the Jews cried out saying, “If you release this Man, you are no friend of Caesar; everyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes Caesar.”
verses.John 19:13: Therefore when Pilate heard these words, he brought Jesus out, and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called The Pavement, but in Hebrew, Gabbatha.
verses.John 19:14: Now it was the day of preparation for the Passover; it was about the sixth hour. And he said to the Jews, “Behold, your King!”
verses.John 19:15: So they cried out, “Away with Him, away with Him, crucify Him!” Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your King?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar.”
verses.John 19:16: So he then handed Him over to them to be crucified.
verses.John 19:17: They took Jesus, therefore, and He went out, bearing His own cross, to the place called the Place of a Skull, which is called in Hebrew, Golgotha.
verses.John 19:18: There they crucified Him, and with Him two other men, one on either side, and Jesus in between.
verses.John 19:19: Pilate also wrote an inscription and put it on the cross. It was written, “JESUS THE NAZARENE, THE KING OF THE JEWS.”
verses.John 19:20: Therefore many of the Jews read this inscription, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city; and it was written in Hebrew, Latin and in Greek.
verses.John 19:21: So the chief priests of the Jews were saying to Pilate, “Do not write, ‘The King of the Jews’; but that He said, ‘I am King of the Jews.’ ”
verses.John 19:22: Pilate answered, “What I have written I have written.”
verses.John 19:23: Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took His outer garments and made four parts, a part to every soldier and also the tunic; now the tunic was seamless, woven in one piece.
verses.John 19:24: So they said to one another, “Let us not tear it, but cast lots for it, to decide whose it shall be”; this was to fulfill the Scripture: “They divided My outer garments among them, and for My clothing they cast lots.”
verses.John 19:25: Therefore the soldiers did these things. But standing by the cross of Jesus were His mother, and His mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.
verses.John 19:29: A jar full of sour wine was standing there; so they put a sponge full of the sour wine upon a branch of hyssop and brought it up to His mouth.
verses.John 19:31: Then the Jews, because it was the day of preparation, so that the bodies would not remain on the cross on the Sabbath (for that Sabbath was a high day), asked Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away.
verses.John 19:32: So the soldiers came, and broke the legs of the first man and of the other who was crucified with Him;
verses.John 19:33: but coming to Jesus, when they saw that He was already dead, they did not break His legs.
verses.John 19:34: But one of the soldiers pierced His side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
verses.John 19:35: And he who has seen has testified, and his testimony is true; and he knows that he is telling the truth, so that you also may believe.
verses.John 19:36: For these things came to pass to fulfill the Scripture, “Not a bone of Him shall be broken.”
verses.John 19:37: And again another Scripture says, “They shall look on Him whom they pierced.”
verses.John 19:38: After these things Joseph of Arimathea, being a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one for fear of the Jews, asked Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus; and Pilate granted permission. So he came and took away His body.
verses.John 19:39: Nicodemus, who had first come to Him by night, also came, bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred pounds weight.
verses.John 19:40: So they took the body of Jesus and bound it in linen wrappings with the spices, as is the burial custom of the Jews.
verses.John 19:41: Now in the place where He was crucified there was a garden, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had yet been laid.
verses.John 19:42: Therefore because of the Jewish day of preparation, since the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus there.
verses.John 20:1: Now on the first day of the week Mary Magdalene came early to the tomb, while it was still dark, and saw the stone already taken away from the tomb.
verses.John 20:2: So she ran and came to Simon Peter and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid Him.”
verses.John 20:3: So Peter and the other disciple went forth, and they were going to the tomb.
verses.John 20:4: The two were running together; and the other disciple ran ahead faster than Peter and came to the tomb first;
verses.John 20:5: and stooping and looking in, he saw the linen wrappings lying there; but he did not go in.
verses.John 20:6: And so Simon Peter also came, following him, and entered the tomb; and he saw the linen wrappings lying there,
verses.John 20:7: and the face-cloth which had been on His head, not lying with the linen wrappings, but rolled up in a place by itself.
verses.John 20:8: So the other disciple who had first come to the tomb then also entered, and he saw and believed.
verses.John 20:9: For as yet they did not understand the Scripture, that He must rise again from the dead.
verses.John 20:10: So the disciples went away again to their own homes.
verses.John 20:11: But Mary was standing outside the tomb weeping; and so, as she wept, she stooped and looked into the tomb;
verses.John 20:12: and she saw two angels in white sitting, one at the head and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had been lying.
verses.John 20:13: And they said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “Because they have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have laid Him.”
verses.John 20:14: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, and did not know that it was Jesus.
verses.John 20:18: Mary Magdalene came, announcing to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord,” and that He had said these things to her.
verses.John 20:20: And when He had said this, He showed them both His hands and His side. The disciples then rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
verses.John 20:24: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.
verses.John 20:25: So the other disciples were saying to him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he said to them, “Unless I see in His hands the imprint of the nails, and put my finger into the place of the nails, and put my hand into His side, I will not believe.”
verses.John 20:28: Thomas answered and said to Him, “My Lord and my God!”
verses.John 20:30: Therefore many other signs Jesus also performed in the presence of the disciples, which are not written in this book;
verses.John 20:31: but these have been written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; and that believing you may have life in His name.
verses.John 21:1: After these things Jesus manifested Himself again to the disciples at the Sea of Tiberias, and He manifested Himself in this way.
verses.John 21:2: Simon Peter, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two others of His disciples were together.
verses.John 21:3: Simon Peter said to them, “I am going fishing.” They said to him, “We will also come with you.” They went out and got into the boat; and that night they caught nothing.
verses.John 21:4: But when the day was now breaking, Jesus stood on the beach; yet the disciples did not know that it was Jesus.
verses.John 21:7: Therefore that disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It is the Lord.” So when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he put his outer garment on (for he was stripped for work), and threw himself into the sea.
verses.John 21:8: But the other disciples came in the little boat, for they were not far from the land, but about one hundred yards away, dragging the net full of fish.
verses.John 21:9: So when they got out on the land, they saw a charcoal fire already laid and fish placed on it, and bread.
verses.John 21:11: Simon Peter went up and drew the net to land, full of large fish, a hundred and fifty-three; and although there were so many, the net was not torn.
verses.John 21:13: Jesus came and took the bread and gave it to them, and the fish likewise.
verses.John 21:14: This is now the third time that Jesus was manifested to the disciples, after He was raised from the dead.
verses.John 21:20: Peter, turning around, saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them; the one who also had leaned back on His bosom at the supper and said, “Lord, who is the one who betrays You?”
verses.John 21:21: So Peter seeing him said to Jesus, “Lord, and what about this man?”
verses.John 21:24: This is the disciple who is testifying to these things and wrote these things, and we know that his testimony is true.
verses.John 21:25: And there are also many other things which Jesus did, which if they were written in detail, I suppose that even the world itself would not contain the books that would be written.
